title: Degree Programmes offered at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The University of Vienna is one of the most prestiguous and largest universities in Europe and offers a wide range of degree programmes. From education, social sciences, philosophy and religion, history, society, politics and arts over culture, media, mathematics, informatics and technology, natural and life sciences, psychology, health and sports tp languages, economic sciences and law.

This diversity gives students the opportunity to choose an individualised way through their studies that best suits their interests and abilities.

Recommendations for your choice of degree

[### Bachelor's/Diploma Programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)

[### Master's Programmes](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/)

[### Doctoral/PhD Programmes](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/)

[### Teacher Education Programme](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

[### Extension Curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/)

[### Postgraduate Programmes](https://postgraduatecenter.univie.ac.at/en/)

[### Student Mobility - Exchange & Mobility Programmes](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)

[### University Sport Institute](https://www.usi.at/en/)

[### Language Centre](https://sprachenzentrum.univie.ac.at/en/)

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

title: Looking for a degree programme?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Choice of degree - School meets Uni
Get to know the University of Vienna!
Get to know the University of Vienna!
text:
You are interested in the University of Vienna and are still looking for the right degree programme? The following pages help you to get to know the large variety of offered degree programmes and to make the choice easier for you.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

# Looking for a degree programme?

[### Offered degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/)

- [From A like Advanced Theological Studies to Z like Zoology -  all degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna](/en/degree-programmes/)

[## Studying - Yes or No?

The University of Vienna organises study fairs dierctly at the university and is represented at external career fairs. You want to get to know the university and talk to representatives personally?

[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Why study at the University of Vienna?
heading_paths: We offer the widest range of degree programmes
text:
With about 90,000 students, 178 degree programmes as well as more than 60 university continuing education and training programmes, the University of Vienna is the largest and most diverse educational institution in Austria.

including non-Austrian students from 140 different nations that account for a third of the student body

[Facts & Folders](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns#c3230)

including more than 110 master's programmes

[Degree programmes A-Z](/en/degree-programmes/#c165230)

the oldest university in the German-speaking world and one of the oldest universities in Europe

[History of the University of Vienna](https://geschichte.univie.ac.at/)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

From A as in African Studies to Z as in Zoology: Our students can select from the largest range of degree programmes in Austria. The University of Vienna offers 178 degree programmes, including more than 110 master´s programmes.

- [Choice of degree](/en/degree-programmes/choice-of-degree/)
- [Degree programmes A-Z](/en/degree-programmes/#c165230)
- [Doctoral/PhD Programmes](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/)

title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Why study at the University of Vienna?
heading_paths: Our research and teaching are inextricably intertwined
text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [The University of Vienna´s strategic priorities](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/research)

title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Why study at the University of Vienna?
heading_path: We are right at the heart of the city of Vienna
heading_paths: We are right at the heart of the city of Vienna
text:
With its more than 70 locations, the University of Vienna shapes Vienna's cityscape. For many years Austria's capital has been the world's most liveable city and has been ranked among the most popular student cities in the world.

- [All locations of the University of Vienna](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/locations-maps/university-of-vienna-locations/)
- [Student Space: room for students](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/)
- [Blog of the University of Vienna](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Why study at the University of Vienna?
text: Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. Many University Departments are located here like the Department of Cultural and Social Anthropology or of Philosophy. © University of Vienna / Peter Wienerroither Another location of the University of Vienna is the Campus. Here you will find, for example, the Department of Art History or of Romance Studies. With its many green courtyards, the Campus offers space for rest and relaxation in the middle of the city.

title: Choosing the right programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
[Austrian National Union of Students Counselling for Secondary-School Graduates](/en/degree-programmes/choosing-the-right-programme/psychologische-studierendenberatung/#c755413)
Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme
text:
© Universität Wien/ Christoph Wannerer

A lot of questions arise for pupils after their final school exams:

- How would I like to continue my education?
- Do I want to study, and if so, what?
- Would I prefer studying at a university or a university of applied sciences?
- How do I choose the right degree programme?
- Which decisionmaking aids do exist?

Here you can find our advice on how to choose the right degree programme!

[### University in a nutshell](/en/degree-programmes/choosing-the-right-programme/university-in-a-nutshell/)

[### Career orientation & educational choice](/en/degree-programmes/choosing-the-right-programme/checking-my-interest/)

[### Who can help me with ...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

© Universität Wien/Barbara Mair

The Psychological Counseling Centers (Psychologische Studienberatung) are service facilities where psychologists offer free and confidential help.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

The Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH) provides comprehensive information for secondary-school graduates on the choice of their degree programme.

(c) Universität Wien/Barbara Mair

The Schulpsychologische Bildungsberatung (School Psychological Educational Counselling) offers advice regarding the question "What shall I do after graduation from school?".

(c) Universität Wien/Barbara Mair

On this website of BMBWF you can discover all study opportunities offered in Austria.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

The programme 18plus offers extensive aid and support to secondary school graduates and provides them with material for their choice of degree programme and career.

© ÖH/ Studieren Probieren

breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Counselling facilities related to the choice of a degree programme

Facilities with a focus on general career choice counselling
Facilities with a focus on general career choice counselling
Facilities with a focus on general career choice counselling
Facilities with a focus on general career choice counselling
text:
Studienplattform (Study Platform) is an important tool for your independent research and offers up-to-date information and useful links to all fields of degree Programmes in Austria.

On this website, which is provided by OeAD (Östereichischer Austauschdienst), you can find a lot of valuable information about studying in Austria.

Watch the film ["Study in Austria"](https://youtu.be/IcBJh8AyHC8)  to find out why Austria is the ideal place to study.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

BerufsInfoZentrum (BIZ - Job Information Centre) is your first contact point for questions on professions, trainings and job opportunities.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

"Get your own idea of your profession": With this slogan the Berufsinformationszentrum der Wiener Wirtschaft (BiWi - Vocational Information Centre of the Vienna Economic Chamber) provides orientation for your career and educational choice.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

Bildungsberatung in Wien (Educational Counselling in Vienna) offers free and neutral educational and career counselling to Viennese residents.

[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

title: Career orientation and educational choice
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: When choosing your career it is crucial to find the best educational training for yourself. This is a gradual process. Quite often it does not make sense to think of a concrete profession, but rather to consider what you are good at and what you are suited for. The following questions can provide orientation for this choice: - What do I enjoy? - What am I escpecially good at? - What is easy for me? Once you have answered these questions in detail and have found out which activities you are particularly interested in, you can research on professions and training possibilities in a next step. During your research, you can have a close look at the degree programmes that will guide you to your professional goal. Are there school subjects you are particularly interested in? Do you have a concrete idea of your future career? Do you know a person who you believe has found the job of his/her dreams? All these questions help you to narrow down your list of possible degree programmes. In case you have a concrete idea of your career, find out which degree programme makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least

title: Career orientation and educational choice
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least three years. Bear in mind that you will deal with topics of this degree programme for the next 3 to 5 years . - Your curriculum (or study plan) is a helpful tool for choosing your degree programme. Each degree programme has its own curriculum which provides you with information on the programme structure, contents and the course of study. On the details page of each degree programme you can find a link to the curriculum. - Get information about possible deadlines, requirements or entrance exam procedures in time. For example, if you would like to study Psychology or Journalism and Communication Studies, you have to participate in an entrance exam procedure. Also, different admission deadlines apply. If you would like to study Sport Science, physical fitness is very important: There is a supplementary examination which you have to complete before you start with your degree programme. Some degree programmes require (previous) knowledge in Latin or Greek. © Universität Wien/Martin Sowa

title: Career orientation and educational choice
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: BerufsInfoZentren of Public Employment Service Austria
text:
Last but not least ... a range of information and counselling facilities can help you with your decision.

- [Counselling facilities](/en/degree-programmes/choosing-the-right-programme/#c298422)
- [Online-Self-Assessments](#c274285)

What does it mean to be a student at the University of Vienna? Start your virtual journey through a Bachelor programme at the University of Vienna!

[Click here for uni:check](https://unicheck.univie.ac.at/en/)

The University of Vienna provides prospective students with various Online Self-Assessments which you can complete on a voluntary basis. These study-related self-assessments can offer you an insight into the degree programme of your choice and also help you get an overview of your own abilities. After having completed an Online Self-Assessment, you receive individual feedback as well as specific advice.

[Online-Self-Assessments](https://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (available in German)

The BerufsInfoZentren (BIZ) of Public Employment Service Austria (AMS) help with choosing a career and course of study.

- [BIZ-Locations](https://www.ams.at/arbeitsuchende/aus-und-weiterbildung/berufsinformationen/biz---berufsinfozentren#wien) (available only in German language)

The Chamber of Labour (Arbeiterkammer - AK) career interest test enables you to find out more about your personal interests and preferences. You receive an individual interest profile and specific suggestions for your career choice.

- [www.berufsinteressentest.at](http://www.berufsinteressentest.at/) (available only in German)

© AK OÖ

title: Psychological Counseling Centers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: **WHO ARE WE?** The **Psychological Counseling Centers** (Psychologische Studienberatung) are service factilities of BMBWF at which psychologists offer free psychological and psychotherapeutical help. **WHO CAN CONTACT US?** - Students of universities and universities of applied sciences - Prospective students with questions about the organisation of studies and the choice of degree programme **WHICH TOPICS DO WE DEAL WITH?** You may contact us if - you are feeling insecure which degree programme to choose, - you are having orientational and adjustment problems at the start of your studies, - you are having difficulties when changing, discontinuing or completing your degree programme, - you are in a critical situation related to your studies, - you want to improve your working or learning behaviour, - you are having difficulties concentrating, motivating yourself, or when taking exams, - you are feeling affected by personal problems in your studies or your everyday routine of studies, - you want to improve your communicative or social competences. **DIAGNOSTIC ASSISTANCE IN YOUR PERSONAL CHOICE OF DEGREE PROGRAMME** With the help of individually composed preformance tests and personality questionnaires it is possible to evaluate interests, motives, skills and ressources more closely for the right choice of degree programme. In a preliminary meeting the questions are clearified and in an individual consultation the results will be descussed extensively. **FIVE STEPS TO THE RIGHT DEGREE PROGRAMME** Visit our [online course for

title: University in a nutshell
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
On this site prospective and first-year students get a first insight into the University of Vienna and into student life.

A university degree mainly differs from school and other vocational trainings in two aspects:

1. I am myself responsible for my study progress. This means, for example, that I have to organise my time and schedule my semester on my own.
2. A university degree does not prepare me for a specific profession, as for example an apprenticeship does. It is a basic academic training in the selected field. I get to know scientific theories and working methods which may open up several career opportunities. I gain professional practice and experience for my later profession while working besides studying, or during an internship.

Our videos in the category "Studying made easier" explain vividly important terms from student life at the University of Vienna.

In the "Uni Stories" students talk about their everyday life at university. The stories contain different topics, such as housing, budget, finances, stays abroad, barrier-free studying or the reasons that were decisive for the students choice of a particular study program.

- [University Know-How](#c274210)
- [Uni Stories](#c345104)

© Universität Wien/ Martin Sowa

title: Our offers and projects
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
text:
On this page you will find current offers and projects from "School meets Uni" as well as from other institutions of the University of Vienna, which are aimed specifically at pupils, teachers and school classes as well as at those students, who have recently started their studies at the University of Vienna:

- [Dr. Hans Riegel prizes](#c539634)
- [School meets Uni - Offers for school classes](#c319792)
- [Youth Science Competition of the University of Vienna](/en/degree-programmes/we-cooperate-with/#c274336)
- [Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna](#c409284)

© Universität Wien/ Martin Sowa

As part of the project "School meets Uni", we offer school classes the opportunity to visit the University of Vienna and "get a taste of university life". In one-hour, free of cost information lectures, we provide a general overview of the differences between school and university, our studies and the admission procedure at the University of Vienna.

Are you interested in a school lecture for your class? Just send us an email: schule-trifft-uni@univie.ac.at.

title: Our offers and projects
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
heading_paths: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
Library tours and research training for school classes
School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

title: Our offers and projects
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
text:
The University of Vienna and the charitable private foundation Kaiserschild award the Dr. Hans Riegel prizes.

The prizes are awarded to pupils in their final school year for the three best prescientific papers in each of the following subjects: Biology, Chemistry, Geography, Computer Science, Mathematics and Physics. The prescientific papers are evaluated by a jury of university professors of the University of Vienna.

All pupils of General Secondary Schools (AHS) in Vienna are free to participate.

The submission deadline for 2023 is April 30th.

[Click here to know more](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/studienangebot/studienwahl/dr-hans-riegel-fachpreise/) (in German)

© Kaiserschild Stiftung

Pupils who will be the first in their family to study at an Austrian university receive support in the [First Generation Project](https://kinderbuero-uniwien.at/en/science-communication/first-generation/) of the Children's Office. Various fields of study and procedures are taught in workshop units. In three modules, a comprehensive overview of the fields of study is given, concrete sources of information are presented and tips and tricks for studying are explained.

title: Our offers and projects
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
heading_paths: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
text:
Who knows the trick of the lotus blossom? What does a living fossil look like? Where does Vienna's biggest bamboo grove grow?

The Botanical Garden of the University of Vienna offers school classes a unique opportunity to experience nature and explore plants in the middle of the city.

The aim of the project Grüne Schule (Green School) is to give pupils an understanding of the fascinating world of plants. Guided tours and workshops stimulate the interest in researching and exploring nature. Expertise, stunning details and inspiring histories as well as the didactic mediation take centre stage - an exciting and adecuate provision of knowledge for our young generation.

[Click here to know more](http://grueneschule.univie.ac.at) (in German)

© Universität Wien / derknopfdruecker.com

[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

Apart from this, the website also contains a clear listing of all open Studieren Probieren dates and free places.

title: We cooperate with ...
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Vienna Chamber of Labour: How to suceed in your studies!
heading_paths: Vienna Chamber of Labour: How to suceed in your studies!
text:
The Vienna Chamber of Labour (Arbeiterkammer Wien - AK Wien) particularly supports those being first of their family to start studying or considering this. The start of studies is frequently connected with insecurities, especially if there are no "academic footprints" within your family which you can follow.

Therefore, AK Wien offers information material and presentations on how to make the start and the time of their studies a success.  The message for the acadmic pioneers is: Do not despair, many first-year students are in the same situation as you! Get helpful advice on the organisation of studies, possible funding and much more!

- [Further information for academic pioneers is available here](https://wien.arbeiterkammer.at/service/broschueren/Bildung/So_gelingt_dein_Studium.html) (in German)

© AK Wien

title: We cooperate with ...
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: "Pupils go to university" - OeAD, Young Science
heading_paths: "Pupils go to university" - OeAD, Young Science
text:
The University of Vienna enables particularly committed pupils to attend courses even before they finish secondary school. After school graduation, examinations will be recognised for the degree programme. The University of Vienna cooperates with the Zentrum für die Zusammenarbeit von Wissenschaft und Schule (OeAD, Young Science).

In the course of the programme "Pupils go to university" prospective students can attend university courses, pass exams, have an in-depth look at specific areas of interest, get to know a degree programme and shorten their overall study time. Certain degree programmes are open to these young students. Also, they are exempted from the tuition fee and only have to pay the Students' Union fee. Additionally to the participation in this programme, pupils have the possibility of being accompanied by a mentor.

- [Further information on the programme](https://youngscience.at/de/angebote/schuelerinnen-an-die-hochschulen/) (available only in German)
- [List with all possible degree programmes](https://youngscience.at/fileadmin/Dokumente/youngscience.at/Dokumente/Sandhos/Leitfaeden_der_Hochschulen/Universitaet_Wien_Waehlbare_Studienrichtungen.pdf) (available only in German)

© OeAD, Young Science

text: The Zentrum für die Zusammenarbeit von Wissenschaft und Schule (Centre for Cooperation of Science and Schools) offers Austrian pupils diverse possibilities to get in touch and work together with researchers. **Topics for pre-scientific paper/diploma thesis****:** On the Young Science thematic platform young people can find numerous suggestions for their final papers originating from Austrian research projects, involving reading suggestions and support offered by researchers. **School visits of researchers****:** In the Framework of the initiative "Young Science ambassadors" researchers from all over Austria voluntarily visit school classes to speak of their everyday work. **Participation in Research projects****:** Pupils can participate in numerous research projects and Support the sciences, for example by collecting data. Once a year, the Citizen Science Award invites all schools (and those interested in science) to participate in research projects and win prizes. title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Offered bachelor/diploma programmes - From African Studies to Transcultural Communication
text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

[### alphabetical order](#c285425)

[### by topic](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

title: Master Programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Master Programmes - From Advanced Theological Studies to Zoology
text:
Here you find a list of all master programmes offered at the University of Vienna.

The instruction language of most master degree programmes is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/). Information about [programmes in another language can be found here](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/).

Information about the application process can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance examination

- [Teacher Education Programme (bachelor and master)](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)
embed_text:
Degree programmes > Master Access Guide (MAG)

Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

title: Joint Curricula
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Joint Programmes are offered in cooperation with one or several other universities. The completion of courses at several universities are an inherent part of the degree programme.

title: Extension curricula and alternative extensions
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Almost all bachelor’s programmes require students to complete extension curricula (EC) in addition to the regular compulsory and elective subjects. An EC is a predifined module group of up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits is defined in the curriculum of your degree programme.

In order to complete an EC, you must first register for the EC in [u:space](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/#c216328). You will be able to register for individual courses of the EC after having passed the STEOP.

Nearly all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless otherwise specified, you may freely choose from the range of EC offered.

You can also complete [alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c273245) of a number of at least 15 ECTS instead of an EC in most bachelor's degree programmes..

- If you have questions about the content of an EC and the recognition of examination results from an EC, please get in touch with the [studies service unit (SSS)/studies service center (SSC)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for the EC.
- If you have questions about the recognition of an EC for your degree programme, please contact the [SSS/SSC](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme.

- [Alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228260)
- [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c216354)
- [Extension curricula (EC)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228433)

title: Extension curricula and alternative extensions
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
With some ECs, you can fulfil the qualitative admission requirements (QZB) for certain master programmes.

English language master programmes with QZBs and the required ECs include:

- [Applied Economics](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/applied-economics-master/)
  - EC: [Economic Methods](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/economic-methods-15-ects-credits/)
  - EC: [Quantitative Principles of Economics](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/quantitative-principles-of-economics-17-ects-credits/)

- [Banking and Finance](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/banking-and-finance-master/)
  - EC: [Principles of Economics](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/principles-of-economics-15-ects-credits/)
  - EC: [Quantitative Principles of Economics](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/quantitative-principles-of-economics-17-ects-credits/)

- [Cognition, Behavior and Neurobiology (CoBeNe)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/cognition-behavior-and-neurobiology-master/)
  - EC: [Cognition, Behavior and Neurobiology](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/cognition-behavior-and-neurobiology-15-ects-credits/)
  - EC: [Ecology](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/ecology-15-ects-credits/)
  - EC: [Evolutionary Anthropology](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/evolutionary-anthropology-15-ects-credits/)

- [Computational Science](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/computational-science-master/)
  - EC: [Fundamentals for Computational Science](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/fundamentals-for-computational-science-18-ects-credits/)

Find further information about the [admission to master programmes](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) and about QZBs in the [Master Access Guide](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/).

For detailed information about all other extension curricula offered at the University of Vienna, please see the German version of this site. [Information about the courses offered in the extension curricula can be found in u:find (see list below).](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/vvz_sub.html?semester=2018W&path=212317)

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

title: Postgraduate Center
heading_paths: Continuing Education  at the University of Vienna
text:
[# Postgraduate Center](/en/)

©
Universität Wien

- [## Education and Democracy](/en/education-democracy)
- [## Digitalization and Communication](/en/digitalization-communication)
- [## Pharmacy and Health](/en/pharmacy-health)
- [## Psychotherapy](/en/psychotherapy)
- [## Law](/en/law)
- [## Environment and Sustainability](/en/environment-sustainability)
- [## University Extension](/en/university-extension)

Do you want to expand your professional competencies, open new perspectives for your career, or pursue advanced academic training? The **Postgraduate Center of the University of Vienna** supports you on your educational path with a diverse portfolio of continuing education programs, certificate programs, master’s programs, and openly accessible education formats.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

Our programs combine the latest research with practice-orientiented knowledge and stand out for their high quality, interdisciplinary content, and international outlook. This offers you an academic continuing education that purposefully supports your professional and personal development.

The **Campus of the University of Vienna** is your central hub for continuing education. We look forward to welcoming you to the Postgraduate Center – the University of Vienna’s competence center for professional continuing education and lifelong learning.

©
Postgraduate Center Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

We accompany prospective students and participants of our continuing education programs on **Instagram, Facebook**, and **LinkedIn**, providing up-to-date insights into our courses, events, and campus life. For those who want to dive even deeper, our **YouTube** channel offers exciting video formats and behind-the-scenes features.

title: The admission procedure at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Detailed information on the first-time admission to bachelor, diploma and master programmes as well as further information on the admission.

You can find information on the admission procedure to doctoral/PhD programmes directly via the [website of the Center for Doctoral Studies](http://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en).

[## Bachelor/diploma](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)

title: The admission procedure at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Please use our [contact form](/en/contact-form/). It allows you to be more flexible and to have your request documented in written form.

For reasons of data protection, we cannot provide any information related to personal data on the phone.

You can reach us via telephone by calling +43/1/4277-12100 at the following times:

- Monday and Thursday: from 12:30 p.m. to 01:30 p.m.

Please take note of the working hours of the Admission Office for the timely submission of applications to the Admission Office that are not submitted via u:space. You can find a summary of the working hours [in this proclamation](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Zulassung/Kundmachung_zur_rechtswirksamen_Einbringung_von_Anbringen.pdf) (in German only).

[## Appointment online first-time admission](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

“Drawing a number and waiting in line” is old news. Comfortably complete your first-time admission at home via video chat. Here you can find detailed [info regarding booking your appointment for online first-time admission.](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

© Universität Wien/derknopfdruecker.com

[## Online info-presentations](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/)

You are interested in studying at the University of Vienna? Come to our info-presentations! In the presentations you will learn everything you need to know for your application to admission to a degree programme. During and after the presentation you can ask your questions in the chat.

It does not matter if you are already in Vienna, or listening from another place: you can comfortably attend the presentation via your own device. Here you can find details regarding [online info-presentation](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/).

title: Admission to master programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

Admission > Admission to master programmes

Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

  - [Psychotherapy](/en/admission/master-programmes/psychotherapy/)
  - [Religion in Europe](/en/admission/master-programmes/religion-in-europe/)
  - [Research in Economics and Finance](/en/admission/master-programmes/research-in-economics-and-finance/)
  - [Science-Technology-Society](/en/admission/master-programmes/science-technology-society/)

To be admitted to a master programme, you must have completed a bachelor/diploma programme in the same discipline (= eligible degree programme). In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/), you can find out with which previous degree programmes and under which conditions admission to a specific master programme is possible. If your previous degree programme is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the specific master programme, a decision whether admission is possible will be made on a case-by-case basis. The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

Master students of other universities can get enrolled to an eligible degree programme at the University of Vienna for one semester as [Visiting Master](/en/admission/master-programmes/visiting-master/) students.

title: Statistical data on master programmes with selection procedure
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Statistical data on master programmes with selection procedure
heading_paths: All other master programmes with selection procedure
text:
How many people have applied for a master programme with selection procedure, and how many places are available in my preferred degree programme? We believe it is important to provide you with a clear insight into the procedures and general conditions. On this page, you will find up-to-date statistical information on master programmes with selection procedure at the University of Vienna.

- Registered for the selection procedure: 430
- Study places: 40

The written test takes place on 6 July 2026. The number of participants will be announced thereafter.

- Registered for the selection procedure: 409
- Study places: 50

The written test takes place on 7 July 2026. The number of participants will be announced thereafter.

For all other master programmes with selection procedure the study places are allocated by selection committees. All applications are currently being reviewed. The applicants will be informed of the outcome by the end of May.

- [Information about the test day](/en/admission/master-programmes/day-of-the-written-test-for-master-programmes-with-selection-procedure/)

title: Change of degree programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
heading_paths: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

- [Receiving the secondary-school leaving certificate "Nachmatura" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/)
- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
heading_paths: EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](https://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_paths: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
text: Entrance exam procedures only take place once per academic year (before the winter semester). The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. 1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure. 2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/entrance-exam/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code. Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted". Only then you are registered for the entrance exam procedure. 3. Participate in the [entrance exam](/en/entrance-exam/). 4. An admission to the degree programme is only possible after you have successfully participated in the entrance exam procedure. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after the results have been announced. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few

text: Entrance exam procedures only take place once per academic year (before the winter semester). heading_paths: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
text: #### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

Winter semester 2026/27:

title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

/

Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

title: Change of master programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

heading_paths: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

title: Additional degree programmes
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: General Information regarding admission to an additional degree programme
text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You would like to be admitted to an additional bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
heading_paths: EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](https://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. Starting from the beginning of the admission period, you will be automatically admitted to the additional degree programme within five working days. Check the status of your admission in u:space > Study overview. If you have not been admitted within this timeframe, please contact the Admission Office via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933).

title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme

text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

heading_paths: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
text: Entrance exam procedures only take place once per academic year (before the winter semester). The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. 1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure. 2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/entrance-exam/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code. Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted". Only then you are registered for the entrance exam procedure. 3. Participate in the [entrance exam](/en/entrance-exam/). 4. An admission to the degree programme is only possible after you have successfully participated in the entrance exam procedure. 5. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after the results have been announced. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days you will see the new degree programme in u:space > Study overview. You will

Choose the desired degree programme in u:space > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/entrance-exam/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code. Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted". Only then you are registered for the entrance exam procedure and the admission office can process your application. 3. Participate in the [entrance exam](/en/entrance-exam/). 4. An admission to the degree programme is only possible after you have successfully participated in the entrance exam procedure and fulfil all admission requirements. After a processing time of few weeks you will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space >

text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Starting from the beginning of the admission period, you will be automatically admitted to the additional degree programme within five working days. Check the status of your admission in u:space > Study overview. If you have not been admitted within this timeframe, please contact the Admission Office via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933).

title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I be admitted to an additional degree programme?

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your admission to an additional degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The admission to the additional degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the admission to the additional degree programme.

title: Additional master programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c807419)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c807424)
