heading_paths: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.
embed_text:
Admission > Additional master programme > Master programmes without entrance exam procedure > Note for graduates of the University of Vienna

The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: How can I close degree programmes?
text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
heading_paths: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: When will my degree programme be closed automatically?
Can I resume my degree programme later?
text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.

- What do I have to keep in mind regarding pending examination results?

  If you want to take further exams or receive examination results, wait until all examination results have been entered by the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme and appear in u:space. After a degree programme has been closed, it is neither possible to take exams nor to enter these examination results.
- When can I close my degree proramme(s)?

  You can close your degree programme(s) year-round via our [Servicdesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz).

title: Resumption of the same degree programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: General information about the resumption of studies
heading_paths: General information about the resumption of studies
text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. After you have resumed your studies, you can see if you still have to complete the minimum academic achievement in u:space. Make sure to check how many semesters you have left to complete the minimum academic achievement. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Curriculum: You will be admitted to the current curriculum without any exception. If the curriculum has changed, you have to submit an application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) (as you did when you first applied to the degree programme. We therefore recommend: Submit your application for the resumption of studies as early as possible! - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of resumption: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities:

text: You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.
embed_text:
Resumption of studies > Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme

You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.

title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
heading_paths: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

Apply for admission in u:space within the application period, as described in the [information regarding the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). You do not have to book an online appointment to complete the admission procedure. Your further steps depend on the country of issuance of your secondary-school leaving certificate and your citizenship. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship are from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland: Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the admission period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the admission and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country: Wait for your admission letter. The processing may take several weeks. title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme

heading_paths: Different bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure
text: You want to be admitted to a different bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure? Apply for admission in u:space within the application period, as described in the [information about the entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/). The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes takes place once per academic year. The entrance exam is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. Admission to a degree programme is only possible after successful completion of the entrance exam procedure. You do not need to book an online appointment to complete the admission procedure. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) after the announcement of results and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. The sporting aptitude admission test takes place twice per year. Therefore, the application for [admission to the bachelor programme in Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/) is also possible before the summer semester. title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
heading_paths: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

title: Application and admission periods
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Your first step before starting your studies is applying for admission to a degree programme. You can submit your application for [first-time admission](/en/admission-procedure/), [change of degree programme](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/), [admission to an additional degree programme](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/) and [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) within the application period.

The application period depends on your citizenship and the degree programme of your choice. You are unsure which period applies to you? Choose the degree programme you are interested in at "[Admission](/en/admission-procedure/)". In the step-by-step instruction of the admission period you find detailed info on the application procedure.

Our tip: Submit your application for admission to the degree programme at the beginning of the application period! Thereby you can complete the admission procedure at the beginning of the admission period and have an easy start of your studies.

Application periods for EU/EEA/CH citizens and [Non-EU/EEA nationals with equivalent status](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 5 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 5 February 2027

Application periods for citizens of non-EU/EEA countries

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 3 August 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027

Our tip: Submit your application for admission to the degree programme at the beginning of the application period! Thereby you can complete the admission procedure at the beginning of the admission period and have an easy start of your studies.

Application periods for EU/EEA/CH citizens and [Non-EU/EEA nationals with equivalent status](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 5 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 5 February 2027

Application periods for citizens of non-EU/EEA countries

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 3 August 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027

title: Application and admission periods

Admission period for bachelor/diploma and master programmes
text:
Your second step before starting your studies is the completion of the admission procedure within the admission period.

The admission is only possible if you have applied for admission within the application period and fulfil all admission requirements.

Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as soon as possible. You can only register for courses and exams after you have been admitted to the degree programme of your choice.

- from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- semester start: 1. October

- from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- semester start: 1 March

title: Application and admission periods
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)
embed_text:

over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

title: Proof of German language proficiency
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: General information about the German language proficiency
heading_paths: General information about the German language proficiency
text: - You need a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 when applying for admission. - In order to acquire level C1, which is required for the admission to all bachelor/diploma programmes instructed in German and most master programmes, you may participate in a German course of the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) (VWU). - While attending the VWU, you have to [consider some things](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) regarding your admission to the University of Vienna. - In case you apply for a bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure, you will be able to participate in the procedure even though you do not yet have an adequate proof of German language proficiency. However, you must have this proof at the time of enrolment. - For the following master programmes, a proof of German language proficiency at level C1 or C2 is required for the application: - [Master programme in German as a Foreign and Second Language (C1)](/en/admission/master-programmes/german-as-a-foreign-and-second-language/) - [Master programme in Translation (C2)](https://transvienna.univie.ac.at/studium/masterstudium-translation/voraussetzungen/)It is not possible to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme in order to be admitted to these master programmes. - In case you apply for a [master programme instructed in English](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/#c157833) or the [bachelor programme Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/), you will not be able to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme, as no proof of German language proficiency is required.

You must provide a proof of English language proficiency to be admitted to the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science or [English or multilingual master programmes](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) at the Universitiy of Vienna.

title: Proof of English language proficiency
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
heading_paths: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
text:
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

title: Proof of English language proficiency
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level C1
text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)
- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)
embed_text:
Admission > Proof of English language proficiency

- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)
- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)

title: Proof of English language proficiency
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
This website is aimed at students from Non-EU/EEA countries who are interested in studying at the University of Vienna. Your steps in the admission procedure depend on several questions.

- Which degree programme are you interested in?
- Is the degree programme instructed in German or English?
- Which country did you complete your previous education in?

If your previous education is from specific countries, you have to keep several things in mind. Carefully read the info about the particularities with education from specific countries, before you submit your application for admission to a degree programme. Afterwards follow the step-by-step instructions for the [admission to bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/) or [admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

Each application is processed individually. We cannot give you any information if your previous education is suitable for admission at the University of Vienna in advance.

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_paths: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
heading_paths: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
text: / - Afghanistan If you only have a secondary-school leaving certificate from Afghanistan, you cannot be admitted to a bachelor or diploma programme at the University of Vienna. The secondary-school leaving certificate from Afghanistan (12-Grade Graduation Certificate) is not adequate as proof of the general university entrance qualification. The differences to a secondary-school leaving certificate from Austria are too large. Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your secondary-school leaving certificate is from Afghanistan and you also have already studied a degree programme you can submit an application for admission to a bachelor or diploma programme. Each application has to be processed individually. - Bangladesh Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - China If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate in China and apply for a bachelor/diploma programme, you require a proof of your university entrance exam Gaokao with [Apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and certification by the [APS of the German embassy in Beijing](https://www.aps.org.cn/internationale-kooperationen/osterreich) (German/Chinese only). --- Exception: If you have already completed a bachelor/diploma programme (at least three years duration or at least 180 ECTS) you do not need a proof of your university entrance exam. The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - India Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - Indonesia If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate in

The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - Ukraine Starting from the beginning of the application period for the summer semester 2027: If you have a Ukrainan secondary-school leaving certificate and are applying for admission to a bachelor’s or diploma programme, you will need the NMT (National Multi-Subject Test): - a minimum of 100 points required - validity: 4 years - applies to diplomas awarded starting in the 2021–2022 school year - must be completed in at least 4 subjects In addition, your Ukrainian educational documents require an [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland ("A-levels") If you receive the General Certificate of Education (GCE/"A-Levels"), you have to fulfil the following requirements: - Four general subjects that are independent from one another have to be completed with grade A\*, A, B or C (respectively grades 9 - 4). These subjects must include: - a foreign language - Mathematics - one of the subjects Biology, Physics or Chemistry - a subject of the Humanities - At least three of the four subjects have to be completed at the GCE Advanced level, the

The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - Ukraine Starting from the beginning of the application period for the summer semester 2027: If you have a Ukrainan secondary-school leaving certificate and are applying for admission to a bachelor’s or diploma programme, you will need the NMT (National Multi-Subject Test): - a minimum of 100 points required - validity: 4 years - applies to diplomas awarded starting in the 2021–2022 school year - must be completed in at least 4 subjects In addition, your Ukrainian educational documents require an [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland ("A-levels") If you receive the General Certificate of Education (GCE/"A-Levels"), you have to fulfil the following requirements: - Four general subjects that are independent from one another have to be completed with grade A\*, A, B or C (respectively grades 9 - 4). title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
heading_paths: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
text: include: - a foreign language - Mathematics - one of the subjects Biology, Physics or Chemistry - a subject of the Humanities - At least three of the four subjects have to be completed at the GCE Advanced level, the fourth subjects at the level of GCSE or IGCSE. - The Advanced Supplementary Level (AS) is to be equatable with the level of the GCSE under the aforementioned conditions. An A-Level in the subject German, can only be accepted as [proof German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/). This does not replace the required proof of German language proficiency at level C1. Therefore you must provide a [proof of German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/), before you can be admitted to the degree programme.

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
heading_paths: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?

text: / - African countries The Higher National Diploma does not fulfil the requirements for admission to a master programme. --- Special case Nigeria: The Austrian embassy or the Austrian consulate require a proof of the University of Vienna, before the over-legalisation can be issued? Proceed as follows: 1. Let your documents be legalised by the ministry of education. 2. Let the documents that were legalised by the ministry of education be legalised by the ministry of foreign affairs. 3. Apply for admission to the degree programme with these documents within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). You will receive an automatic confirmation, that you have applied for admission at the University of Vienna via e-mail. 4. Provide this confirmation at the Austrian representation and let your documents be over-legalised. This automatic confirmation is accepted as proof. 5. A few weeks after your application you will receive an amendment request with a deadline for submission (as the over-legalisaiton by the Austrian representation was missing in your original submission). 6. Submit the documents with the full diplomatic certification within the deadline. 7. Wait for your admission letter. - Afghanistan If you have only completed a bachelor programme in Afghanistan, you cannot be admitted to a master programme at the University of Vienna. The differences regarding the level of education are too large. Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your bachelor

title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Can I apply without the required legalisation (according to the issuing country)? No, this is not possible. You have to fulfil the formal requirements. This means that you must provide all required documents in your application for admission to the degree programme. Only apply for admission, if you fulfil all requirements and if you have all required documents. - I don't have my bachelor certificate yet. Can I already apply for admission to a master programme? You can only apply for admission after you have entirely completed your degree programme and have lost the entitlement to take exams. However, you do not have to have the final documents confirming the completion of your studies. The University of Vienna also accepts a confirmation issued by your educational institution about the completion of studies in combination with your final transcripts/diploma supplement, as long as this also contains the legalisation required for your country. Confirmations that you will complete your degree programme cannot be accepted (only exception: master programmes with selection exam procedure). Provisional transcripts cannot be accepted as well. - Can I already know which supplementary exams I will have to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university

title: Legalisation and translation
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You wish to apply for admission and your documents were issued in a non-EU/EEA country? Please have them translated after they were diplomatically certified (i.e. legalised) first.

Your documents were issued in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland? Such documents need only [be translated](#c1122153), while legalisation is not required.

There are four possibilities:

- [Full diplomatic certification](#c1121827)
- [Apostille](#c1121828)
- [Diplomatic certification is not required](#c1121829)
- [Diplomatic certification is temporarily deferred](#c1121830)

title: Legalisation and translation
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
1. **Inform yourself in the issuing country about the national public authority responsible for legalising documents.**
   The process of legalisation usually begins at the ministry of education, followed by the ministry for foreign affairs. Finally, the appropriate Austrian representation authority (embassy or consulate) issues an over-legalisation. In some countries, in lieu of the ministry of foreign affairs, the ministry of justice is responsible for legalising documents.
2. **Have the appropriate ministries certify your documents by means of the respective seals and attestation clauses.**
3. **Present your documents to the Austrian representation authorities.**
   Schedule an appointment with the [appropriate representation authority](https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/embassies-consulates/search-for-austrian-representations) and provide them with your legalised documents. The embassy or consulate will issue an over-legalisation.
4. **Have your documents translated.**
   Your [documents as well as all seals and attestation clauses they contain have to be translated](#c1122153), if they were not issued in German or English.
5. **Apply for admission to a study programme**
   Scan all pages of your documents, including all seals and (if necessary) the translation. After assuring yourself that the scan is legible, combine your documents into a PDF-file. Follow the next steps in the [admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/).

Finally, the appropriate Austrian representation authority (embassy or consulate) issues an over-legalisation. In some countries, in lieu of the ministry of foreign affairs, the ministry of justice is responsible for legalising documents.
2. **Have the appropriate ministries certify your documents by means of the respective seals and attestation clauses.**
3. **Present your documents to the Austrian representation authorities.**
   Schedule an appointment with the [appropriate representation authority](https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/embassies-consulates/search-for-austrian-representations) and provide them with your legalised documents. The embassy or consulate will issue an over-legalisation.
4. **Have your documents translated.**
   Your [documents as well as all seals and attestation clauses they contain have to be translated](#c1122153), if they were not issued in German or English.
5. **Apply for admission to a study programme**
   Scan all pages of your documents, including all seals and (if necessary) the translation. After assuring yourself that the scan is legible, combine your documents into a PDF-file. Follow the next steps in the [admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/).

title: Legalisation and translation
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
1. Inform yourself in the issuing country which national public authority may issue an apostille, and if there are any requirements.
2. Have an apostille added to your documents.
   The apostille can be added on the document itself, on an attached page, or by means of an electronic apostille. Do not detach the apostille! The apostille must attest any signature(s) by the issuing educational institution (directorate, rectorate, or deanship) that the document bears. The signature of the notary or translation agency may not be attested.
3. Have your documents translated.
   Your [documents as well as the apostille has to be translated](#c1122153), if they were not issued in German or English.
4. Apply for admission to a study programme.
   Scan all pages of your document, including all seals and (if necessary) the translation. After assuring yourself that the scan is legible, combine your documents into a PDF-file. Follow the next steps in the [admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/).

Documents issued in certain countries do not need to be diplomatically certified. However, we require an [official translation in German or English](#c1122153), if the documents were not issued in either of these languages.

Documents issued in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from all legalisation requirements.

title: Legalisation and translation
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: How do my documents become translated?
heading_paths: How do my documents become translated?
text: We only accept documents that were issued in German or English. If your documents were issued in a different language, a translation is required. Please regard the following terms: - If full diplomatic certification is required, your documents have to be legalised first. Only then they may be translated including all stamps, seals, and certification notes. - An apostille must also be translated. This means that the apostille has to be affixed to the original document first. Afterwards it can be translated. - The documents must be translated by official sworn and court certified translators. - The translation must be inseparably attached to the original document. Do not detach the documents (not even when scanning them!). Else the translation will be void. # FAQ / - Which documents require diplomatic certification? We require diplomatic certification of all documents that attest your prior education. - Bachelor/diploma programmes: All pages of your secondary school leaving certificate and, if applicable, transcripts of records/diplomas (if you have studied at a university before). - Master programmes: Your higher education degree certificate, relevant transcripts of records and/or the diploma supplement. - My documents have not been diplomatically certified yet. Can I still apply for admission? No, as you may only apply once all documents have been diplomatically certified and translated, as required. If your application is incomplete, it may prolong its lead time. - For how

If your application is incomplete, it may prolong its lead time. - For how long is the diplomatic certification valid? A diplomatic certification is valid indefinitely. - I am a non-EU/EEA citizen, but I previously studied in an EU/EEA country/Switzerland or graduated from school there. title: Competence check at VFS Global
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Important information about the competence check
text:
You have completed your previous education in one of the following countries and are interested in pursuing a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna?

- India
- Pakistan
- Bangladesh

Starting from winter semester 2026/27, you need to take a competence check at VFS Global before submitting an application for admission to a degree programme. You can find all the relevant information on this page.

The competence check by VFS Global...

- ... takes place **in person and on site** at the VFS Global locations.
- ... **is mandatory** for all prospective students who have completed their previous education in Bangladesh, India or Pakistan and wish to begin a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna in winter semester 2026/27 or thereafter.
- ... **does not replace the formal and content-related review** of your application by the Admission Office.
- **... does not replace** successful participation in the **entrance exam/aptitude test or selection procedure**.
- **... does not replace** completion of **supplementary examinations in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities** (if any are prescribed in your admission letter).

- **... does not replace** completion of **supplementary examinations in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities** (if any are prescribed in your admission letter).

title: Competence check at VFS Global
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
heading_paths: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

title: Competence check at VFS Global
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Step 2: Book an appointment for the competence check
Step 2: Book an appointment for the competence check
Step 3: Participate in the competence check
Step 4: Submit your application for admission to the degree programme
Step 4: Submit your application for admission to the degree programme
text:
You fulfil the admission requirements? Book an appointment for the competence check at VFS Global and pay the fee of around € 100 directly to VFS Global.

When booking your appointment, you have to indicate which Austrian university you would like to study at and which degree programme you are interested in.

[Book the appointment here](https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=LWHGAXGqfEO0Y6oDCSiOO28cS5yMYn1Coq9ZncGMJhxUNDBPSTVOM0xFSUdESVFIRDVXOEFQSE5aUS4u&route=shorturl)

VFS Global will check the following during your personal appointment:

- Your identity (passport)
- Your documents (depending on your chosen degree programme)
- Your general competence in the following areas

- German (for degree programmes instructed in German)
- English
- Mathematics
- Biology
- Chemistry
- History/Geography
- Physics

Follow the steps in the admission procedure, depending on your desired degree programme.

Upload the confirmation of VFS Global in u:space at "Further documents and proofs" as document type "VFS certificate of participation". VFS Global will send the results of the competence check directly to the University of Vienna.

Different [application periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) apply to bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure and to master programmes with selection procedure. You can only apply for these degree programmes once a year (in spring)! So make sure you find out in good time whether your desired degree programme has an entrance exam/selection procedure.

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection who possess a secondary-school leaving certificate and/or a university degree from a non-EU/EEA country may apply for admission to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. The requirements are as follows: you must fulfil the admission requirements and adhere to the [application and admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

The University of Vienna offers over 150 bachelor, master and doctoral programmes. You can get more detailed information about the programmes offered on the following pages:

- [All degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/)
- [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) of the degree programmes](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)
- [Students’ Union (ÖH) of the University of Vienna](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Center for Doctoral Studies of the University of Vienna](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/)

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
heading_paths: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
heading_paths: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
text: [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. The bachelor programme in [Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/) (as well as the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education) require the successful participation in the sporting aptitude admission test (only available in German). The test takes place twice per academic year (ahead of the respective winter and summer semester) and is valid for four semesters. Admission to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education also requires the successful participation in the entrance exam test for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. Choose your desired degree programme and follow the [step-by-step admission guide](/en/admission-procedure/).

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
heading_paths: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
text:
All bachelor/diploma programmes as well as master programmes instructed in German require proof of [German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/) at the time of application. Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: How much is the tuition fee?
heading_paths: How much is the tuition fee?
text:
Students who reside in Austria as asylum seekers, recognised refugees (persons entitled to asylum, not displaced persons), or people granted subsidiary protection may take a discounted German language course in the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) if they fulfil all admission requirements. You will be admitted as a non-degree student while you attend the VWU. During this time, you are only required to pay the [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).

Otherwise, the tuition fee for degree programmes depends on your citizenship. Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection may file for [equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) and, if granted, are then exempt from paying the tuition fee for the standard duration of your study programme + two tolerance semesters for a bachelor/diploma or master programme. During this time, you are only required to pay the Students’ Union fee.

Before commencing your studies, please consider how starting a degree programme may impact your financial situation or benefits. Different regulations apply depending on your student or resident status.

- [Allowance ("Grundversorgung") for asylum seekers](https://www.fluechtlinge.wien/grundversorgung/) (in German)
- [Needs-based minimum benefit for recognised refugees and people granted subsidiary protection](https://www.wien.gv.at/gesundheit/leistungen/mindestsicherung/) (in German)
- [Study grant for recognised refugees by the Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studienbeihilfe) (in German)
- [Austrian Database for Scholarships and Research Grants](https://grants.at/en/)

title: Information for asylum seekers
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: How can I get my academic diplomas/degrees recognised?
How do I organise/plan my studies after the admission?
text:
Through the process of nostrification, a foreign university degree can get recognised as equivalent to an Austrian university degree programme. This recognition also entails all rights and duties that follow, such as bearing an academic title and having permission to purse a certain profession, among others.

The official Austrian information centre for international nostrifications in the university field is [ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA](https://www.aais.at/).

You may file for nostrification at the [office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/).

In order to apply for admission to another degree programme, you do not have to have your higher education degree recognised through nostrification.

Once you have been admitted to a degree programme, you may [plan your semester](/en/semester-planning/) and schedule your classes.

If you are in need of assistance, you may reach out to the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme. The [Student representatives (STV)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/vertretung/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen) of your degree programme can also help you with questions related to organising your studies.

During your studies, the [Center for Teaching and Learning (CTL)](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) will offer valuable support. The [STEOP-Mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/your-first-semester/steop-mentoring/) programme will help you particularly at the beginning of your studies. Any questions or concerns that you may have can be addressed there, while mentors will also provide insights about organising your study programme and exams as well as useful advice on reading and studying techniques.

title: Leave of absence
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Leave of absence before the beginning of the semester
Leave of absence during of the semester
text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

title: Leave of absence
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

title: Leave of absence
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
heading_paths: What do you need to consider?
text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

title: Leave of absence
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: A leave of absence is not an option for you?
A leave of absence is not an option for you?
text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

- [§ 21 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), § 67](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

title: Leave of absence

text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)
