title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Step 1: Create your u:account and register in u:space
text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Step 3: Register for courses and exams
How do I continue my co-registration?
text:
1. It can take up to two weeks to process your application. As soon as your application has been approved, you will receive an e-mail notification. Please also check your spam folder if necessary.
2. Afterwards you can register for courses and exams in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html).
   - Registration periods may vary depending on the course and degree programme.
   - The admission does not guarantee a place in an ongoing course with continuous assessment.
   - Further information regarding  semester planning, registration for courses and exams, etc. is available on our website for [study organisation](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/study-organisation/).

As soon as the co-registration has been approved, you can order the [u:card](https://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) (Personal matters > My u:card). However, your u:card is not valid as a student ID card, but only as a library card.

You can also print your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) > Personal matters > My documents.

In order to continue your co-registration, submit the following documents

- [the pre-approval form](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/745) within the above mentioned application period of the respective semester.

title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: When is a co-registration not possible?
text:
- If you are already admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. You can participate in courses of another degree programme and, if needed, get them recognised at another university.
- If you study at a university of applied sciences.
- If you study at a university college of teacher education - with the esception of the [Teacher Education Programme for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/)
- If you are a student of a non-Austrian university.
- If you are admitted at a different university within a student mobility programme.
- If you are a non-degree student at your home university.
- If you are currently taking a leave of absence at a different university.
- If you study at a private university.

[§ 63 para. 9, Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/NormDokument.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128&Artikel=&Paragraf=63&Anlage=&Uebergangsrecht=)

- If you are a student of a non-Austrian university.
- If you are admitted at a different university within a student mobility programme.
- If you are a non-degree student at your home university.
- If you are currently taking a leave of absence at a different university.
- If you study at a private university.

text: The registration period for the course I would like to complete ends earlier than the application period for co-registration. What deadline do I have to meet? You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet

title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Making up for exams for admission to master programmes
text:
You can be admitted to non-degree programmes for different reasons. In a non-degree programme

- ... you are not admitted to a specific degree programme.
- ... you can download your student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment in u:space, as soon as your [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) is received by the University of Vienna.
- ... a transcript of records will be issued for any exams you take. It is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
- ... you have to take note of several consequences.

Choose the reasons, why you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme. You will find info on the admission procedure and possible consequences you have to keep in mind as a non-degree student.

title: Studying in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
text:
© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

You can attend courses and take the supplementary examinations prescribed in your admission letter in the VWU. This is necessary to be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. You may take up to four semesters to pass all supplementary examinations at the VWU.

You can find important information for your time at the VWU and your admission as non-degree student at the University of Vienna on these pages.

[## Bachelor/diploma](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/vwu-for-bachelordiploma-programmes/)

[## Master](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/vwu-for-master-programmes/)

Please inform yourself about the VWU courses, costs and deadlines directly at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/)

title: Studying in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
heading_path: Important note for students in the VWU
heading_paths: Important note for students in the VWU
text:
Once you have been admitted to the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities as a non-degree student, only the supplementary examination in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch, EPD)  is accepted as a proof of German language proficiency! It is not possible to leave the preparation programme and take a German language exam elsewhere. Disregard may lead to severe consequences such as the loss of your residence permit.

Persons who have quit the VWU and have passed a different German language exam in the meanwhile can still take the supplementary examination in German (EPD).

[Detailed information on the regulation](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/detailed-information/)

While you are admitted to the non-degree programme "attending the VWU" ("Besuch des Vorstudienlehrgangs"), you are only allowed to attend courses in the VWU and complete the prescribed supplementary examinations there. Taking exams from the degree programme is not allowed.

Once you have been admitted to the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities as a non-degree student, only the supplementary examination in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch, EPD)  is accepted as a proof of German language proficiency! It is not possible to leave the preparation programme and take a German language exam elsewhere. Disregard may lead to severe consequences such as the loss of your residence permit.

Persons who have quit the VWU and have passed a different German language exam in the meanwhile can still take the supplementary examination in German (EPD).

[Detailed information on the regulation](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/detailed-information/)

While you are admitted to the non-degree programme "attending the VWU" ("Besuch des Vorstudienlehrgangs"), you are only allowed to attend courses in the VWU and complete the prescribed supplementary examinations there. Taking exams from the degree programme is not allowed.

title: Attending courses out of interest
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

title: Attending courses out of interest
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

title: Attending courses out of interest
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
heading_paths: Your steps in the admission procedure
Your steps in the admission procedure
Your steps in the admission procedure
Step 3: Creating a semester schedule and ordering the u:card
text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

/

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Making up for qualitative admission requirements
text:
Qualitative admission requirements (QZB) are binding and determine which knowledge and competencies are required for admission to a specific master programme. You have received a rejection letter, because you do not fulfil the qualitative admission requirements? You can make up for the qualitative admission requirements in a non-degree programme. Therefore, you will not be admitted to a specific degree programme, but to "attending individual courses".

You can complete courses for example from extension curricula (EC) and thereby you are able to fulfil the qualitative admission requirements. Inform yourself which courses the EC consists of and register for these courses. A registration for the EC as a non-degree student is neither required nor possible technically.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Making up for qualitative admission requirements
text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Making up for qualitative admission requirements
heading_paths: Creating a semester schedule and ordering the u:card
text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Making up for qualitative admission requirements
text:
/

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. You can only complete the examinations from an EC as lecture examination, not as module examinations. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Making up for subject-specific supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme
text:
The admission to master programmes is possible with supplementary examinations to an extent of up to 30 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. If this extent is exceeded, your application for admission will be rejected.

Your application for admission was rejected for this reason? You can make up for lacking supplementary examinations in a non-degree programme, until 30 ECTS or less remain. For this purpose, you will be admitted to "attending individual courses", not to a specific degree programme.

For example: You lack 39 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. Therefore, your application is rejected. Subsequently, you complete supplementary examinations to an extent of 9 ECTS or more as a non-degree student.  Afterwards, you can apply for admission to the degree programme again within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

If the supplementary examination in German was prescribed in your admission letter, please read the information regarding the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) carefully.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

title: Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without secondary-school leaving certificate
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The Studienberechtigungsprüfung allows you to study without a secondary-school leaving certificate. One of the prerequisites to be eligible for the Studienberechtigungsprüfung at the University of Vienna is a German language level of at least C1. Therefore, all further information regarding this topic is only available in German.

text:
On this page you can find out which steps you have to complete to be admitted to the degree programme if you have received a letter of award ("Zuerkennungsschreiben").

If you are interested in a CEEPUS mobility grant, please contact the [Central CEEPUS Office](http://www.ceepus.info/content/contact).

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- the CEEPUS Letter of Award and Scholarship Details ("Zuerkennungsschreiben")

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 31 March 2027

Admission > CEEPUS students

On this page you can find out which steps you have to complete to be admitted to the degree programme if you have received a letter of award ("Zuerkennungsschreiben").

If you are interested in a CEEPUS mobility grant, please contact the [Central CEEPUS Office](http://www.ceepus.info/content/contact).

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- the CEEPUS Letter of Award and Scholarship Details ("Zuerkennungsschreiben")

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 31 March 2027

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.

title: CEEPUS students
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Your steps in the admission procedure
Your steps in the admission procedure
Step 3: Creating a semester schedule and ordering the u:card
text:
1. Contact the admission office
   Let us know, which programme you were nominated for. Add your Letter of Award and Scholarship Details merged into one PDF file via
   - the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/1945) for bachelor's/diploma programmes or
   - the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1995) for master's programmes.
2. You will be preliminarily admitted to the degree programme
   You will receive an e-mail with your student ID number and information on further steps.
3. Pay the Students' Union fee after the appointment
   Once the Students' Union fee is received by the University of Vienna, the admission procedure is complete. You can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

- [Central CEEPUS Office](http://www.ceepus.info/content/contact)
- [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

title: Additional exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the

Admission > Additional exams > Latin

For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. title: Additional exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).
embed_text:
Admission > Additional exams > Greek

For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).

title: Additional exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
For the Teacher Education subject Descriptive Geometry, an additional qualification in Descriptive Geometry has to be attained prior to admission.

If you attended Descriptive Geometry at a secondary school for at least four hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

If you did not attend enough classes of Descriptive Geometry at school, you have to prove the additional exam by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.

To gain admission to the bachelor programme Sport Science and the subject of instruction Sports and Physical Education you need to pass the sporting aptitude admission test prior to admission. For further information, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/).

text: / - I've already passed the additional exam in Latin/Greek, but it still shows up in my student record sheet. What am I supposed to do? The completion of the additional exam in Latin or Greek should be registered automatically. If your school reports prove that you have sufficient knowledge, this is done directly during the first-time admission procedure. If you did not present the appropriate school reports at the Admission Office, you can send them via [contact form](/en/contact-form/). Please include your u:account e-mail address in your message. In case you take the additional exam at the University of Vienna, the [StudienServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at) of the corresponding course will register your grade. In this case, you can pass the additional exam in Latin/Greek at the University of Vienna if you have already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of

title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
heading_paths: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
text:
- ### Step 1

  **From now on**

  Find out more:

  - [What is an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c4427)
  - [Does my preferred programme have an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c7588)
  - [How do I find my dream degree programme?](#c4476)
  - [What will change in 2026/27?](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)
- ### Step 2

  **Deadline: 2 March to 4 May 2026**

  The application period has ended. There is no extension period. You can only take part in this year’s entrance exam/aptitude test procedure if you applied within the application period.

  Please note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedure only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 3

  **Written tests: From 6 July 2026 onwards**

  The written tests take place on site in Vienna. We will inform you until the end of this year about the exact test dates. There are no alternative dates. Therefore, make sure to keep the test day free!

  Note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedures of the University of Vienna only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 4

  **Results: Around 2 weeks after the test**

  We will send your test result via e-mail. If you were successful, you will receive info about your further steps for your admission and starting your studies.

  The winter semester always starts on 1 October.

Which programmes have an entrance exam procedure?
text:
The University of Vienna conducts an entrance exam or aptitude test procedure for certain bachelor's and diploma programmes. This means: a written test will take place in July 2026.

On this page, you can find out everything you need to know about the programme content, deadlines, required documents and the test procedure. We provide a step by step guide on how to prepare and how to successfully apply.

When clicking the play button personal data may be transferred to YouTube.

You can activate English subtitles in the video settings.

title: Deadlines and dates
heading_paths: Which period is important for me?
text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

title: Deadlines and dates
heading_paths: Which period is important for me?
Deadlines for bachelor's/diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Test details: Test literature and sample exercises

What do I need to study for the test? How is the test structured? On this page you'll find information about the test literature and sample exercises. This will help you get ready for the written test!

Please note:

- The tests for some degree programmes take place at the same time.
- The test literature is only valid for the academic year 2026/27.

[Here you'll find an overview of all test dates.](/en/deadlines-and-dates)

#### Test literature

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the bachelor's programmes in Business Administration, International Business Administration and Economics](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_wiwi) (only available in German)

#### Non-compulsory preparation material

- Mathematics: <https://www.matura.gv.at/srdp/mathematik>
- Applied Mathematics (especially part A): <https://www.matura.gv.at/srdp/angewandte-mathematik>
- As further literature to prepare for the exam, the following book can be used (recommended complimentary literature): Sydsaeter, Knut & Hammond, Peter (2018): Mathematik für Wirtschaftswissenschaftler – Basiswissen mit Praxisbezug. (aktuelle Auflage; derzeit 5. Auflage), Hallbergmoos: Pearson Studium. ISBN 978-3-86894-306-1, Kapitel 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10.

#### Note on applying

You're interested in Business Administration and/or International Business Administration as well as in Economics? Apply for each programme separately. Changing from Business Administration or International Business Administration to Economics or vice versa is not possible.

Open all

#### Test literature

The written aptitude test examines the contents of the mathematics curricula of the Austrian general higher education schools (grades 9 to 12) as well as elementary combinatorics (addition and multiplication rules, permutations) and the comprehension of simple pseudocode.

- [Contents of the Austrian secondary-school leaving examination in Mathematics](https://www.matura.gv.at/srdp/mathematik) (only available in German)
- [Syllabus for the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Mathematical Foundations of Data Science 2026](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testliteratur/Syllabus_Aptitude_test_2026-1.pdf)

title: Test details: Test literature and sample exercises
text:
Collapse all

Open all

#### Test structure

The written test examines competencies in the following areas:

- **Part A: Ability to acquire subject-related knowledge from literature for first-year students** (50 % weighting)
- **Part B: Ability to think methodically, formally and analytically** (25 % weighting)
- **Part C: Ability to understand simple texts on the subject matter in English and German** (25 % weighting)

The results are ranked according to the sum of the weighted scores per part. The highest score corresponds to ranking position number 1, the lowest to ranking position number n (n = number of test participants). Participants ranking between position 1 and 485 receive a study place. To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil all requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

#### Test literature

[Psycho … logisch! Einführung in die Grundlagen der Psychologie. Lernskript für die Aufnahmeprüfung Bachelor Psychologie 2026 (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testliteratur/avpsy26_Lernskript_hoheBildaufloesung.pdf) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises

[Sample exercises for the bachelor's programme in Psychology (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testliteratur/avpsy_ba26_Beispielitems.pdf) (only available in German)

[Aufnahmeverfahren für das Diplomstudium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität Wien – Auszug basierend auf „Grundbegriffe der Rechtswissenschaften“ von Franz-Stefan Meissel; Helmut Ofner; Bettina Perthold-Stoitzner; Michaela Windisch-Graetz](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2046181) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the diploma programme in Law](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_rewi) (only available in German)

Collapse all

Open all

#### Ranking

The ranking is determined as follows:

- 400 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with German as their A-language
- 262 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with a different language as their A-language

After these, participants with descending overall scores follow alternately (according to the zipper merge method) on the ranking list:

- one test participant who registered with a language other than German as A-language,
- and one test participant who registered with German as A-language.

To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil the requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

#### Test literature

[Transkulturelle Kommunikation: Zur Einführung und Vorbereitung auf das Studium](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:1165618) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the bachelor's programme in Transcultural Communication](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_trans) (only available in German)

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure

Find out everything you need to know about possible exemptions from the entrance exam and aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna on this site.

An exemption from the entrance exam procedure means:

- You don't have to take part in the entrance exam procedure.
- You can apply every semester.
- You don't have to pay an administrative fee.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

- ### Which exemptions exist?

  - [Previous programme at the University of Vienna](#c4286)
  - [Changing between or studying an additional degree programme within programme groups](#c4292)
  - [60-ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law](#c4287)
  - [Exemptions for students of a Teacher Education programme](#c4436)
  - [Exchange students](#c4332)

  There are no further exemptions.
- ### Deadlines

  Apply for the exemption within the following deadlines, irrespective of your citizenship.

  - **Winter semester 2026/27:**
    - 13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027:**
    - 11 January to 31 March 2027

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_paths: Previous degree programme at the University of Vienna
Previous degree programme at the University of Vienna
Changing your degree programme or studying an additional programme within certain groups
text:
You have started and interrupted a bachelor's or diploma programme with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna? If so, you are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the same programme. This is also the case if there was no entrance exam procedure at the time.

**Follow these steps:**

- Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) within the [deadline](#c4319).
- Select the topic “Bachelor/diploma programmes”.
- State which programme you would like to resume.
- Attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card.

i

There are **no exemptions from the entrance exam procedure** in the bachelor's programme in Psychology. Apply for admission within the [application period for degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/deadlines-and-dates) and participate in the written test. Resuming your studies is only possible if you are successful in the entrance exam.

An exemption is in place for certain groups of degree programmes: If you are already admitted to a programme at the University of Vienna and want to study an additional programme of or change your programme within that group you do not have to participate in the entrance exam procedure again.

This rule only applies the the University of Vienna's programmes listed below, and not to students of other universities.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
heading_paths: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
text:
You are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law if you

- were admitted to a jurisprudential degree programme at a recognised educational institution and
- if you can prove having achieved at least 60 ECTS.

The 60 ECTS have to be recognisable for compulsory or elective subjects or compulsory modules of the diploma programme in Law at the University of Vienna. The StudiesServiceCenter Law has created an [overview about recognitions](https://ssc-rechtswissenschaften.univie.ac.at/diplomstudium-neu-2025/anerkennung/) (only available in German). Please check beforehand if your achievements can be recognised.

- were admitted to a jurisprudential degree programme at a recognised educational institution and
- if you can prove having achieved at least 60 ECTS.

The 60 ECTS have to be recognisable for compulsory or elective subjects or compulsory modules of the diploma programme in Law at the University of Vienna. The StudiesServiceCenter Law has created an [overview about recognitions](https://ssc-rechtswissenschaften.univie.ac.at/diplomstudium-neu-2025/anerkennung/) (only available in German). Please check beforehand if your achievements can be recognised.

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
heading_paths: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
text:
- [#### Step 1: Send an e-mail to the SSC Law

  Send your proof of the 60 ECTS and a scan of your personal ID via e-mail to the StudiesServiceCenter Law no later than 5 February 2026. By this time you must have already completed the 60 ECTS. The StudiesServiceCenter will evaluate your documents. You will receive a confirmation via e-mail.

  If less than 60 ECTS credits can be recognised, no exemption can be made. You will receive the relevant information via e-mail. In this case, you must successfully complete the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law.

  Message the SSC](#)
- [#### Step 2: create a u:account

  Create your u:account in u:space no later than the deadline. Make sure you remember the login data, because you'll also need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space and complete the registration.

  Go to u:space

  ©
  Ian Ehm](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en)
- [#### Step 3: Submit the application for admission

  Send the following documents via the Servicedesk no later than th deadline:

  - the confirmation of the StudiesServiceCenter Law
  - your secondary-school leaving certificate (all pages!)
    - including a translation (for certificates that were not issued in German or English)
    - including a legalisation (if required for your country)
  - proof of German language proficiency at level C1

  Afterwards you will receive further information via e-mail.

  Message us via the Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)

heading_paths: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna
text:
Collapse all

Open all

You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure if you

- have interrupted the programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna and want to [resume your studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)
- [change your teaching subjects](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/#c806587)
- want to be admitted to a [third teaching subject as a complimentary degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/) (only available in German)
- want to voluntarily transfer to the current curriculum or if are automatically transferred to the current curriculum (e.g. if the curriculum expires).

For resuming your studies or changing your teaching subject message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) during the [application period](#c4319). Send a scan of your passport or personal ID and state the teaching subjects you want to study.

Contact the [StudiesServiceCenter Teacher Education](https://ssc-lehrerinnenbildung.univie.ac.at/) if you have questions regarding the transfer to a new curriculum.

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
heading_paths: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you have studied or are currently studying one of the following programmes at a university or college of teacher education in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland.

Sie sind vom Eignungsverfahren für das Bachelorstudium Lehramt an der Universität Wien ausgenommen, wenn Sie eines der folgenden Studien an einer Universität oder Pädagogischen Hochschule eines EU/EWR-Landes oder der Schweiz studieren oder abgeschlossen haben.

- Bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary schools (general education)
- Programme in Business Education
- Programme in Religious Education

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. your current confirmation of studies or your degree certificate
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message. Note: If you want to study the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education please also send your confirmation of studies to [StudiesServiceCenter Sport Science](#).

text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you fulfil the following:

- You work as teacher at a primary or secondary school with publics rights within the EU/EEA or Switzerland.

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate/degree certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. a confirmation about your work issued by the headmaster
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message.

title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure

Completed aptitude test procedure at another Austrian university
heading_paths: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Completed aptitude test procedure at another Austrian university
text:
Once you have successfully completed the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary education (general education) at an Austrian university or college for teacher education you are exempt from the aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna. It does not matter when you completed the aptitude test. The aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education is valid indefinitely.

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935) and state which subjects you want to be admitted to. Send the following proofs (merged into one PDF file) attached to your message:
   - scan of your valid passport or personal ID card (both pages)
   - all pages of your secondary-school leaving
   - confirmation of passing the aptitude test procedure at another university/college for teacher education

Creative teaching subject at a university of arts
heading_paths: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Creative teaching subject at a university of arts
text: You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you study at least one teaching subject at one of the following universities of arts - [University of Music and Performing Arts Vienna](https://www.mdw.ac.at/studiencenter/zulassung-zum-universitaetsuebergreifenden-lehramts-studium/) (only available in German) - [Universität of Applied Arts Vienna](https://www.dieangewandte.at/lehramt_kombination) (only available in German) - [Akademie der bildenden Künste Wien](https://www.akbild.ac.at/de/studium/zulassungspruefungen/bachelorstudium-kuenstlerisches-lehramt) (only available in German) You first participate in the aptitude test procedure of the university of arts. title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
text:
Exchange students attending the University of Vienna as part of the following mobility programmes are exempt from entrance exams/aptitude test procedure:

- Erasmus+
- Non-EU Student Exchange Programme (formerly Joint Study)

Your point of contact at the University of Vienna is the [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/).

Students coming to the University of Vienna via CEEPUS programmes: Please contact the [CEEPUS Office](https://oead.at/en/cooperations/international-he-cooperations/ceepus) directly. Here you'll also find further Information about the [admission](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/ceepus-students/#c1006426) at the University of Vienna.

Any questions about the admission procedure?

Message us via the contact form. You can reach us by phone on Mondays and Thursdays from 12:30 to 13:30.

[+43-1-4277-12100](tel:+43-1-4277-12100)
[To the contact form](/en/contact)

Do you have questions about registering for courses and examinations? Or about grades and the recognition of exams? The StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) are the right place to go.

[To the SSCs](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/studies/students/organisation-of-studies-and-teaching)

Would you like to know more about support services such as STEOP mentoring or writing workshops? The team at the CTL looks forward to answering your questions.

[Email](#)
[To the CTL website](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en)

title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

©
[Translate to English:] derknopfdruecker.com

title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level A2
heading_paths: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level A2
text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - School report of a secondary-school including the subject German
  - Passed courses of 8 ECTS credits or 200 hours instructed in German within a degree programme at a recognised post-secondary educational institution

  The proofs mentioned here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – A2 (ÖSD Zertifikat A2, ZA2) or higher
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat A2 or higher
  - telc Deutsch – A2 or higher
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz Stufe I – DSD I or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (A2) or higher
  - Language Center of an Austrian University – successfully passed exam of level A2 or higher
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF) with at least level TDN 3 in all parts or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 2 years.

title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level C1
heading_paths: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level C1
text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - Secondary-school leaving certificate from a German-speaking school (with German as the language of instruction and examination\*) or an Austrian or German school abroad
  - Completion of a degree programme of at least three years with German as the language of instruction at a recognised post-secondary educational institution
  - Passing of the supplementary exam in German (Ergänzungsprüfung Deutsch) at a University Preparation Programme of an Austrian university

  \*Secondary-school leaving certificates from Austria, Germany, South Tyrol, Luxembourg and Liechtenstein are C1 certificates. You do not need to upload a proof of German with your application.

  The proofs listed here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – ÖSD Zertifikat C1 (formerly called: C1 Oberstufe Deutsch)
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat C1
  - telc Deutsch “C1 Hochschule”
  - Deutsche Sprachprüfung für den Hochschulzugang DSH2
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz DSD II
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF), at least level 4 in all parts
  - Language Center of the University of Vienna – course and successfully passed exam of level C1/2 or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (C1) or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 3 years.

heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
heading_paths: We accept the following proofs of German
text: clarify with the Austrian Embassy, the Austrian Consulate or Magistratsabteilung 35 (Municipal Department 35) in advance what impact this will have on your residence permit. Once you have been admitted to the non-degree programme “University Programme Preparation Programme”, you must also take the supplementary German examination there. Other certificates or proofs will then no longer be accepted. #### I a citizen of a German-speaking country, or German is my first language. Do I need to provide a proof of my German language proficiency? Your citizenship is no proof of your German language proficiency. **Is German your first language?** Do you consider your German language proficiency to be of at least at level C1 in terms of written and spoken language as well as listening and reading comprehension? If so, please upload a sworn statement (= a statement of facts with your signature) under the items “Proof of German language proficiency A2” and “Proof of German language proficiency C1”. In this statement, confirm that German is your first language. Please note: Once you have been admitted to the degree programme, you will no longer be able to attend a German course at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities. The Admission Office has the right to request proof of German language proficiency in case of doubt.

title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: German course in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
heading_paths: German course in the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
text:
- [### What is the VWU?

  The VWU is a preparation programme for university studies. When you apply for a degree programme and we have reviewed your documents, you may be required to take supplementary examinations (e.g. in German, if you do not yet have level C1). You can take these supplementary examinations in the VWU.

  You cannot apply directly for the VWU. Submit your application for admission to a degree programme.

  Info about the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna)
- [### Important information about the VWU:

  Once you have been admitted to the “University Programme Preparation Programme”, only the supplementary German examination (EPD) of the VWU will be accepted as proof of German language proficiency without exception. In this case, it is not possible to discontinue the VWU and take a German examination at another institution. Failure to comply can have serious consequences for your residence permit.

  Individuals who have discontinued the VWU and have taken another language examination in the meantime can retake the supplementary examination in German.

  Detailed information about this rule](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/detailed-information/)

title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_paths: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
Step 1: Find out about the steps in the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test
text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure

It is important to us that all prospective students have equal access to our range of degree programmes. Does a disability or impairment limit your ability to take part in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? We will provide you with individual support and adapt the test to your needs.

On this page, we will show you what you need to bear in mind, how to report your needs and what deadlines apply. Please let us know your needs directly when you submit your application so that we can work together to create the best possible conditions for you.

Previous

©

Next

- ### Deadline for registering special testing accomodations

  **2 March to 4 May 2026**

  This is also the [application period](/en/deadlines-and-dates) for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure.
- ### Required documents

  - [Diagnosis-free form (German version, PDF)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Barrierefreies_AV/Formular_Bestaetigung_studienrelevante_Beeintraechtigungen_24-03-26.pdf) or
  - [Diagnosis-free form (English version, PDF)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Barrierefreies_AV/Formular_Besta__tigung_studienrelevante_BeeintraechtigungenEN.pdf) or
  - Other specialist medical, psychotherapeutic, or clinical psychological confirmation

Collapse all

Open all

Select your desired degree programme from the [list of degree programmes with entrance exam procedure/aptitude test](/en/degree-programmes). There you will find information about the required documents, step-by-step instructions for your application for admission to a degree programme, and everything else you need to know about the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: What counts as an impairment? How are testing procedures adapted?
heading_paths: What counts as an impairment? How are testing procedures adapted?
text:
- ### Examples for impairments

  This list is only exemplary and not to be taken as a full account of all possible impairments.

  - Vision impairment
  - Hearing impairment (to such a degree, that announcements at the testing venue might not be heard clearly)
  - Impaired gross and/or fine motor skills (e.g. paraplegia, spasms and/or myasthenia)
  - Cognitive or mental impairment (e.g. attentiveness and/or anxiety disorders)
  - Learning disorders (e.g. dyslexia)
  - Illnesses that require special equipment (e.g. diabetes)
- ### Examples for testing accommodations

  - Smaller rooms with and quieter testing environment
  - Extension of the test time (only possible in the smaller testing rooms)
  - Additional items (e.g. medication, medical devices, vision aids, writing aids)
  - Adjustments of the test documents (e.g. larger type size, special fonts, digital version)
  - Special testing equipment (e.g. laptops and screens, desks suitable for wheelchairs)
  - Specific seating (e.g. at the edge, at the front or back, close to the bathrooms)
  - Translation in Austrian Sign Language (ÖGS)

title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: Example: Adapted entrance exam procedure in a smaller testing venue
heading_paths: Example: Adapted entrance exam procedure in a smaller testing venue
text:
- ### Step 1

  **Before the written test**

  No later than 14 days before the test date, we will send you the admission confirmation and the access plan for the test rooms. In this e-mail, we will also inform you which items are permitted during the test.
- ### Step 2

  **Your admission on the test day**

  You will find your time slot and access point on your admission confirmation. We will meet you there, verify your identity, and then accompany you to your test room.
- ### Step 3

  **The written test**

  In the test room, you will have a few minutes to settle in. The test administrators will then read out the instructions, including all the details regarding the examination process

  At least one supervisor will be in the room with you throughout the duration of the exam.
- ### Step 4

  **After the written test**

  Once the test time has expired, we collect the documents and check that they are complete. We will escort you back to the exit.

  After the test has been evaluated, you will receive an e-mail about the next steps in the admission procedure.

title: The day of the written test
text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# The day of the written test

The big day for your degree programme with entrance exam procedure/aptitude test is just around the corner. But what actually happens on a test day? On this page, you will find information about the procedure, permitted and prohibited items, and all the important rules for the day.

The University of Vienna holds the written admission and aptitude tests at the [VIECON – Vienna Congress & Convention Center](https://vieconcenter.at/en/). Please visit the [Wiener Linien website](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) for information on how to get there and tickets for public transport.

You can find the admission time and the expected end of the test on your permission to attend (“Einlassbestätigung”). However, we recommend that you keep the entire day free. Please be punctual and, if necessary, arrive the day before. If you arrive too late, you will be excluded from the entrance exam/aptitude test! There are no alternative dates.

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

title: The day of the written test
text:
You are allowed to bring a bag to the written test. This bag must not be larger than hand luggage. During the test, all prohibited items must remain in your bag without exception. Only explicitly permitted items are allowed to be on your desk.

If you use a prohibited item during the test, this will count as an attempt to cheat. In this case you will be excluded from the test.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

- ### Required and permitted items

  The following items are **required** at your seat:

  - printout of the permission to attend (“Einlassbestätigung”) in the format A4 (available via u:space > Personal matters > My documents)
  - valid passport or personal ID card

  The following items are **permitted** at your seat:

  - drinks and snacks
  - recommended: sweater/jacket/vest (the test venue is air-conditioned)
- ### Prohibited items

  - your own paper, sticky notes
  - white out pens, erasers, rulers
  - pens of any kind (including highlighters)
  - electronic objects (calculator, mobile phone, tablet, smartwatch, headphones, etc.)
  - watches of any kind (there are watches in the test hall)
  - study materials, dictionaries, encyclopaedias, formula collections, periodic tables, etc.
  - hearing protections
  - (E-)cigarettes, vapes, matches, lighters, purse, keys

title: The day of the written test
text:
Please note: Only those with a permission to attend (printed in A4 format!) are allowed to enter the VIECON. In this permission and in the access plan you will find information about your access to the test hall. The entry is organised in stages via several entrances.

Only use the entrance intended for you! We will check your identity. Therefore, please have your passport or personal ID card and your printed permission to attend on hand.

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

- ### Entry to the test room

  The personalized QR code on your permission to attend will be scanned at the entrance to the test hall. You are not allowed to leave the test hall after that. Toilets are located in the test hall. Please note: Smoking is not permitted.
- ### Personalized seat

  Your seat is personalized and can only be accessed via the entrance assigned to you. Please proceed directly to your allocated sector. Supervisory staff will help you find your sector and seat. You may leave your own seat to go to the toilet, but you may not go to any other seats.

Before the test, you will receive detailed instructions on how to proceed (for example, how to mark your answers or how to correct incorrectly marked answers). The exact end time of the test will also be announced.

Take a look at the sample exercises and possible questions for the tests on our test details website.

For organizational reasons, you may not leave the test hall early. Please take this into account when planning your schedule and travel arrangements. Upon request, you can obtain time confirmations (for military service, civil service, etc.) after the written test.

We will inform you of your test result and your next steps in the admission procedure via e-mail.

©
Universität Wien
