title: Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: What do students attending the VWU have to keep in mind?
Info for students in the VWU
text:
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

[## How much do I have to pay?](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/)

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
- Bachelor programme:
  Stipulated degree programme duration 6 semesters + 2 tolerance semesters. You pay the tuition fee from the 9th semester onwards.
- Bachelor programme in Teacher Education:
  Stipulated degree programme duration 8 semesters + 2 tolerance semesters. You pay the tuition fee from the 11th semester onwards.
- Diploma programme:
  During the stipulated duration of each stage of your degree programme + 2 tolerance semesters you only pay the Students’ Union fee. Afterwards, also the tuition fee is prescribed. When you hand in a completed stage at your [StudiesServiceCenter](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) you receive a diploma certificate. The calculation of semesters starts at one once again. This means: you are exempt from the tuition fee again. Stipulated degree programme duration (and for each stage 2 tolerance semesters).
  - Law (Degree Programme Code 101): 8 semesters (2 +2/3+2/3+2 semesters)
  - Law (Degree Programme Code 100, from winter semester 2025 onwards): 8 semesters (2 +2/4+2/2+2 semesters)
  - Catholic Theology: 10 semesters (6+2/4+2 semesters)
- Master programme:
  Stipulated degree programme duration 4 semesters + 2 tolerance semesters. You pay the tuition fee from the 7th semester onwards.
- Doctoral/PhD programme:
  Stipulated degree programme duration 6 semesters + 2 tolerance semesters. You pay the tuition fee from the 9th semester onwards.

  - Law (Degree Programme Code 100, from winter semester 2025 onwards): 8 semesters (2 +2/4+2/2+2 semesters)
  - Catholic Theology: 10 semesters (6+2/4+2 semesters)

title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee

text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and the Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Citizens of Non-EU/EEA countries pay the tuition fee from the first semester. The duration of studies is not relevant for the amount of the tuition fee. However, there are exemptions. title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

text: / - Attending the VWU You have to complete supplementary examinations in the VWU? As long as you attend courses at the VWU you can be [admitted to a non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/). During your time at the VWU you only pay the Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. - Further reasons for non-degree programmes - "Nachmatura" (receiving the Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate late) You have not passed your final exam of the Austrian Matura yet, but want to register for courses? In this case the [admission to a non-degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/) (only available in German) is possible. In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee. - Attending courses out of interest You want to attend individual courses out of interest? In this case the [admission to a non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/attending-courses-out-of-interest/) is possible. In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee. - Making up for subject-specific supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme You have to make up for courses from the bachelor programme to be admitted to a master programme? In this case the [admission to a non-degree programme is possible](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-subject-specific-supplementary-examinations-from-the-bachelor-programme/). text: / - I am EU/EEA citizen. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? Check [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Study overview to see which degree programmes you are admitted to. As soon as you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna all degree programmes are continued. It does not matter if you actively study them or not. The tuition fee is prescribed if you exceed the stipulated degree programme duration plus tolerance semesters. If a degree programme that you (currently) do not want to continue is in your study overview, [you can close it](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/). The amount of the tuition fee will then be calculated anew. - I have equal status. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? If you have equal status with EU/EEA citizens you are exempt from the tuition fee as long as - your residence permit is valid and - you are within the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. [Detailed information about equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) - Will my Erasmus+ semester be counted for the tuition-free period for EU/EEA citizens? Semesters during which you are abroad with a mobility programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the

title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You are admitted to a degree programme and your citizenship is from a non-EU/EEA country? Under certain conditions, you can be treated as equal to EU/EEA citizens regarding the tuition fee regulations. This means:  the same [tuition fee regulations as for EU/EEA citizens](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/tuition-fee/amount-eueeach-citizens/#c151947) apply. Equal status does not take effect automatically. You have to submit an application.

On this page you will find everything you need to know about equal status.

- Submit your application for equal status as soon as possible.
- If you are granted equal status, the same [tuition fee regulations as for EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/#c1123191) apply to you.
- If your residence permit has a limited period of validity, this also applies to equal status. Apply for equal status as soon as you have received the extension of your residence permit.
- We only accept the residence permits/reasons for equal status listed below.

- For summer semester:
  until 31 March
- For winter semester:
  until 31 October

text: You can apply for equal status if you are admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and: - have a residence permit accepted in Austria other than a "Aufenthaltsbewilligung - Student" or - you are a member of a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/) (PersGV) or - are eligible for equal status according to international law (§ 91 para. 1 Universities Act 2002) If you have a "[Aufenthaltsbewilligung - Student](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/themen/menschen_aus_anderen_staaten/aufenthalt/3/2/1/1/Seite.120121.html)", equal status is not attainable. Unless you also fulfil one of the other requirements (e.g. residence title "Daueraufenthalt - EU"). / - Accepted residence permits If you have one of these residence permits, you can apply for equal status. ### Frequent residence permits - [Red-White-Red – Card](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/themen/menschen_aus_anderen_staaten/aufenthalt/3/2/2.html)Proof: Scan of the card (both sides) as JPG or PDF - [Red-White-Red – Card plus](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/themen/menschen_aus_anderen_staaten/aufenthalt/3/2/Seite.120307.html)Proof: Scan of the card (both sides) as JPG or PDF - [Aufenthaltstitel Daueraufenthalt – EU (issued in Austria)](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/themen/menschen_aus_anderen_staaten/aufenthalt/3/2/Seite.120402.html)Proof: Scan of the card (both sides) as JPG or PDF - Daueraufenthalt - EU (issued in a member state of the EU) Proof: Scan of the card (both sides) and valid Austrian residence permit (e.g. residence permit - student), merged into one PDF file ### --- ### Residence permit for family reunification - [Aufenthaltstitel Familienangehöriger (residence permit for close family members of Austrian citizens)](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/themen/menschen_aus_anderen_staaten/aufenthalt/3/2/Seite.120401.html) - Proof: Scan of the card (both

status regarding the right to work according to international law (§ 91 para. 1 UG 2002) Turkish nationals with an exemption certificate from the AMS can be granted equal status. To do so, open the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/702) and select reason for equal status "I have equal status regarding the right to work in Austria according to international law (§ 91 para. 1 UG 2002).". title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Your steps towards application for equal status
heading_paths: Your steps towards application for equal status
text:
1. Prepare your documents
   Scan the required documents and save them in high quality in JPG or PDF format.
2. Submit your application
   [Complete the online form in the Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/702) and attach the required supporting documents (at the top of the online form).
3. Wait for a reply
   Each application is reviewed individually. Processing takes about two weeks. You will then receive an e-mail from the Admission Office to your u:account e-mail address.
4. Pay the tuition/students' union fee
   If your application is approved, you will be subject to the same rules as students who are citizens of an EU/EEA country. This means that how much you will pay as your tuition fee, depends on your degree programme duration and tolerance semersters. All students must pay the Students' Union fee. Make sure to pay the fee within the payment deadline. Please note: If you do not pay on time, your studies will be automatically closed.

title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - I have equal status. Why am I suddenly being charged with the tuition fee? - Option 1: Your residence permit had a limited period of validity and has expired. You must submit a new application for equal status as soon as you have received the extension of your residence permit. - Option 2: You have exceeded the planned duration of study and the two tolerance semesters. You must therefore now pay the same tuition fee as students who are nationals of an EU/EEA country. If you fulfill a waiver reason, you can apply for a waiver of the tuition fee. - I have the "Aufenthaltsbewilligung - Student". Can I be granted equal status? The "Aufenthaltsbewilligung - Student" on its own is not accepted proof of eligibility for the application for equal status. With the "Aufenthaltsbewilligung - Student" you can only be granted equal status if you: - additionally have one of the other accepted residence permits or - are eligible for equal status according to international law (§ 91 para. 1 UG 2002) - My application for equal status has been approved. When do I have to start paying? If your application for equal status has been approved, the same rules apply to you as for students who are nationals of an EU/EEA country. This means that the duration of your studies affects the amount of tuition fee.

title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: paying? If your application for equal status has been approved, the same rules apply to you as for students who are nationals of an EU/EEA country. This means that the duration of your studies affects the amount of tuition fee. Your first semester is the semester in which you were first admitted to a degree program. It doesn't matter when you were granted equal status. The semester count does not restart. - I have already paid the tuition fee and have now been granted equal status. Will I get my money back? Yes, a reimbursement of the difference in fees is possible. To claim it, you must submit an [application for reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/#c1105696). - If you are within the planned duration of studies and tolerance semesters, everything except the Students' Union fee can be refunded. - If you have exceeded the planned duration of studies and tolerance semester, you will only be refunded the difference. When applying for a reimbursement at the Service Desk, select the reimbursement reason "I have paid too much, so I am applying for a reimbursement of the difference". - Why do I have to submit the application again and again? If your residence permit has a limited period of validity, so does your equal status. You must therefore submit a new application when you extend your residence permit.

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
heading_paths: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
text: If you study at multiple universities in Austria, you can decide where you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee yourself. You have already paid at another university? Then you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself each semester. Otherwise your studies will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. / - How do I re-enrol in my studies if I pay at another university? 1. Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. Look for the degree programme you want to continue studying at Studies > Study overview. Confirm that you want to continue studying this degree programme. To do so choose the button "continue studying". If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree
embed_text:
Tuition fee > Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) > I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university

Then you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself each semester. Otherwise your studies will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. / - How do I re-enrol in my studies if I pay at another university? 1. Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. Look for the degree programme you want to continue studying at Studies > Study overview. Confirm that you want to continue studying this degree programme. To do so choose the button "continue studying". If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
heading_paths: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
text: If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree programme. Afterwards you can download your student documents in Personal matters > My documents. Private universities do not forward the payment information to the University of Vienna. In this case you have to also pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna. Afterwards you can contact the [Austrian Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). There you can apply for a refund of the fee you have paid twice. Austrian universities of applied sciences only forward the information about the payment of the Students' Union fee. The tuition fee is not forwarded to the University of Vienna.

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)

heading_path: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
heading_paths: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
text:
As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

title: Remission and reimbursement
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
If you study at the University of Vienna you have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester in due time. If certain grounds are at hand, you can submit an application for remission. If you have already paid, you can apply for a reimbursement of the tuition fee provided you fulfil the applicable reasons.

If one of the reasons mentioned below applies to you, you submit your application for remission of the tuition fee within the application period.

We recommend: Submit your application for remission no later than one month before the deadline. The processing of your application may take several weeks. We will inform you about the outcome of your application as well as any further steps that may be required via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

title: Remission and reimbursement
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
- We only accept the reasons for remission and proofs listed below.
- The reasons for remission apply only to degree programme students, with few exceptions. If they also apply for non-degree students it is explicitly stated.
- You also have to pay the Students' Union fee in due time even if the remission is granted.
- You can only apply for remission in the semester in which the reason for remission occurs. A remission in retrospect, e.g. for an illness in the past, is not possible.
- You have to add the required proofs for the remission to each application.

title: Remission and reimbursement
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: This is how you apply for remission and/or reimbursement
heading_paths: This is how you apply for remission and/or reimbursement
This is how you apply for remission and/or reimbursement
Submit your application in due time!
text:
1. Scan the required proofs for remission and/or reimbursement. Save them in a good quality that is easy to read. Merge all documents into one PDF file using an online tool of your choice. The proofs have to be issued in German or English or translated into German/English by an official court appointed translation agency.
2. Complete the [online form in the Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/700) and add the required proofs as attachment. If you want to apply for remission as well as reimbursement choose the option "I want to submit an application for remission AND reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee" in the online form.
3. If you apply for reimbursement: Add or update your bank data in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > Personal data. The transfer is only possible with correct and current bank data.

If you miss the application period, your application will be rejected without any exceptions. A reimbursement is no longer possible in this case.

2. Complete the [online form in the Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/700) and add the required proofs as attachment. title: Remission and reimbursement
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- Remission due to illness: What qualifies als illness?

  Reasons for illness may be, among other things, major operations, chronic illness or also mental impairments. You need a [confirmation by a medical specialist](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_Fachaerztliche_Bestaetigung_Erlass_EN.pdf), that you are thereby impaired from your studies for at least two months during the semester. The diagnosis does not need to be stated.
- What shall I do if I do not have all proofs for applying for remission until the application deadline?

  In this case you must pay the tuition fee. Afterwards, you will be able to apply for remission and reimbursement at the same time. These two applications can be submitted for the winter semester until the following 28/29 February and for the summer semester until 30 September.
- My application for remission of the tuition fee was granted, but I have already paid the tuition fee. What can I do?

  If your application for remission was granted, you can apply for the reimbursement of the tuition fee in order to get the money transferred back to your bank account. The application for reimbursement can also be submitted at the same time as the application for remission of the tuition fee.
- What happens after I have applied for remission/reimbursement?

  The processing of your application takes approximately four weeks. You will receive information about the outcome via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.
embed_text:
Tuition fee > Remission and reimbursement > FAQ about the remission

/

- Remission due to illness: What qualifies als illness?

  Reasons for illness may be, among other things, major operations, chronic illness or also mental impairments. You need a [confirmation by a medical specialist](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_Fachaerztliche_Bestaetigung_Erlass_EN.pdf), that you are thereby impaired from your studies for at least two months during the semester. The diagnosis does not need to be stated.
- What shall I do if I do not have all proofs for applying for remission until the application deadline?

  In this case you must pay the tuition fee. Afterwards, you will be able to apply for remission and reimbursement at the same time. These two applications can be submitted for the winter semester until the following 28/29 February and for the summer semester until 30 September.
- My application for remission of the tuition fee was granted, but I have already paid the tuition fee. What can I do?

  If your application for remission was granted, you can apply for the reimbursement of the tuition fee in order to get the money transferred back to your bank account. The application for reimbursement can also be submitted at the same time as the application for remission of the tuition fee.
- What happens after I have applied for remission/reimbursement?

  The processing of your application takes approximately four weeks. You will receive information about the outcome via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

title: How do I organise my studies?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
#### Financing your studies

In addition to the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), you will have to cover other costs as well: You need to consider your living expenses (accommodation, food, mobility, etc.) and study-related expenses (books, writing material, etc.), and plan accordingly.

- We have compiled the most important information about scholarships, funding and work under "[Studying and Living](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)".
- You can find further information at the [Sozialreferat der ÖH Uni Wien](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/offices/social-affairs) (students' union social services division).

title: How do I organise my studies?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
#### Study law

You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions regarding study law, you can contact the following offices:

- The [directorate of studies](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and organisational tasks during your studies.
- Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of deviating examination methods for students with disabilities. The SPL makes any fundamental decisions in this regard. The [Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei](/en/accessible-studies/)') offers (also anonymous) advice on this.
- The [Office of the Studienpräses](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at) provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the directorate of studies can solve.
- If you have questions about [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) and [tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/), please get in touch with the [Admission Office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/).
- In individual cases, the [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/)) of the Federal Ministry can help find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution concerned.

A degree programme is based on regulations and laws. In the following section you find the most important regulations:

- [Curriculum - the legal basis for degree programmes](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/)
- [Statutes of the University of Vienna - study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

title: How do I organise my studies?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Please follow the Guidelines for Good Scientific Practice from the beginning of your studies until the graduation. You find useful information here:

<https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/sicherung-der-guten-wissenschaftlichen-praxis/plagiat/> (in German)

[oeawi.at/en/guidelines/](https://oeawi.at/en/guidelines/)

- [Regulation for pregnant students and nursing mothers](/en/pregnant-students-and-nursing-mothers/)

#### Student participation

The Austrian National Union of Students represents all students studying at Austrian universities on both the federal level, and the level of university and studies.

- [Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft (ÖH)](https://www.oeh.ac.at/) - Austrian National Union of Students
- [ÖH Uni Wien](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/) (Austrian National Union of Students at the University of Vienna)
- [Students' representatives at the University of Vienna](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen)
- Union of Students Act [(HochschülerInnenschaftsgesetz)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)

The Unit for Quality Assurance carries out course evaluations and graduate surveys to improve teaching at the University of Vienna. Students are welcome to voice their opinions.

- [Course evaluation](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/evaluationen/lehrveranstaltungen/)
- [Graduate survey](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/analysen/studienabschlussbefragung/)
- [Unit for Quality Assurance](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/)

#### Student mobility

The International Office helps you plan and realise stays abroad during your studies in the framework of mobility programmes.

- [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/study-abroad-with-erasmus-and-co)

#### Diversity

Diversity enriches our community life by opening up different ways of understanding, thinking and acting. The perception, recognition and utilisation of diversity are therefore key fundamental values of the University of Vienna.

- [Dimensions of diversity](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Culture & Equality](http://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/gleichstellung-diversitaet/)
- [Contact persons](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/prevention-counselling/counselling-office)
- [Accessible studies](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [Accessibility at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/)

Not only your studies but also obligations beyond university life are posing challenges. There are many aspects that you need to reconcile with your studies, e.g.

title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: What else could you be interested in?
text:
Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

©

title: Web services
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Web services - u:space, u:find, Moodle, Webmail
heading_paths: u:space - the portal for your degree programme
text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

text:

- Register for Moodle courses

For information about introductory training courses on using Moodle, please go to the website of the [Vienna University Computer Center](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/e-learning/).

The Vienna University Computer Center (ZID) provides a wide range of IT services for students.

For more information on ...

title: Studying and exams – Things worth knowing about studying
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Studying: on site, remotely, in hybrid form
Assistance in planning your schedule of classes
text:
Here, you can get up-to-date information about the semester and about the culture of studying and holding exams at the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien / derknopfdruecker.com

[### Studying on site](/en/studying-exams/studying-on-site/)

[### Studying remotely](/en/studying-exams/studying-remotely/)

[### Studying in hybrid form](/en/studying-exams/studying-in-hybrid-form/)

[### University in a nutshell](/en/degree-programmes/choosing-the-right-programme/university-in-a-nutshell/)

[### Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/)

[### Study support services](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/)

Studying and completing achievements (exams) at the University means that you have a lot of freedom on the one hand, but also a lot of responsibility, on the other. The biggest difference to student life at school, universities of applied sciences and some other universities is the organisation of studies, which is characterised by planning your schedule of classes relatively freely.

The courses and exams that you have to take (regardless of their form, i.e. on-site, digital or hybrid) are not specified entirely in a strict schedule. They are a combination of your degree programme’s curriculum and the range of courses offered in the current semester.

Teachers use various digital learning platforms. Here, the [learning platform Moodle](https://moodle.univie.ac.at/theme/university_boost/login/index.php?errorcode=4) is a crucial tool. You can find an overview of the different tools on the [service pages of the Vienna University Computer Center (ZID)](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/software-ai/software-for-students).

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

[#### STEOP - introductory and orientation period](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/)

[#### Web services for your student life](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

[#### Students' representatives](https://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen)

title: Studying and exams – Things worth knowing about studying
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - the beginning of my studies? The Admission Office is the gateway to the University of Vienna. It is responsible for the admission to bachelor’s, diploma and master’s programmes. In addition, it is responsible for all other matters related to the admission to degree programmes, such as entrance exam procedures, the tuition/Students’ Union fees or the university entrance qualification examination. For further information, please visit <https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/>. If you have any questions, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). All information and contacts for a successful start to your studies can be found in the University of Vienna [Welcome Guide](/en/welcome/welcome-guide/). (Last updated: 21 September 2022) - the organisation of studies? The website of Teaching Affairs and Student Services provides a comprehensive overview of information and services relating to the organisation of studies: [studying.univie.ac.at](http://studying.univie.ac.at). If you would like to contact us, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). - study-related matters? The best contact points for particular questions during your studies are the [StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) as well as the students’ representatives of your degree programme (e.g. for subject-related information about studies and the range of courses, the registration system, registration and de-registration deadlines, exams, recognition of exams and graduation). [Contact details of StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=studienservicecenter%20c%3A18%20%20s) - a specific course? If you have any questions about a specific course or exam, please contact the relevant lecturer. - international matters? The

title: Studying on site
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Check list: Before the beginning of the semester
Check list for the day of a scheduled course/exam
text:
This website provides students with information, tips and tricks as well as with the most important [FAQs on studying on site](/en/studying-exams/studying-on-site/#c612968).

© Barbara Mair

- Particularly for new students: Get yourself the University of Vienna Welcome Guide as an [e-book](/en/welcome/welcome-guide/) or order a [printed copy](https://www.facultasdruckt.at/welcomeguide) free of charge via facultas.
- Check on [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html), how courses/exams will be held (on site, digital or hybrid).
- Register for all courses/exams on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/gast/home). Following registration, you can access the learning materials on Moodle.

© Universität Wien / derknopfdruecker.com

- Check your [Webmail account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/e-mail) as well as [Moodle](https://moodle.univie.ac.at) to see if there are any short-term changes regarding the relevant course/exam.
- Do not forget to bring
  - laptop, charger and/or a powerbank for courses
  - if necessary a headset with a microphone, if you are at the University and participate in online courses and
  - u:card for exams

If you want to use the on-site services of the [Vienna University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/), please get informed about the applicable security rules in advance.

[### ub.univie.ac.at](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/)

You can study at the following locations.

[### Computer Rooms](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/computer-rooms/)

[### Studying remotely](/en/studying-exams/studying-remotely/)

[### Studying in hybrid form](/en/studying-exams/studying-in-hybrid-form/)

title: Regulations for certain groups of students who cannot take part in courses/exams on site.
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
deviating regulations for groups of students
text:
"In case of force majeure (such as natural disasters, pandemics) or in case of comparable severe restrictions in emergency situations (such as war or war-like situations), the Rectorate may enact deviating regulations for students who are directly affected by the relevant situation and who are able to complete a partial achievement as part of a course with continuous assessment online or take an exam in a digital format, but who are unable to participate on site."

Statutes of the University of Vienna, Section Study Law, § 13i

The corresponding regulations currently in force are summarized below. The official text of the ordinance can be found in the [University Gazette](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/).

Regardless of this special rule, students with impairments are still entitled to request an [alternative mode of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/).

No corresponding regulations are currently in force.

Statutes of the University of Vienna, Section Study Law, § 13i

The corresponding regulations currently in force are summarized below. The official text of the ordinance can be found in the [University Gazette](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/).

Regardless of this special rule, students with impairments are still entitled to request an [alternative mode of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/).

No corresponding regulations are currently in force.

title: Studying in hybrid form
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Hybrid in terms of ‘mixed’ refers to the way in which courses and exams are held. These can take place on site, digitally or in a hybrid form (a combination of on-site and digital teaching).

A hybrid course as a combination of digital and on-site teaching can take various forms and is labelled in the course directory.

Students are allocated to groups that take turns attending the on-site course units. The group that is not on site works on digital assignments asynchronously. This means that courses held in a hybrid form also have course units that students attend in person.

Part of the students attend the course unit in the lecture hall, while the other part of the group joins the course digitally (e.g. via video conferencing tools or u:stream in the lecture hall) at the same time. For this setting, the lecturers will announce the procedure for registration (seat reservation) for the on-site course units.

The contents taught via digital media are also addressed during the on-site lessons and, therefore, form a complete picture of digital and personal exchange and the knowledge acquired. The communication of knowledge is based on the broad spectrum of digital opportunities, such as videos, video chats, podcasts, online tests and more.

[### Studying on site](/en/studying-exams/studying-on-site/)

[### Studying remotely](/en/studying-exams/studying-remotely/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

title: Planning your semester
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

Study organisation > Planning your semester

At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

#

title: Planning your semester
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,

text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)

text:
STEOP-Mentoring aims to support you during your first semester at the University of Vienna! Small peer-goups led by student STEOP-mentors serve as a voluntary addition to the lectures of the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) and are there to help you in mastering its challenges.

title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
heading_paths: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
text: Have you ever come across the phrase ‘prüfungsaktiv’ or ‘number of exams taken’ and wondered what it means? In a nutshell: Several years ago, a new university financing scheme was introduced in Austria. The Republic of Austria provides funding to the public universities and, thus, also funds a large part of your studies. The allocation of financial resources to universities in Austria is based on the number of students being 'prüfungsaktiv' i.e. students taking a certain number of exams at the relevant university. Bachelor’s, master’s and diploma programmes in which a student completes at least 16 ECTS credits or at least 8 SWS within the academic year are considered 'prüfungsaktiv'. In the academic year of 2020/2021, 53,000 students in total took a certain number of exams at the University of Vienna. The target to be achieved is set every three years between the ministry and the university. Every ECTS credit counts: for you personally and for the University of Vienna Taking exams and writing papers is part of studying. They contribute to your personal success and allow you to progress in your studies. If you take a certain number of exams in your degree programme(s), you also contribute to more funding being allocated to the University of Vienna for innovative research and high-quality teaching. Therefore, the University of Vienna strives to ensure that you pass many exams. Your directors of

title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
heading_paths: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
text: exams. # FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity) / - When are students ‘prüfungsaktiv’? Students who obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in their degree programme per academic year have taken a certain number of exams and are thus considered 'prüfungaktiv’. This is relevant in bachelor’s, diploma and master's programmes. It does not apply to doctoral programmes. Only ECTS credits of courses passed are considered in the calculation. All degree programmes in which students take a certain number of exams are taken into account. - Do all earned ECTS credits count towards examination activity? ECTS credits from recognitions are not taken into account for the calculation. For cooperative degree programmes, only the share of ECTS credits that counts towards examination activity at the University of Vienna is considered. - Why is the number of students who are ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (taking a certain number of exams) important? The more students are ‘prüfungsaktiv’ in all their degree programmes, the more financial resources the University can use for research and teaching. In addition, evidence of having taking a certain number of exams can be a prerequisite for receiving financial aids, such as Austrian family allowance. - What is the difference between the minimum number of credits and the number of students taking a certain number of exams? Minimum number of credits: 16 ECTS credits obtained within 4 semesters For any bachelor’s and diploma programme

title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
heading_paths: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
text: exams easier to ensure that students take a certain number of exams?esenkt und die Prüfungen leichter? No. The performance requirements are not changed at all. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

title: Minimum number of credits
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
You have to obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in every bachelor’s and diploma programme within four semesters.

This applies

- to all programmes to which you are admitted since the winter semester of 2022/2023
- and to all programmes that you [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) from this time.

Otherwise, the admission to the degree programme expires. If you would like to apply for re-admission to the same degree programme (= resumption of studies) at the University of Vienna, you can only do so after two [academic years](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/) (= four semesters).
embed_text:
Study organisation > Minimum number of credits

You have to obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in every bachelor’s and diploma programme within four semesters.

This applies

- to all programmes to which you are admitted since the winter semester of 2022/2023
- and to all programmes that you [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) from this time.

Otherwise, the admission to the degree programme expires. If you would like to apply for re-admission to the same degree programme (= resumption of studies) at the University of Vienna, you can only do so after two [academic years](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/) (= four semesters).

title: Minimum number of credits
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

title: Minimum number of credits
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
heading_paths: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
text: / - Who does not have to obtain the minimum number of credits (exemptions)? Students with proven non-temporary physical and/or mental impairments do not have to obtain the required minimum number of credits. You can take the following steps: 1. The University of Vienna strives to enable equal opportunities during studies and to promote students’ examination activity (‘Prüfungsaktivität’). Students with an impairment have a right to [alternative modes of assessment](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/). The [Accessible Studying team](/en/accessible-studies/counselling/) provides information about different possibilities to compensate for disadvantages at the University of Vienna. It is our common objective to help you progress in your studies. 2. If, despite these measures, you cannot obtain the minimum number of credits in time [(deadline as displayed in u:space)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/), you can apply for an exemption. You will need a [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_aerztliche_Bestaetigung_MSL_EN.pdf) stating that a physical or mental impairment makes/made it difficult for you to participate in your degree programme for at least six months. A diagnosis is not required. You must upload the confirmation to the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/1837) in due time (= before the end of the fourth semester). Please note: According to [section 3 of the Federal Equal Opportunities for People with Disabilities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/eli/bgbl/i/2005/82/P3/NOR40066740?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Kundmachungsorgan=&Index=&Titel=Bundes-Behindertengleichstellungsgesetz&Gesetzesnummer=&VonArtikel=&BisArtikel=&VonParagraf=3&BisParagraf=&VonAnlage=&BisAnlage=&Typ=&Kundmachungsnummer=&Unterzeichnungsdatum=&FassungVom=14.02.2021&VonInkrafttretedatum=&BisInkrafttretedatum=&VonAusserkrafttretedatum=&BisAusserkrafttretedatum=&NormabschnittnummerKombination=Und&ImRisSeitVonDatum=&ImRisSeitBisDatum=&ImRisSeit=Undefined&ResultPageSize=100&Suchworte=&Position=1&SkipToDocumentPage=true&ResultFunctionToken=a3c06bb6-ee80-4fe3-8c36-f07d635726c2) (Bundes-Behindertengleichstellungsgesetz), a non-temporary impairment is an impairment expected to last more than 6 months. - Who has to obtain the minimum number of credits? / - Who does not have to obtain the minimum number of credits (exemptions)? Students with proven non-temporary physical and/or mental impairments do not have to obtain the required minimum number of credits. You can take the following steps: 1. The University of Vienna strives to enable equal opportunities during studies and to promote students’ examination activity (‘Prüfungsaktivität’). Students with an impairment have a right to [alternative modes of assessment](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/). The [Accessible Studying team](/en/accessible-studies/counselling/) provides information about different possibilities to compensate for disadvantages at the University of Vienna. It is our common objective to help you progress in your studies. 2. If, despite these measures, you cannot obtain the minimum number of credits in time [(deadline as displayed in u:space)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/), you can apply for an exemption. You will need a [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_aerztliche_Bestaetigung_MSL_EN.pdf) stating that a physical or mental impairment makes/made it difficult for you to participate in your degree programme for at least six months. A diagnosis is not required. You must upload the confirmation to the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/1837) in due time (= before the end of the fourth semester). Please note: According to [section 3 of the Federal Equal Opportunities for People with Disabilities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/eli/bgbl/i/2005/82/P3/NOR40066740?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Kundmachungsorgan=&Index=&Titel=Bundes-Behindertengleichstellungsgesetz&Gesetzesnummer=&VonArtikel=&BisArtikel=&VonParagraf=3&BisParagraf=&VonAnlage=&BisAnlage=&Typ=&Kundmachungsnummer=&Unterzeichnungsdatum=&FassungVom=14.02.2021&VonInkrafttretedatum=&BisInkrafttretedatum=&VonAusserkrafttretedatum=&BisAusserkrafttretedatum=&NormabschnittnummerKombination=Und&ImRisSeitVonDatum=&ImRisSeitBisDatum=&ImRisSeit=Undefined&ResultPageSize=100&Suchworte=&Position=1&SkipToDocumentPage=true&ResultFunctionToken=a3c06bb6-ee80-4fe3-8c36-f07d635726c2) (Bundes-Behindertengleichstellungsgesetz), a non-temporary impairment is an impairment expected to last more than 6 months. - Who has to obtain the minimum number of credits? All students who are admitted to a bachelor’s or diploma programme since the winter semester of 2022/2023 have to obtain the minimum number of credits for this programme within four semesters. This applies to newly admitted students as well as to students who [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) or [change](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/) their bachelor’s or diploma programme at this time and to students who enrol in an [additional degree programme](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/). The minimum number of credits does not apply to bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you were admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23. The minimum number of credits also does not apply to master’s or doctoral programmes. - By when do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits and what are the deadlines? The relevant examination results and other study achievements must be obtained no later than one month after the end of the fourth semester from the start/resumption of your studies. This means you have time until 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. For example: You start your bachelor's programme in the winter semester of 2022/2023. You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain

You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain the minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2024 the latest. The deadline is on 31 October 2024. No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all bachelor's and diploma programmes who begin or [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) their studies in the winter semester of 2022/2023 or later must obtain the minimum number of credits. The minimum number of credits must be obtained once within four semesters. If you interrupt your studies (again) during these four semesters, the ECTS credits and semesters are counted after you resume your studies. However, once the minimum number of credits has been obtained, it does not have to be obtained again for this degree programme. For example: You started your studies in the winter semester of 2022/23, studied for 2 semesters and obtained 12 ECTS credits. You interrupt your studies in the following semester. You resume your studies in the summer semester of 2024. You still have 2 semesters to obtain the missing 4 ECTS credits - i.e. until one month after the end of the fourth semester at the latest. If you interrupt your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not
