Many instructions for prompt engineering can be reduced to a simple principle:
embed_text:
Study organisation > Prompting correctly

The quality of  AI output depends to a great extent on how precisely and thoughtfully a query is formulated. AI tools do not respond independently to scientific requirements; rather, they are guided by the specifications they receive through prompts.

#

A prompt is the input or instruction you give to an AI to perform a specific task or respond in a desired manner.

This means that AI does not work "on its own", but always responds to what you specify. Think of AI as an extremely powerful assistant with enormous expertise that can work very quickly. However, it is crucial that you clearly formulate what you expect from this assistant. Unclear or vague instructions usually lead to correspondingly imprecise results.

The clarity, structure and thoroughness of a prompt directly impact the quality of the answers provided by the AI.

- The AI-tool independently supplements missing information – based onprobabilities.
- The AI-tool determines all actions that are not explicitly specified by the user.

It is therefore very important to think carefully about the prompt and give the AI sufficient guidance. Only then will you get truly relevant answers. With superficial prompts, the answer often remains very generic and general.

Many instructions for prompt engineering can be reduced to a simple principle:

title: Prompting correctly
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Academic work and writing with AI
Controlling AI in a targeted manner: the importance of prompting correctly
heading_path: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
heading_paths: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
A. Aim and Action: What exactly should the AI do?
text:
Write your instructions as if you were working with a real person who needs precise and complete instructions.

This idea gives rise to the A.C.T. principle, which outlines the three key components of a good prompt. The principle "Act as if it's human" serves exclusively as a communicative aid: it helps to formulate instructions clearly, completely and comprehensibly – just as one would do when dealing with human assistants. This results in better control of the AI, not an attribution of human characteristics.

An effective prompt answers three questions:

Describe the specific task that the AI should perform in this prompt, e.g.:

- Explanation
- Analysis
- Comparison
- Translation
- Rephrasing
- Brainstorming
- Feedback
- ...

The more specific you are in describing the task, the more targeted the response will be.

title: Prompting correctly
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Academic work and writing with AI
Controlling AI in a targeted manner: the importance of prompting correctly
heading_path: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
heading_paths: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
C. Context: What information is relevant to the task?
Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
T. Ton: In what form should the answer be given?
text:
Here, you provide the AI with the specific, content-related or situational framework it needs to adapt its response. This may include:

- An assigned role (e.g. "You are an expert in ...")
- Target audience
- purpose of the text
- Subject area
- Situation (university, seminar paper, presentation, etc.)
- Examples
- Your own documents
- A specific use case

Defining the role that AI is to take on provides an important framework. By assigning a role, AI can provide the answer from the perspective of that role and activate the associated domain knowledge.

The context helps the AI-tool to classify who the answer is intended for and what it is intended for.

The tone determines the style and format in which the reply should be written, e.g.:

- Objective and scientific
- Simple and understandable
- As continuous text
- In bullet points
- As a table
- In the style of a presentation slide
- Level of detail and limits (how long, short or detailed)
- ...

The desired style and format have a significant impact on the output of the AI tool.

If you include these three elements in your prompt, you will get a much more targeted response on your first attempt. Experience shows that well-thought-out prompts require far fewer attempts.

text: - Use emotion prompts: emotion prompts deliberately add emotional or evaluative elements to an instruction, for example by emphasising particular importance or consequences. Formulations such as “I will give you 1 million dollars” or “This is very important for my career” do not attribute genuine emotions to AI, but function as signals indicating relevance and the need for care. This means that AI assigns greater weight to the task and tends to respond in a more detailed, structured or cautious manner (recommended reading: [Large Language Models Understand and Can be Enhanced by Emotional Stimuli](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.11760)) - Guidance on diversity and perspectives: Clear instructions such as "use gender-neutral language", "take diverse perspectives into account" or "include the viewpoints of marginalised groups" influence how AI prioritises content and increase the likelihood of more balanced and inclusive responses. - Addressing hallucinations: Explicitly state in your prompt that uncertainties should be indicated (for example “If you are unsure, please say so.”). This can reduce hallucinations. However, they cannot be entirely ruled out, which means that you must still carefully verify the content. - Q&A: It is not possible to anticipate everything, so it is advisable to instruct AI to ask you follow-up questions proactively: “Ask me as many questions as necessary to complete the task well.” - New topic? New chat: If you are working on a new topic, it is best to start a

New chat: If you are working on a new topic, it is best to start a new chat. This prevents previous content from inadvertently influencing the response. In addition, it is advisable to deactivate the memory function, if your AI tool provides one ([ChatGPT memory function](https://openai.com/de-DE/index/memory-and-new-controls-for-chatgpt/)). When working with AI, there are sometimes situations where the tool does not seem to understand your questions or gives unexpected answers. The problem can often be solved with the following tips:

title: Prompting correctly
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Academic work and writing with AI
Controlling AI in a targeted manner: the importance of prompting correctly
heading_path: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
heading_paths: Rule of Thumb: „Act as if it’s human“
text:
- Reduce complexity: If you give AI complex tasks, it may not perform them satisfactorily. In this case, it can help to break the task down into smaller parts and also tell the AI in which order the tasks should be performed:
  - Do X first, then Y
  - Proceed step by step
- Use different wording: Sometimes it helps to rephrase the prompt to make a difference.
- Check the scope of knowledge: AI systems are based on training data that was available up to a certain point in time. The AI you are using may not know anything about current events or specific topics (see Knowledge Cut-Off at [u:ai](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/uai-the-university-of-viennas-ai-tool/)). If your prompt refers to something very new or niche knowledge, the AI may not have an answer. In this case, there is a high risk of [hallucinations](?id=225758#1268300).
- Take a break and try again: When a platform is heavily used or before or during technical updates, the systems sometimes do not respond as desired. In such cases, the best solution is often to take a break and try again later.
- Use online resources: Many AI platforms have helpful documentation or user forums where you can find tips and advice.

text: The use of AI in scientific work offers numerous possibilities, but requires conscious and reflective use based on the [principles of good scientific practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035). Honesty, transparency, independence and traceability are key principles that must also be upheld when working with AI.

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
AI can provide useful support during studies and exam preparation, for example by structuring the learning process, revision, feedback or self-assessment.

However, it is crucial to note that whether and how AI is permitted in examinations is determined by the respective lecturers. Students bear full responsibility for the independence, accuracy and transparency of their work. AI does not replace independent learning or engagement with the subject matter.

#

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Examinations with AI: What does apply here?
heading_paths: Examinations with AI: What does apply here?
text: At the University of Vienna, an examination is a formal opportunity for students to demonstrate the level of knowledge and skills they have acquired and the achievement of the learning outcomes defined in the curriculum. It is not merely a matter of assessing performance for the award of a grade or academic degree, but also of receiving valuable feedback on learning progress and ensuring fairness and academic integrity. In addition to oral and written examinations, written assignments and presentations also count as examinations. In the current age of AI, the nature of examinations is evolving. The focus is shifting from mere knowledge reproduction to assessing higher levels of competence, such as reflection, transfer and design. Consequently, emphasis is now on both the final outcome and the individual learner's progress and development. Whether and how you may use AI in examinations is determined by the respective examiners. - Where can you find the rules and regulations? The binding provisions specifying which AI tools are permitted for a particular course or examination can be found directly in the course description in u:find. As a general rule, anything not explicitly listed there as permitted is prohibited. If you are unsure, you should always consult your lecturers in order to clarify expectations and framework conditions at an early stage. - Transparency and documentation: If you are permitted to use AI tools for written assignments,

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: What can AI be used for?
How can I use AI tools for learning in practice?
text:
AI tools can support exam preparation when used in a targeted and reflective manner. They are not a substitute for independent learning, but they can help to structure, deepen and reflect on the learning process.

AI tools can be particularly helpful in organising and supporting the learning process. This includes, among other things:

- Reflection on your own time management
- Support in organising learning activities and setting priorities
- Creating and adapting study and revision plans
- Motivation through structured learning steps or gamification elements
- Revising content, for example through questions or study plans on specific topics
- Gaining an overview of key terms, concepts or subject areas

This means that AI can help to structure the learning process, but it does not replace the need to engage with the subject matter.

AI tools can support learning in various ways, especially when used specifically for organisation, repetition, feedback and self-assessment. The following examples show typical usage scenarios that students can usefully integrate into their exam preparation.

- Motivation through structured learning steps or gamification elements

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Some learning platforms use AI to provide personalised support for learning processes, adapting them to each user's individual progress.

- Duolingo, for instance, assists language learners in practising regularly, identifying weaknesses, and automatically adapting tasks to their current level.
- [Khan Academy](https://de.khanacademy.org/) offers adaptive exercise formats, particularly in mathematics and science, that respond to tasks that have already been solved.
- Flashcard apps such as [Anki](https://apps.ankiweb.net/) can also support learning. They facilitate the creation of flashcards and present them using repetition algorithms at precisely the right time, based on experience, when content should be repeated. This can be particularly beneficial for long-term exam preparation.
- [Notebook LM](https://notebooklm.google.com/) is a highly versatile tool. Here, you can create audio summaries in the form of podcasts, quizzes, flashcards, videos and infographics from documents you have uploaded yourself.

AI supports organisation and repetition here, not actual understanding.

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
AI tools can be used as active feedback partners in learning. The AI tool can

- Suggest learning strategies
- Assess learning objectives
- Provide feedback on self-formulated answers
- Ask specific comprehension questions
- Offer suggestions for improvement
- Point out ambiguities or gaps in an explanation
- Present alternative perspectives

Example:

You answer an exam question in writing and then let the AI check whether the answer is coherent, which points are still missing or where terms have been used imprecisely. You remain responsible for the content.

Some AI-supported applications assist with specific tasks or with understanding complex content.

- Apps such as [Socrat.ai](https://socrat.ai/) can help with solving various tasks by explaining steps and providing additional resources.
- Tools like Perplexity can be used for initial thematic orientation or rough research.
- Generative AI tools such as ChatGPT or u:ai can be used to explain complex content in simple language, compile key terms in a subject area, or illustrate connections using examples.

Study organisation > AI and Exams > AI in exam preparation

AI tools can be used as active feedback partners in learning. The AI tool can

- Suggest learning strategies
- Assess learning objectives
- Provide feedback on self-formulated answers
- Ask specific comprehension questions
- Offer suggestions for improvement
- Point out ambiguities or gaps in an explanation
- Present alternative perspectives

Example:

You answer an exam question in writing and then let the AI check whether the answer is coherent, which points are still missing or where terms have been used imprecisely. You remain responsible for the content.

Some AI-supported applications assist with specific tasks or with understanding complex content.

- Apps such as [Socrat.ai](https://socrat.ai/) can help with solving various tasks by explaining steps and providing additional resources.
- Tools like Perplexity can be used for initial thematic orientation or rough research.
- Generative AI tools such as ChatGPT or u:ai can be used to explain complex content in simple language, compile key terms in a subject area, or illustrate connections using examples.

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Important information for learning with AI
heading_paths: Important information for learning with AI
text: The use of AI tools can support the learning process, but it also presents challenges. In order to use AI sensibly and responsibly, it is important to consider a few fundamental aspects. - Due to its [functionality](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268970), AI may produce incorrect, incomplete or imprecise information, even if it sounds plausible. Therefore, it is advisable to always compare responses with your own materials. - Subject-specific terminology may be used differently depending on the discipline. When asked to define technical terms, AI generally draws on the meaning most frequently represented in its training data. This may result in definitions that do not correspond to the specific discipline or that lack subject-specific nuances. Only adopt definitions or explanations if they align with the academic requirements of your course. - AI-supported learning tools can heavily structure the learning process, for example through predefined learning paths, automatic revision or recommended content. While this can be helpful, it also entails the risk that your learning process becomes overly externally directed: learning is individual and not every recommendation generated by AI suits your personal learning style. Therefore, consciously set your own priorities. - A common risk factor is increasing dependence on AI tools. If explanations, solution strategies or summaries are regularly delegated to AI, this may impair your ability to think through content independently and to solve problems on your own. Especially at the beginning of your

title: AI and Exams
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Important information for learning with AI
Tip: Combine digital and analogue learning methods
heading_paths: Important information for learning with AI
Tip: Combine digital and analogue learning methods
text:
AI-supported learning is most effective when combined with other forms of learning. Textbooks, lecture notes, lecture materials, videos and exercise sheets remain key learning resources. In addition, taking handwritten notes also promotes the processing of contexts.

The reflective use of AI is a skill that must be learned. This includes understanding the areas in which AI is and isn't suitable, formulating precise and targeted [prompts](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/controlling-ai-in-a-targeted-manner-the-importance-of-prompting-correctly/#c1269203), critically evaluating answers, and taking responsibility for one's own learning process. This approach develops with experience and requires continuous reflection.

Regardless of the tools used, the following applies:

- Responsibility for the learning process, for understanding the content and for the results always lies with the individual.
- AI can support, structure and provide inspiration. However, learning, understanding and assessment remain independent achievements.

title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool
text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool
text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool
text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool
heading_paths: Build your own chatbots with Tailored AI
Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool
heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
heading_paths: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
text:
u:ai also offers an important advantage in terms of copyright. Since uploaded content is not used for AI training, you can upload copyrighted material to u:ai (but please note the regulations on [private copying](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1268388)).

With commercial tools, uploads can be incorporated into training data or further processed. This means that users lose control over how their content is used. Therefore, you may only use copyrighted material in commercial tools if the data is not used for training AI tools.

In summary, the following applies:

u:ai offers a clearly regulated, transparent, and legally compliant framework for the use of AI in academic studies. This makes the platform particularly suitable for university work processes where data protection, copyright, and scientific integrity play a central role.

title: Further information
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Who should I ask about AI?
text:
Further links with additional information, services and guidelines on dealing with artificial intelligence can be found here.

If you have any questions about the use of AI in courses, please always consult the following first:

- the information in u:find
- notes on Moodle and
- information on specific assignments and examinations

If anything is unclear, please always speak to the course teacher.

There are interdisciplinary courses on AI that are also eligible for ECTS:

- Extension Curriculum ["Understanding Artificial Intelligence"](https://informatik.univie.ac.at/studium/studienangebot/erweiterungscurricula-ec/kuenstliche-intelligenz-verstehen/)
- Courses on AI in [Studienstart Plus](https://studienstartplus.univie.ac.at/)

The [Centre for Teaching and Learning](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) offers consultations and workshops on AI:

- **[Peer Point](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/student-research-hub/ctl-peer-point/)** for questions about using AI in teaching? Peer Point is here to help you.
- **[Handouts for Students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/ressourcen/handouts-leitfaeden-und-infomaterial/#c1123913)**
- [Schreib-](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/schreibberatung/moegliche-themen-einer-schreibberatung/) und Lernberatungen ([PFSB](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/waehrend-des-studiums/peer-studienfortschrittsberatung/))
- Workshop: "AI, but how? Using tools appropriately in academic work" as part of the writing marathons and the University Library [Nachtschicht](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/events/008620.html)

- Library materials for the workshop "AI, but how – effective prompting and research?" [Slides, working materials and further links to the workshop](https://padlet.com/annakrenn4/ai-literacy-ki-kompetenz-f-r-studierende-ai-literacy-ai-comp-tk3fa216fnjk5c22)
- Library materials for the workshop "AI literature search tools" [Slides, working materials and further links to the workshop](https://padlet.com/lillinemetz/ki-literaturrecherche-tools-ai-literacy-2-8ocvkv7s7tn0a5co)

Information about the Computer Center's AI services can be found on the following pages:

- [u:ai – AI-Software](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uai)
- [AI-Services of the Computer Center](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/ai/ai-services-of-the-zid)

- [University of Vienna guidelines on the use of AI in teaching](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2092606) (in German)

title: Studying safely
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

- [Security management](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/en/services/security/)
- [Threat management – non-violent university](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/violence-free-university-prevention-and-support)
- [House Rules](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/house-rules/)
- [Laboratory and Workshop Regulations](https://intra.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/upload/rrm/ANS/Downloads/Laborordnung/LABOR-und_WERKSTA%CC%88TTENORDNUNG_EN.pdf)

- [General Fire Safety Regulations](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/d_rrm/Downloads/2015_2016_Brandschutzordnung.pdf) (in German)
- [Procedure in case of student accidents](#c1215109)
- [Student’s Union (ÖH) insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/services/insurance-policy-austrian-students-union-oh)
- [Emergency text message](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

title: Studying safely
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Procedure in case of student accidents
heading_paths: Procedure in case of student accidents
text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

title: Studying safely
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Do not give thieves a chance!
text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [IT security: Tips for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/services/it-security/)
- [12 IT security commandments for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/it-news/article/news/12-it-security-gebote-fuer-nutzerinnen/?no_cache=1)
- [Password management](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password/)
- [Tips regarding the password](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password-tips/)

- [Social media and blog netiquette](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/netiquette/)
- [How to prevent Zoombombing](https://zid.univie.ac.at/it-news/artikel/news/keine-angst-vor-zoombombing/) (in German)

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. You can contact the following organisational units to seek help for yourself or for all conflict parties, or to establish a network that can help you in case of problems.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [ÖH Helpline](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/helpline/)
- [Psychological Counselling Service](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)
- [Accessible Studying Team of the University of Vienna](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/counselling/)
- [Sexual Harassment and Bullying Counselling Office](https://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/en/gender-equality-diversity/sexual-harassment-bullying-office/)
- [AKGleich - Equal Opportunities Working Party](https://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Arbiration Commission of the University of Vienna](https://schiedskommission.univie.ac.at/en/)

title: Studying safely
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: The following rules apply at the University of Vienna:
Not sure where to turn for help?
text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

title: Change of personal data
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
During your studies, some of your personal data might change (e.g. your address or academic degree). It may also happen that some of your personal data in our system are incorrect (e.g. date of birth, spelling of your name).

- You can change certain data yourself via u:space, while other changes require you to provide documentation.
- Data changes are only made for active students and persons who are in the process of applying for admission.
- Doctoral/PhD students who would like to change their personal data should contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
- If you are attending a university course and would like to change your personal data, please contact the [Service Center of the Postgraduate Cente](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/)r.

You can change the following data yourself via [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast).

- Forwarding e-mail address
- Phone number
- Home and different delivery address
- Bank account details

Please note that some of these fields are required fields. Bear in mind to keep your data up to date.

If your name has changed (e.g. through marriage), please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable documentation (e.g. marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card. As soon as your has been changed, you can order a new [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal Matters > My u:card.

title: Change of personal data
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Correction of date of birth or spelling of the name
text:
If you want your academic degree(s) that were awarded by a different university/higher education institution to appear on all your documents, please send the applicable documentation (e.g. official notice of degree award) via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office. We can only enter degrees awarded by academic institutions within the EU/EEA or degrees recognised through a [nostrification](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/) procedure. Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=ssc) of your degree programme, if an academic degree awarded by the University of Vienna does not appear in your personal data in u:space.

If you changed your citizenship, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (proof of citizenship and passport or ID card).

If your gender has been entered incorrectly, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (passport or ID card).

[Information about change of name and person for trans\*persons](http://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/aufgaben/namens-und-personenstandsaenderungen-fuer-transpersonen/)

In case your date of birth is incorrect or your name is misspelled in our system, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing documentation for the correct spelling or date of birth (passport or ID card).

title: Accessible Studies
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Students with impairments, disabilities or illnesses
Who does not belong to the target group?
text:
[### Accessibility portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/)

[### Alternative mode of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)

[### Remission of tuition fees](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[### Discontinuation (exam/course) (in German)](https://backend.univie.ac.at/index.php?id=104730&L=0)

Accessible Studies

Accessible Studies

This website provides comprehensive information about accessible studying for students with impairments, disabilities and/or illnesses. The Accessible Studying Team uses the term "students with impairments" as a short form. This term includes all persons belonging to the target group.

Do not hesitate to [ask if you are not sure](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');) whether you belong to the target group!

Information online for students with disabilities, impairments and/or chronical diseases are currently not fully available in English.

Counselling of the "Accessible Studying team ([Team Barrierefrei](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/zustaendigkeiten/anlaufstellen-intern/team-barrierefrei/))" is held in German and English. Students can contact the team via [E-Mail](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dctttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');), during the phone counselling times or in the personal counselling times, which can be found (in German) under "[Informationsangebote und Beratung](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/information-und-beratung/)".

Persons who experience disadvantages during their studies due to e.g.

- disabilities,
- physical and/or motor impairments,
- sensory impairments,
- mental impairments,
- chronic illnesses,
- autistic perception,
- dyslexia,
- acute illnesses, injuries, consequences of an accident
- or similar conditions.

Persons who experience difficulties that have no medically verifiable cause, such as

- language barriers (e.g. language of instruction is not the first language) or
- (general) organisational difficulties, fatigue or similar issue.

Accessible studies > Accessible Studies

[### Accessibility portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/)

[### Alternative mode of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)

[### Remission of tuition fees](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[### Discontinuation (exam/course) (in German)](https://backend.univie.ac.at/index.php?id=104730&L=0)

Accessible Studies

Accessible Studies

This website provides comprehensive information about accessible studying for students with impairments, disabilities and/or illnesses. The Accessible Studying Team uses the term "students with impairments" as a short form. This term includes all persons belonging to the target group.

Do not hesitate to [ask if you are not sure](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');) whether you belong to the target group!

Information online for students with disabilities, impairments and/or chronical diseases are currently not fully available in English.

Counselling of the "Accessible Studying team ([Team Barrierefrei](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/zustaendigkeiten/anlaufstellen-intern/team-barrierefrei/))" is held in German and English. Students can contact the team via [E-Mail](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dctttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');), during the phone counselling times or in the personal counselling times, which can be found (in German) under "[Informationsangebote und Beratung](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/information-und-beratung/)".

Persons who experience disadvantages during their studies due to e.g.

- disabilities,
- physical and/or motor impairments,
- sensory impairments,
- mental impairments,
- chronic illnesses,
- autistic perception,
- dyslexia,
- acute illnesses, injuries, consequences of an accident
- or similar conditions.

Persons who experience difficulties that have no medically verifiable cause, such as

- language barriers (e.g. language of instruction is not the first language) or
- (general) organisational difficulties, fatigue or similar issue.

title: Team Barrierefrei - Counselling
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The Team Barrierefrei is a small team dedicated to accessible studying at the University of Vienna. We advise students/prospecitve students with disabilities and lecturers on all aspects of accessible studying and teaching.

Bei der Beratung. © Barbara Mair

- Counselling and providing information for students/prospective students with disabilities
- Counselling and providing information for lecturers/examiners on the implementation of accessible teaching and accessible examination
- Conselling and providing information to colleagues on accessibility aspects in various fields of application
- Management of special medical evidence and confidential information for the approval of alternative modes of assessment
- Providing Information and referral to external institutions
- Participation in the further development of accessibility measures within the University

title: Team Barrierefrei - Counselling
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
To contact us by email, please write to [barrierefrei@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');) for initial inquiries.

Please note that inquiries will be answered in the order in which they are received and may take several weeks. The response may be delayed if, for example, information needs to be obtained from third parties. We therefore ask for your patience.

Attention: Processing can also take longer at peak times such as the start or end of the semester.

Please make your request as precisely as possible. If you require different examination methods to compensate for disadvantages due to an impairment relevant to your studies, please attach your evidence to the email. This can shorten the processing of your request. You can find further information about different testing methods and the evidence required for them at the following link: [Different testing methods](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)

For information and questions on the following topics, please use the links provided:

- [Accessible admission and aptitude procedures](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800)
- [Study Admission](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz)
- [Tuition fee](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39)

Personal consultation appointments are available on Wednesdays between 1pm and 5 pm with prior scheduling. Please contact us via [barrierefrei@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');) for an appointment.

title: Changes to exams and teaching
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Outweighing disadvantages resulting from an impairment
Outweighing disadvantages resulting from an impairment
Medical certificate as evidence at the University of Vienna
text:
Many students and prospective students with impairments are entitled to request exam adaptations or alternative modes of assessment. Read the information provided below to find out whether you are eligible, how the application and/or implementation works and what types of evidence you have to provide.

- [Exam adaptations for the entrance exam procedure (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/aufnahmeverfahren/aufnahme-eignungsverfahren-mit-beeintraechtigung/)

- [Alternative modes of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)
- [Modified curriculum (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/modifiziertes-curriculum/)
- [Discontinuing exams or courses for an important reason (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/)

By changing the mode of assessment, it is possible to outweigh many disadvantages resulting from an impairment. © Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

For many adaptations and support offers, you have to provide evidence to the University of Vienna of how the impairment manifests itself and how it affects your studies. Please note the general information about types of evidence you have to present to the University of Vienna.

[Link to further information about evidence of impairment](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/)

title: Alternative mode of assessment
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Alternative modes of assessment allow for exams that

- cover the same contents;
- require the same performance;
- but are held in a different/flexible mode.

The aim is to design exams and courses in such a way that affected students do not experience disadvantages due to their impairment.

An alternative mode of assessment does not mean that

- the affected students' achievements are not assessed;
- affected students are given an advantage;
- the examiners “turn a blind eye” or that
- studies are made easier for the affected students (“being carried through the degree programme”).

The changed mode of assessment comes closest to the initially intended mode while taking the student’s impairment into account. This is what makes studying for people with disabilities (creating more equal opportunities) possible in the first place.

Any type of exam and proof of performance can have an alternative mode of assessment. This includes exams that take place on site as well as in digital or hybrid form.

Examples of exam types where alternative examination methods can be arranged include:

- Oral exams
- Written exams
- Multiple-choice examinations
- Intermediate tests
- Presentations
- Active participation (e.g., discussions)
- Lab work
- Submissions (e.g., essays, final papers)
- Excursions, trips, museum visits, etc.

####

title: Alternative mode of assessment
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Procedure for claiming an alternative mode of assessment
heading_paths: Procedure for claiming an alternative mode of assessment
Procedure for claiming an alternative mode of assessment
Option 1: Direct communication with the lecturer or examiner
text:
Exams can be modified in two ways to meet your needs based on your impairment:

- O[ption 1](#c639477): Direct communication and an agreement with the lecturer/examiner
- O[ption 2](#c639478): Submitting an application to the directorate of studies (SPL) [JP1] and presenting this application to the lecturer/examiner

In some cases, submitting a formal application ([option 2](#c639478)) can make more sense and/or be necessary. Please discuss your situation with your teachers/examiners and/or the Accessible Studying Team or the responsible directorate of studies. Please read the regulations by the individual directorates of studies (SPL) (links below) and check whether your SPL recommends one of the two options.

Consider which alternative mode of assessment might be most useful and effective for you to outweigh the disadvantages you experience.

- You are welcome to use the counselling offers of the Accessible Studying Team to find suitable (additional) alternative methods for your situation.

1. Discuss the necessary alternative modes of assessment – as soon as possible – with your lecturers/examiners directly and agree on the practical implementation.
2. Together, record what you agreed on in writing (e.g. via e-mail to ensure that all parties involved are well-informed and that the agreement is also transparent for others in the future).
3. If you are asked to provide evidence of your impairment because it is not obvious, please read the [information about the provision of evidence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/) (in German).

Decide if you - wish to submit the application form to the Team Barrierefrei. On the application form, the Team Barrierefrei will note the relevant impairments in point 1. Afterward, you will receive the application form back and can submit it to the SPL, who is responsible for the final decision. - wish to submit the application form, along with the evidence of impairment, directly to the responsible [SPL](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/pvz_sub.html?id=99999904). - Please note: You have to submit a separate application for each degree programme (as well as for each extension curriculum (EC) and teaching subject) to the responsible SPL (for example, separate applications for the degree programmes in Chemistry, Sport and History). 4. A specialist medical/clinical psychological certificate confirming a study-relevant impairment. This can be, for example, a document stating a diagnosis (such as ADHD, blindness). Alternatively, you can use the [diagnosis-free form](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/p_barrierefreistudieren/bf_docs/Formular_Besta__tigung_studienrelevante_Beeintraechtigungen_EN.pdf). 2. The [application form](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/p_barrierefreistudieren/bf_docs/2026_antrag_abweichende_pruefungsmethoden.pdf) for alternative modes of assessment, filled out under the sections "Personal Information" and "Point 2." Additional information regarding the required evidence can be found at the following link: [Further Information on evidence of impairment (in German).](https://backend.univie.ac.at/index.php?id=109242&L=0) #### Other types of evidence - [Information about the presentation of evidence (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/)

title: Alternative mode of assessment
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Reporting your needs in due time
Legal framework at the University of Vienna
text:
If your application is granted or if you received a “letter of recommendation” (issued until February 2020), your teachers/examiners are not automatically notified and you are not automatically registered in the relevant university system.

A granted application merely confirms your right to the alternative mode of assessment and provides a solid basis for communication. You have to independently contact your lecturers/examiners in all your courses/exams and inform them that you require an alternative mode of assessment. This is also due to reasons of data protection. Furthermore, some impairments occur in episodes or phases. Therefore, affected students might not have the same needs at all times. An automated system could not take this into account.

Therefore, contact your lecturer or examiner as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

According to section 59, para. 1, item 12 of the [2002 Universities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) (in German), degree programme students and non-degree programme students with an [impairment that directly affects their studies](/en/accessible-studies/) have the right to alternative modes of assessment.

In addition, the Statutes of the University of Vienna specify that there are [equal opportunities for people with a disability and/or a chronic illness](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/equal-opportunities-for-people-with-disabilities-andor-chronic-illnesses/).

You can find the detailed legal texts on the [Accessible University of Vienna portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/weiterfuehrend/rechtliches-und-nachweise/pruefungen/) (in German).

title: Proof of impairment
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Discontinuing exams or courses for an important reason
text:
In order to access many of the offered support serivces at the University of Vienna it is required to provide proof of impairment.

Depending on your individual needs, different offices are responsible for managing your documentation. The type of proof needed may vary. Listed below you can find out which kind of documentation you need in order to access different services:

Alternative modes of assessment involve adjustments to exams/courses to compensate for disadvantages students with impairments/illnesses may face.

To apply for alternative modes of assessment, you will need:

- **a medical/clinical psychological certificate**
- or the “**[Form without Diagnosis](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/p_barrierefreistudieren/bf_docs/Formular_Besta__tigung_studienrelevante_Beeintraechtigungen_EN.pdf)**” which must be filled out by a medical provider
- a "[Disability Identity Card](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/en/themen/menschen_mit_behinderungen/behindertenpass.html)" with up to date information about a the relevant impairment

Students that experience acute health problems or happen to be in an accident right before or during an exam or course with compulsory attendance can be signed out without any consequences.Your lecturer/examiner may ask you for documentation to validate the existence of a justified reason for signing you out of a course/exam which is already in progress.

In order to do so you can provide either:

- a  **medical/clinical psychological certificate**
- or a relevant doctor's note by your primary care provider

title: Proof of impairment
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Students can, if they have an appropriate reason, apply for remission of the tuition fee during the respective period. In order to do so, you will need one of the following froms of documentation:

- either a confirmation by a medical specialist
- or an up to date Disability Identity Card

Please click on the following link for further information: [Remission of the tuition fee](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/tuition-fee/remission/)

title: Individual study support (ISU)
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: What is the goal of ISU?
text:
ISU is a support service offered by the University of Vienna for students. The following services are currently available:

- [ISU peer counselling](/en/accessible-studies/individual-study-support-isu/isu-peer-counselling/) (counselling in September 2025 at the start of the academic year)
- [ISU learning café](/en/accessible-studies/individual-study-support-isu/the-isu-study-cafe/)

The main goal of individual study support is to enable students to study with (more) equal opportunities. Our services are inclusive. They are also aimed at students with disabilities and at strengthening social interaction and a sense of community among students at the University of Vienna.

ISU-Sujet

title: ISU Peer Counselling: Support for your everyday student life
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
ISU Peer Counselling offers individual counselling sessions to make your everyday student life easier. Peer counselling means that you will be counselled by students from the Master's programme in Psychology. Your colleagues have been trained for this format as part of a [course](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/course.html?lv=200234&semester=2026S).

This inclusive peer support program is for students at the University of Vienna - especially those just starting their studies - who

- have difficulty concentrating or require special attention in their learning environment.
- have difficulty structuring their course material or the requirements of their courses.
- need support in organising and planning their studies.
- suffer from exam anxiety and are looking for strategies to cope with it.

Students with disabilities and neurodivergent students are very welcome. Our counsellors are sensitive to your concerns. No proof is required.

- Semester planning
- Study organisation
- Optimisation of personal time management
- Creation of a study plan and structuring of study material
- Writing emails to lecturers or university staff
- Discussion and joint development of possible strategies for exam anxiety
- Joint strategy development for motivation difficulties

text:
Consultation appointments will be held during the summer semester, from May 11 through the end of June. You can book an appointment starting now.
There will be additional dates in September; exact times to be announced.

A peer counseling session lasts 50 minutes and takes place on-site in the main building on Universitätsring 1 (also available online upon request). No sessions are held on holidays or days when classes are not in session.

title: The ISU study café
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: What is the ISU study café?
heading_paths: What is the ISU study café?
text:
The ISU Learning Café of the Individual Study Support (ISU) serves to support and network students at the University of Vienna. The central idea is to create a space for the most efficient, community-orientated learning possible.

Specifically, the presence of others is to be utilised in the sense of body doubling. The concept of body doubling has proven to be an effective method for increasing concentration and productivity while learning through the presence of others.

Hybrid sessions aim to offer the same benefits in a virtual format, allowing you to participate from anywhere and benefit from the collective learning atmosphere.

title: The ISU study café
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: What can you expect in the ISU-Study-Café?
heading_paths: What can you expect in the ISU-Study-Café?
text:
- Study  Space – on site and online
  - **On site:** Our traditional learning café remains a central meeting point for students who want to come together in a physical space. Here you will benefit from direct interaction with study guides and fellow students.
    - Mondays 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM in the main building.
    - Tuesdays 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM in the main building.
    - Thursdays 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM in the main building.
  - Hybrid:
    This semester, we are offering you an even more diverse range of options with a new space at Althanstraße 8B and a new setting. Participation in the hybrid learning café is possible both on-site and digitally. For students who need flexibility or are unable to attend in person, we are opening up our virtual learning space. This allows you to immerse yourself in a productive, structured and collaborative learning environment regardless of your location.
    - Wednesdays 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM at Althanstraße 8B and online.
- Regularity and time structure: Fixed study times and planned breaks help you to develop and maintain a study rhythm.
- Shared breaks: Short, shared breaks with other students not only promote social interaction, but also relaxation and motivation.
- Learning support: Our experienced learning mentors are on hand to provide you with advice and support regarding learning planning and study organisation.

title: The ISU study café
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
The project is especially aimed at students of the University of Vienna who have motivation, concentration and/or attention difficulties.

You can register for the dates via the freely accessible ["ISU -Study-Café"-Moodle course](https://moodle.univie.ac.at/course/view.php?id=442689)!
You can register for the Moodle course yourself at any time.

- On site:
  Max. Number of participants: 14 people per date (first come first serve!)
  In the Moodle course you can see how many places are still available per date.
  The dates are always released by the 20th of the previous month.
- Online:
  Unlimited number of participants; via BigBlueButton.

Contact via: [peerpoint.ctl@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,rggtrqkpv0evnBwpkxkg0ce0cv');)
Please enter “ISU Learning Café” in the subject line.

title: Incoming students with disabilities
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
# Incoming students with disabilities

Incoming students (e.g. on an ERASMUS+ programme) with disabilities can find information in the FAQ listed below and get counselling provided by the "Accessible Studying team (Team Barrierefrei)".

The disability advisory service of the University of Vienna is called [Team Barrierefrei](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/zustaendigkeiten/anlaufstellen-intern/team-barrierefrei/) (Accessible Studying team). The Accessible Studying team offers information and support concerning barrier-free and accessible studying. They provide assistance with all kinds of questions concerning studying with disabilities, impairments and/or chronic illnesses.

- [Team members](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/zustaendigkeiten/anlaufstellen-intern/team-barrierefrei/)
- Counselling hours
- [E-mail](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');)

Members of the "Accessible Studying team" offer counselling in person, via telephone, via chat- and via e-mail during the opening hours.

If you are interested in studying at the University of Vienna and need information about accessible studying, please contact the Accessible Studying team in advance. They can offer you support in:

- exam adaptations or alternative modes of assessment
- evidence of disability
- information and counselling
- support and technical equipment
- legal framework

title: Incoming students with disabilities
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
heading_paths: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
text: Search term / - Is there a disability advisory service? The disability advisory service of the University of Vienna is called "Accessible Studying team" (Team Barrierefrei). They offer information and support concerning barrier-free and accessible studying. They provide assistance with all kinds of questions concerning studying with disabilities, impairments and/or chronic illnesses. - [Team members](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/zustaendigkeiten/anlaufstellen-intern/team-barrierefrei/) - Counselling hours - [E-mail](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');) - Where do I find housing in Vienna? Can I live "on campus"? The Accessible Studying team is not responsible for questions concerning living in Vienna or studying in general. The University of Vienna does not offer support regarding student housing and there are no housing opportunities at university premises. Furthermore, it is important to know that there is not "one campus". The University of Vienna is located in more than 70 different buildings throughout Vienna. Depending on your degree programme, you will have courses in different locations. Students of the University of Vienna have to search for housing independently. You might find it helpful to search for student housing online (using the German keywords: “Studierendenheim” or “Studentenheim”). We recommend that you check if housing is accessible for you, even if accessibility is mentioned on websites. - For incoming Erasmus+ students, the “[Oead](https://housing.oead.at/en/)” provides information on housing. - The International Office offers information and links on [studying and living in Vienna](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/information-for-incoming-exchange-students/living-and-studying-in-vienna), including information on public transport and (accessible) accommodation. -

heading_path: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
heading_paths: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
text: if accessibility is mentioned on websites. - For incoming Erasmus+ students, the “[Oead](https://housing.oead.at/en/)” provides information on housing. - The International Office offers information and links on [studying and living in Vienna](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/information-for-incoming-exchange-students/living-and-studying-in-vienna), including information on public transport and (accessible) accommodation. - There is no medical support in university facilities. Depending on the length of your stay and exchange programme, students with disabilities or a chronic illness can apply for [remission of tuition fees](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Furthermore, there is a [scholarship for students with disabilities](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/stipendium-zur-besonderen-unterstuetzung-studierender-mit-beeintraechtigung/) and financial support for mobility and orientation trainings at the University for blind and visually impaired students. Erasmus+ students can apply for [financial aid](https://ec.europa.eu/programmes/erasmus-plus/opportunities/individuals/physical-mental-conditions_en). You have to submit your application before the beginning of your student exchange. - What evidence of disability/medical certificate do I need? You can submit documents in English or German language. Please check with the Accessible Studying team if you would like to submit your documents in any other language. Furthermore, certificates should focus on concrete difficulties or required support measures, rather than provide diagnoses in medical terminology. - Gibt es Unterstützung für schwerhörige oder gehörlose Studierende? title: Incoming students with disabilities
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
heading_paths: FAQ for incoming students with disabilities
text: schwerhörige oder gehörlose Studierende? [GESTU](https://www.uniability.org/studium/studienbezogene-serviceeinrichtungen/gestu-gehoerlos-studieren/)bietet Unterstützung durch Gebärdensprachdolmetscher\*innen, Schriftdolmetscher\*innen oder Unterstützung Studierender für Mitschriften. - Is personal assistance available? The University of Vienna does not offer personal assistance. For cases where state-funded personal assistance is not available or insufficient, the Accessible Studying team coordinates a project called “ISU”. As part of this project, students enrolled in a teacher education programme provide support to students with disabilities or chronic illnesses. You have to apply in advance. Please check the deadline or [contact the Accessible Studying team](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dcttkgtghtgkBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). - Is there accessible transportation around the University? The University of Vienna is not located on a university campus. Courses take place at many different university locations throughout Vienna. You might have to access different buildings and use public transport to reach them. Vienna has a well-developed public transport network. Almost all busses, trains and stations are wheelchair-accessible, but some of the trams are not. You will find tactile guidance orientation in and around many of the stations and stops. Most sidewalks are barrier-free. If you prefer cycling, Vienna is a bike-friendly city. Please note that tickets for public transport are not included in tuition fees. [Information about public transport](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) - What is the Austrian National Union of Students? You can also contact the accessibility department of the Austrian National Union of Students (“Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft – ÖH”), which is elected by students studying in Austria.

title: Health during your studies
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
An overview of all services related to balanced nutrition, physical activity and mental health – especially for students. Whether you are looking for a sports course, cafeteria tips or help during stressful periods of study, this website provides information to help you stay healthy, focused and balanced throughout your studies. The range of services will be updated continuously.

© Ian Ehm

[### The extension curriculum ‘Health literacy and empowerment for students’.](/en/degree-programmes/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/gesundheitskompetenz-und-empowerment-fuer-studierende-ec-15-ects/)

This extension curriculum helps you develop practical skills in health promotion and sustainable nutrition, physical activity and mental health, and supports you in making self-determined, health-related decisions.

[### VU Running as the key to better performance at university](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/course.html?lv=330185&semester=2026S)

To deepen evidence-based knowledge of training planning and optimal nutrition, and to gain practical experience together

The course is aimed at both beginners and experienced runners, regardless of their individual level of experience.

[### Lecture series „Health in Society“](https://gig.univie.ac.at/en/study)

As part of the lecture series, we will be presenting a talk entitled “Healthy Campus Life: Focus on student health” on 9 June 2026.

Whether you cycle to university, take a sports course at the University Sports Institute or simply take the stairs – even small activities support your well-being and health.

Discover a wide range of sports courses – from yoga to volleyball.

[Click here to discover the offers](https://musi.usi.at/)

title: Health during your studies
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: How can I strengthen my resilience?
text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

Well-being starts in the mind. Strategies to strengthen resilience and focus on studying can help.

[Tips and tricks for dealing with stressful periods of study can be found on the blog.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/facing-challenges-wie-kann-ich-meine-resilienz-staerken/)

[Decisiveness, control over your own reactions and more.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/resilienz-im-studium-strategien-fuer-mehr-handlungskompetenz/)

[Support through psychological counselling and psychotherapeutic methods.](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/)

title: Confirmations
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

title: Confirmations
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: - Confirmation of study periods In your confirmation of study periods you find an overview of all degree and non-degree programmes you are or were admitted to at the University of Vienna. It contains data regarding the beginning and end of studies, re-registered semesters and semesters on leave. The confirmation of study periods is available in German and English. - Confirmation of enrolment Your confirmation of enrolment confirms that you are enrolled for a specific degree or non-degree programme in a specific semester. If you are admitted to multiple programmes at the University of Vienna there is a separate confirmation of enrolment for each programme. Other authorities often refer to the confirmation of enrolment as "Inskriptionsbestätigung". The confirmation of enrolment is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee has been received by the University of Vienna. - Student record sheet In your student record sheet you can find an overview of all programmes you are and were admitted to. It is issued for each semester. The student record sheet contains information about the curriculum and version, beginning of studies, enrolment status (or end of studies) and possible resumptions of studies. The student record sheet is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed

title: Confirmations for (former) students
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

title: Confirmations for (former) students
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

title: Confirmations for (former) students
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Confirmations of forms of other authorities
Confirmations for the Austrian Armed Forces or Civil Service Agency
text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).
