title: Confirmations for (former) students
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Confirmations about the completion of a degree programme/academic degree
text:
The payment confirmation for the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Semester specific documents > payment request/confirmation.

A confirmation of the payment of the administration fee for [bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) is included in the registration confirmation for the entrance exam procedure. This document is available when you have applied for admission to one of these programmes.

Your examination results are included in your transcript of records and the confirmation of courses passed in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en).

If you need confirmations beyond that or if you have no access to u:space anymore please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a duplicate of your degree certificate or diploma supplement please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a confirmation of your academic degree for an external agency please contact the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,xgtkhkecvkqpuBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Exempt from this are degrees from dental or medical studies.

If you have studied dental or human medicine in the past please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/) directly. Since 1 January 2004 it is no longer part of the University of Vienna. Your data is available at the Medical University and can be verified there.

title: Confirmations for third parties
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

title: Confirmations for third parties
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Verifications of education or academic degree
heading_paths: Verifications of education or academic degree
text:
Due to data protection regulations you have to keep several things in mind for the verification of academic degrees.

The Büro Studienpräses needs a scan of the personal ID of the person (as JPEG or PDF) as well as a signed declaration of consent. This declaration has to include the following information:

- Specification of the data required (e.g. academic degree, number of achieved ECTS etc.)
- Name
- Date of birth
- Address of the office or name and address of the person concerned
- Degree programme
- Student ID number
- Purpose

Send your request for verification of academic degrees to the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](mailto:verifications@univie.ac.at). An exemption are degrees of dental and medical degree programmes.

For confirmations of degrees of dental and medical programmes please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/), even if the diploma was issued by the University of Vienna (this was the case until 2004).

text: / - Academic degree You receive an academic degree after you have successfully completed your higher education by means of a final examination and/or presentation (e.g. public defence). Subsequently, you will receive your graduation document. Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Graduation Ceremony](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30178&cHash=9ddb0bcf6c9f35c935ca67da70afc04b) - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Public defence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30846&cHash=792c91a61dadd4e25ab727e239f93133) - ["Promotion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31237&cHash=e793360b6437022540232d8d31aeb91e) - ["Sponsion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31260&cHash=e8c5fd8a99c8b76330762d7d81dabe59) - Academic year The academic year starts on 1 October and ends on 30 September. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: exam In some bachelor programmes, you have to provide proof of certain exams, such as Latin or Greek, upon admission. Admission is only possible if you have already taken these additional exams. In other bachelor or diploma programmes, you can take the additional exams during your studies. The University of Vienna offers the relevant courses preparing you to take these additional exams. Related Links: - [Additional exams](/en/admission/additional-exams/) Related entries: - [Supplementary examination (in the admission letter of bachelor/diploma programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31928&cHash=cf8842ba1574a00f9737609278349b82) - [Latin - additional exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31942&cHash=240a418eec281e7a226867181893f710) - Administration fee for entrance exam procedures An administraion fee of 50 euros is charged for bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedures as a regulatory measure. This ensures that only people who are actually interested in the degree programme apply for admission. Pay the fee by credit card or eps ttransfer when you apply for admission to the degree programme. A reimbursement is not possible. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: to a bachelor, diploma or master programme via u:space, your application will be processed by the Admission Office. Your documents will be checked by various units at the University of Vienna. If you fulfil all requirements, you will receive an admission letter to the e-mail address provided in u:space or as a download in u:space. The admission letter will inform you about any supplementary examinations or whether you can be admitted without further supplementary examinations. Persons with citizenships and and a secondary-school leaving certificate or university degree from EU/EEA/CH, who submit an application for a bachelor, diploma or a consecutive master programme, do not receive an admission letter. Related entries: - [Official notice of rejection ("Abweisungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30175&cHash=bd73fa15486562aa72b70b91100f6b68) - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30804&cHash=de9edcdfc251764f175fcd79064e1f34) - [German language proficiency/courses](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?cHash=9d72e6340e75474fb1b6a96b446b194c&tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30848) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Official notice of refusal ("Zurückweisungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31359&cHash=13d14e1c4ce86edf1aa441afde2c06f9) - [Admission Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31491&cHash=5b5c82a3dec8d510092ddf58cbe7779e) - [Supplementary examination (in the admission letter of bachelor/diploma programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31928&cHash=cf8842ba1574a00f9737609278349b82) - [Supplementary examinations (in the admission letter of master programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39934&cHash=afe5309d2001380a942b0e76da2bdf2b) - Admission Office The Admission Office is the gateway to the University of Vienna. to a bachelor, diploma or master programme via u:space, your application will be processed by the Admission Office. Your documents will be checked by various units at the University of Vienna. The admission letter will inform you about any supplementary examinations or whether you can be admitted without further supplementary examinations. Persons with citizenships and and a secondary-school leaving certificate or university degree from EU/EEA/CH, who submit an application for a bachelor, diploma or a consecutive master programme, do not receive an admission letter. Examples: Maxym finished school in Russia, can only speak German at level A2 and wants to study History. He takes care to have all required documents long before the application deadline. The authorities always take longer and his documents have to be notarised and translated. In June, as soon as the application period begins, he applies for admission. The sooner the better, because he has read that it takes time to process his application and then issue his residence permit for Austria. text: it takes time to process his application and then issue his residence permit for Austria. As he still has to do a German course, he finds out about the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) early on. The VWU has its own registration deadlines. Maxym waits for the admission letter and registers for the German course within the registration deadline of the VWU. Than he books an online appointment for the first-time admission within the admission period. He completes the admission procedure and starts the German course. Sophie has the Austrian Matura. In June, at the beginning of the application period, she applies for admission to the bachelor programme in History. She waits until the admission period begins in July and books an appointment for online first-time admission a few days beforehand. This allows her to put together her timetable in August/September, register for the courses, avoid stress shortly before the start of the semester and get off to a good start to her studies. text: Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30804&cHash=de9edcdfc251764f175fcd79064e1f34) - [Resumption of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31347&cHash=9910413b5ab4f055a5b778c7f9dcbab4) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Admission Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31491&cHash=5b5c82a3dec8d510092ddf58cbe7779e) - Admission procedure The admission procedure is the entire process from the application for admission to bachelor, diploma or master programmes to the payment ot the tuition/Students' Union fee. The steps you have to take depend, among other things, on wether - it is a degree programme with or without entrance exam procedure/selection procedure - you have already studied the progamme once before - you would like to change your degree programme or study an additional degree programme. Please note the different application and admission deadlines! The respective steps and deadlines in the admission procedure depend on your nationality, the country of issue of your secondary-school leaving certificate/degree, the level of your German language skills and the desired type of degree programme. In order to compete the admission procedure, you must pay the prescribed tuition/Students' Union fee in due time. If certain requirements are met, students can apply for this scholarship once per semester. The decision on the approval is made by the office of the Studienpräses. Related Links: - [Information on the advancement scholarship](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/foerderungsstipendien-nach-dem-studfg/) (in German) Related entries: - [Office of the Studienpräses](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30836&cHash=c3a56d28cd36231aea5c9162266cf396) - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Allocation of available places For courses with continuous assessment, only a limited number of places are available to students. Therefore, you have to register for these courses in u:space in due time. After the end of the registration period, the places are allocated. Registrations are organised according to the specifications of the curricula (e.g. points system, preference system or recommended path of study). Places will be allocated accordingly. [First-time admission](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30866&cHash=d40aa40f71c9ead7df1d946461e81e3d) - [Master Access Guide](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31176&cHash=a610798eae6fb6582ff75b0860eff1a1) - [Resumption of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31347&cHash=9910413b5ab4f055a5b778c7f9dcbab4) - [Admission letter (official notice of admission, "Zulassungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31353&cHash=df3d01a947ecdf5301df27c00a744fab) - [Admission period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31355&cHash=d36d029e558b0a11f1437ca8ae35078b) - [Official notice of refusal ("Zurückweisungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31359&cHash=13d14e1c4ce86edf1aa441afde2c06f9) - [Admission Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31491&cHash=5b5c82a3dec8d510092ddf58cbe7779e) - [Matriculation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31934&cHash=3068741d97523f94ab73acef74cbbccc) - Advancement scholarship You can apply for an advancement scholarship for the writing of a scientific thesis (master's, diploma or doctoral thesis). If certain requirements are met, students can apply for this scholarship once per semester. The decision on the approval is made by the office of the Studienpräses. Related Links: - [Information on the advancement scholarship](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/foerderungsstipendien-nach-dem-studfg/) (in German) Related entries: - [Office of the Studienpräses](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30836&cHash=c3a56d28cd36231aea5c9162266cf396) - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Allocation of available places For courses with continuous assessment, only a limited number of places are available to students. Therefore, you have to register for these courses in u:space in due time. After the end of the registration period, the places are allocated. Registrations are organised according to the specifications of the curricula (e.g. points system, preference system or recommended path of study). Places will be allocated accordingly. Related Links: - [Registering for courses and examinations](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) Related entries: - [Registration status in u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30191&cHash=71de51db4a7852d1f6b8c1de1a4de491) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31232&cHash=71ef139afe33c7e9cb44ca0bdf597ad4) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - [Point allocation system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31249&cHash=7ee6d54f9ca9814dd47249f0c7aa9d9d) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - [Allocation of places in courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31363&cHash=0620ca3cce0949ea363afb2149c1b262) - [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32965&cHash=f6d48ac305556ae51a0a3869bf9a2e10) -

breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31232&cHash=71ef139afe33c7e9cb44ca0bdf597ad4) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - [Point allocation system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31249&cHash=7ee6d54f9ca9814dd47249f0c7aa9d9d) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - [Allocation of places in courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31363&cHash=0620ca3cce0949ea363afb2149c1b262) - [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32965&cHash=f6d48ac305556ae51a0a3869bf9a2e10) - Allocation of places in courses and exams After the registration deadline, you can see whether or not you have been allocated to a place on a course/exam in u:space in the section > Studies > Registration Overview. Each directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) determines how places for courses with a limited number of participants and exams are distributed in its degree programmes. The allocation of places will take place either according to the point allocation system, the preference system or the study achievement system. The status of your registration in u:space informs you whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (Auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If students deregister from a course, you possibly move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/login?p_p_id=ssploginportlet_WAR_ssploginportlet&p_p_lifecycle=0&_ssploginportlet_WAR_ssploginportlet_redirect=%2Fweb%2Fstudium%2Fanmeldeuebersicht) Related entries: - [Registration status in u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30191&cHash=71de51db4a7852d1f6b8c1de1a4de491) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Course with continuous

As part of alternative extensions, you do not take a prescribed EC, but instead look for other courses or examinations that can be assigned to your own programme. Which achievements can be recognised? Courses and examinations from... 1. the range offered by your own degree programme at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 2. other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 3. other degree programmes at other recognised domestic post-secondary educational institutions; 4. recognised foreign post-secondary educational institutions within the framework of mobility programmes with advance notification or via the [Circle U. Open Campus.](https://www.circle-u.eu/open-campus) Other options: - Courses, examinations, and certificates at post-secondary educational institutions that focus on the certification of foreign language skills; - Austrian National Union of Students activity according to HSG, whereby 1 semester hour

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: via the [Circle U. Open Campus.](https://www.circle-u.eu/open-campus) Other options: - Courses, examinations, and certificates at post-secondary educational institutions that focus on the certification of foreign language skills; - Austrian National Union of Students activity according to HSG, whereby 1 semester hour is converted to 2 ECTS credits. Further information can be found in the Senate Decree about Alternative Extensions and on the websites of the StudiesServiceUnits (SSSt) and the directorates of studies (SPL). Related Links: - [Senate Decree about Alternative Extensions (PDF)](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2009_2010/2009_2010_173.pdf) - [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Alternative mode of assessment (due to impairment, disability or illness) If you cannot take exams in the required form due to an impairment, a disability, illness or because it might be a disadvantage to you, you can use an alternative mode of assessment. The Team Barrierefrei (Accessible Studying Team) provides relevant general information and counselling. In some cases, it sets out the conditions together with the directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) and teaching staff/examiners, who are in charge of the actual implementation. Related Links: - [Accessible Studies](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/) - Alumni Association The Alumni Association of the University of Vienna is a network which provides exchange and networking opportunities to graduates, e.g. at alumni reunions. Several different programmes such as alma (a mentoring programme students in the final phase on their way to a professional career) or u:start (education programme for self-employed and start-ups) are offered

text: and networking opportunities to graduates, e.g. at alumni reunions. Several different programmes such as alma (a mentoring programme students in the final phase on their way to a professional career) or u:start (education programme for self-employed and start-ups) are offered to young professionals. Related Links: - [Alumni Association](https://www.alumni.ac.at) (in German) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - ["Promotion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31237&cHash=e793360b6437022540232d8d31aeb91e) - ["Sponsion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31260&cHash=e8c5fd8a99c8b76330762d7d81dabe59) - Apostille An apostille is a type of legalisation for documents that can be done in the member states of the Hague Convention of Legalisation. It confirms a document’s issuing institution. The competent authority that can issue an apostille varies from state to state. Related Links: - [Legislation provisions](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c180113) Related entries: - [Legalisation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31907&cHash=ece0672df79bf5dda0ad3dd1dae3d29f) - [Full legalisation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31996&cHash=b2d27c84616f8a39b0d4f3ad8770c2f8) - Application for admission to a degree programme You have to apply for admission to teh degree programme if you want to study at the University of Vienna. The application is possible online via u:space within specific application periods. For the application for admission, certain documents are required, depending on the degree programme of your choice. Related Links: - [Admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30804&cHash=de9edcdfc251764f175fcd79064e1f34) - [Reference number (GZ)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31125&cHash=938878568ada8478939a4b2125bee0a4) - [Secondary-school leaving certificate](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31219&cHash=034fa8b22f596441976619f7a869f17d) - [Instructions on the right to appeal](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31223&cHash=45f698c9d68c0c25ea3bc4348280e842) - [u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31311&cHash=571431f2f0b27f0eec7e3fbf0fcdd580) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Translation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31986&cHash=9e2105ff1332d8df3d85f9bef6f26e99) - [Supplementary examinations (in the admission letter of master programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39934&cHash=afe5309d2001380a942b0e76da2bdf2b) - [Eligible degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39936&cHash=dfd91548de08cd065d97a7e77cf3bdd3) - Application period If you want to study

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

  Related entries:
  - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27)
  - [Academic degree](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30179&cHash=d228567a3a5b3afa103c538bf628a7aa)
  - [Diploma programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30851&cHash=67b823c84d2ebd9ac71438deaba4ff7a)
  - [ECTS credits](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30862&cHash=5a07e0ec2271e001eb09cf487f209db1)
- Board examination

  If you have not passed an exam three times, this exam must be taken orally before an examination committee for the fourth and last time. The registration is done at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS).

  Related Links:

  - [StudienServiceCenters (SSC)/StudienServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)
  - [Büro Studienpräses: Types of exams](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/pruefungen/pruefungsarten/) (in German)

  Related entries:
  - [Registration for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30193&cHash=7fbe81eeddcfae3bd66265dbbd660f93)

  - [Types of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31247&cHash=14da2163035d24929e65688b4d4ec343)
  - [Re-sitting an exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31346&cHash=cacde75698218ded9fcaf869bbc17318)

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - c.t. (cum tempore) If you find the indication "cum tempore" (c.t.) next to a course in the course directory, the course starts 15 minutes later than scheduled. These fifteen minutes are referred to as "academic quarter of an hour" or "academic quarter" (in German: „akademische Viertelstunde“ or „akademisches Viertel“). Example: Laura would like to attend the lecture „Höhere Analysis“. According to [u:find](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273318), it starts at 10:00 c.t. In order to be punctual, Laura must arrive at the lecture hall no later than 10:15. Related Links: - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Course (LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31168&cHash=d64c2055ed409809bec50d8788f88a1d) - [s.t. (sine tempore)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31264&cHash=e39e894b27ec93846814c05fe533dbdf) - [u:find](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31303&cHash=01a70d2a519ee232e089a410de849d5e) - Campus In 1988, the University of Vienna took over the grounds of the old Vienna General Hospital (AKH). Besides numerous institutions, a centre of lecture halls, shops and restaurants are located on the Campus. Related Links: - [Campus of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://campus.univie.ac.at/) - CEEPUS CEEPUS is a Central European mobility programme and the abbreviation of Central European Exchange Programme for University Studies. The CEEPUS network enables students from certain European countries to study at the University of Vienna. It is also in charge of

/ - c.t. (cum tempore) If you find the indication "cum tempore" (c.t.) next to a course in the course directory, the course starts 15 minutes later than scheduled. These fifteen minutes are referred to as "academic quarter of an hour" or "academic quarter" (in German: „akademische Viertelstunde“ or „akademisches Viertel“). Example: Laura would like to attend the lecture „Höhere Analysis“. According to [u:find](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273318), it starts at 10:00 c.t. In order to be punctual, Laura must arrive at the lecture hall no later than 10:15. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: There are courses with continuous assessment and courses with non-continuous assessment. In the first case, you have to deliver results already during the semester. Courses with non-continuous assessment end with an examination at the end of the semester resp. in the following semester. Examples of course types: lectures ("Vorlesungen", VO), seminars ("Seminare", SE) or exercises ("Übungen", UE). Related Links: - [u:find – Course directory](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/vvz.html) Related entries: - [c.t. (cum tempore)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29988&cHash=d3f291088c28f3236a034bf0f74c89e8) - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - [Discussion Course (KO)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31162&cHash=ef89e051a7ef0e9b8edce3220d09b3b9) - [Start of exams/courses](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31242&cHash=36078ee9db0dfadeb8f853f306425089) - [s.t. (sine tempore)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31264&cHash=e39e894b27ec93846814c05fe533dbdf) - Course directory Related entries: - [u:find](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31303&cHash=01a70d2a519ee232e089a410de849d5e) - Course exam A course exam is an exam on the contents of a specific [course](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c279844). Grading is based exclusively on the results of the exam. The attendance or active participation in the course will not be assessed. Course exams are offered mostly in lectures and can be in oral, written or combined form. Four exam dates are offered for each course. Related Links: - [u:find – Course directory](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Types of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31247&cHash=14da2163035d24929e65688b4d4ec343) - Course with continuous assessment (pi LV) Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, exercises), you have to complete oral and/or written partial achievements. Your final grade is calculated on the basis of these partial achievements. Information about the partial achievements that will be assessed is provided on u:find and during the respective course.

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: exercises), you have to complete oral and/or written partial achievements. Your final grade is calculated on the basis of these partial achievements. Information about the partial achievements that will be assessed is provided on u:find and during the respective course. Each directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) determines how places for courses with a limited number of participants and exams are distributed in its degree programmes. The allocation of places will take place either according to the point allocation system, the preference system or the recommended path of study. The status of your registration in u:space informs you whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (Auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered. Students on the waiting list will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places. Examples: Marion attends a seminar (SE) in History. Her presentation, seminar paper and contributions to the discussion of her colleagues’ presentations will be assessed. Max attends an exercise (UE) in Biology. During the exercise,

text: - Curriculum item Usually, degree programmes are divided into modules and further into curriculum items. One or several courses may be offered for one curriculum item. Some courses might also be assigned to several curriculum items. In your record of examinations in u:space you can find the structure of your degree programme and the corresponding curriculum items. When registering for a course/exam, you have to specify the curriculum item for which you would like to use it. text: / - Dean/Dean's Office Together with the vice dean, the dean presides over a faculty or department. The dean’s office is the central contact point for all administrative and organisational issues of the respective faculty. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna, two diploma programmes remain: Law and Catholic Theology. These comprise between eight and ten semesters. Diploma programmes are divided into several sequential stages, which must be passed one after the other. The language of instruction of these degree programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Graduates are awarded the academic degree "Mag.iur." or "Mag.theol.". Related Link: - [Range of studies: bachelor and diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/) Related entries: - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Stage of the degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31266&cHash=a31be547fd42def4414091d3fe87e1d1) - Diploma Supplement Together with the degree certificate graduates receive a Diploma Supplement. The bilingual (German/English) Diploma Supplement aims at increasing the transparency and recognition of higher education programmes. It includes details on the kind and level of qualification as well as information about the completed degree programme (requirements, course of study, grading scale) and the higher education system in Austria. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Certificate](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31351&cHash=c3e3d659836541090973c22a7a942111) - Directorate of studies (Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) The directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) of your degree programme fulfils administrative and legal tasks. It is responsible for providing students with legal information and consultation and for organising the teaching programme. Related Links: - [Directorate of studies (SPL)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Discontinuing an exam/course with continuous assessment If you must prematurely discontinue an exam or a course with continuous assessment for a valid reason (e.g. sickness), please contact your examiner/course instructor immediately. If you have a valid reason, you will not be graded. If your examiner/course instructor cannot accept your reason because your reason is not clear, you can apply for recognition of your reason at the Office of the Studienpräses within 14 days after discontinuing the exam/course with continuous assessment. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Please note: If you prematurely discontinue an exam or course with continuous assessment (pi LV) without a valid reason, your study achievements up to the discontinuation will be graded. Related Links: - [Accessible Studies (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/) - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Unauthorised absence from a course with continuous assessment/exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31313&cHash=c248aea8f5dc38f00ab9fb6e422e59cd) - [Use of unauthorised aids ("cheating"/plagiarism)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31333&cHash=570c7de0d629f82638db9de24984fb9c) - Discussion Course (KO) The discussion course ("Konversatorium", KO) belongs to the types of courses.

entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Certificate](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31351&cHash=c3e3d659836541090973c22a7a942111) - Directorate of studies (Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) The directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) of your degree programme fulfils administrative and legal tasks. It is responsible for providing students with legal information and consultation and for organising the teaching programme. Related Links: - [Directorate of studies (SPL)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Discontinuing an exam/course with continuous assessment If you must prematurely discontinue an exam or a course with continuous assessment for a valid reason (e.g. sickness), please contact your examiner/course instructor immediately. If you have a valid reason, you will not be graded. If your examiner/course instructor cannot accept your reason because your reason is not clear, you can apply for recognition of your reason at the Office of the Studienpräses within 14 days after discontinuing the exam/course with continuous assessment. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Please note: If you prematurely discontinue an exam or course with continuous assessment (pi LV) without a valid reason, your study achievements up to the discontinuation will be graded. Related Links: - [Accessible Studies (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/) - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Unauthorised absence from a course with continuous assessment/exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31313&cHash=c248aea8f5dc38f00ab9fb6e422e59cd) - [Use of unauthorised aids ("cheating"/plagiarism)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31333&cHash=570c7de0d629f82638db9de24984fb9c) - Discussion Course (KO) The discussion course ("Konversatorium", KO) belongs to the types of courses.

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Unauthorised absence from a course with continuous assessment/exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31313&cHash=c248aea8f5dc38f00ab9fb6e422e59cd) - [Use of unauthorised aids ("cheating"/plagiarism)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31333&cHash=570c7de0d629f82638db9de24984fb9c) - Discussion Course (KO) The discussion course ("Konversatorium", KO) belongs to the types of courses. It usually is a course with continuous assessment, which means that attendance is compulsory and the number of available places is limited. The charasteristic of a discussion course is that an intense exchange between students and course instructors shall be stimulated, for example by means of discussions. Related Links: - [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en) Related entries: - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - [Course (LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31168&cHash=d64c2055ed409809bec50d8788f88a1d) - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Distribution of remaining places (courses) Related entries: - [Distribution of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31221&cHash=e3836119665b8be9d92bc830b88e4804) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - Service Unit: Teaching Affairs and Study Services (DLE SLW) The Teaching Affairs and Student Services unit consists of three divisions (Coordination of Student Services, Center for Teaching and Learning [CTL] and Admission Office). The Teaching Affairs and Student Services unit (SLW) supports teachers and employees of the University of Vienna in the administration of studies and teaching and provides prospective students with information about the range of degree programmes, the admission procedure, the tuition fee/Student's Union fee, etc. The SLW service unit is also responsible for the admission to degree programmes. Related Links: -

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - ECTS credits The "European Credit Transfer System (ECTS)" contributes to the mobility between degree programmes, universities and countries, as ECTS credits facilitate a comparison of academic achievements. ECTS credits describe the average workload required to achieve the objectives of a course, module or degree programme. One ECTS credit corresponds to an average workload of 25 full hours. Course participation as well as the preparation and follow-up of a course, the duration of the exam and studying time are considered as part of the workload. In u:find you can see how many ECTS credits you have to obtain to successfully complete a course. Example: Max attends a seminar comprising 4 ECTS credits (= 100 hours). The seminar takes place ten times and takes 1.5 hours each time (= 15 hours). He has to prepare a presentation and a summary (which takes him 30 hours), a seminar paper (30 hours) and he has to read texts for every second session (5 hours each). Over the course of the semester, he will be working 100 hours for the seminar. Related Links: - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Prescribed duration of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. text: of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. Both can be found in the Study overview on u:space and on the student record sheet. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) Related entries: - [u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31311&cHash=571431f2f0b27f0eec7e3fbf0fcdd580) - Educational leave An educational leave is a time off from work to complete education and continue education and training. During an educational leave, the Austrian Public Employment Service (AMS) pays a continuing education allowance under certain prerequisites. Please obtain information directly from the AMS. As part of educational leave, you can study in a non-degree programme. Please consider the consequences of a non-degree programme if you have a secondary-school leaving certificate. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [Austrian Public Employment Service](https://www.ams.at/arbeitsuchende/topicliste/bildungskarenz-oesterreich) (in German) Related entries: - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - Eligible degree programme The admission to a master programme is only possible if you have previously completed an eligible degree programme. After having applied for admission to the degree programme, the Admission Office will evaluate the eligibility of your previous degree. If it is found to be eligible, but some exams (of max. 30 ECTS) are missing, supplementray exams are prescribed in your admission letter. The Master Access Guide illustrates which degree programmes enable you to continue with a certain master programme. Related Links: - Master Access Guide Related

These programmes have their own application deadlines (always in spring, for the following academic year) and the number of study places is limited. As part of the application process, you must complete an online self-assessment (OSA), except for Psychology. You also have to pay an administration fee to apply for admission. You are then registered for the entrance exam procedure and can take part in the written test. You can complete the admission procedure if you were successful in the entrance exam procedure and fulfil all admission requirements. They are then subject to the same tuition fee regulations as EU/EEA citizens. Related Links: - [Equal status with EU/EEA citizens with regard to the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) Related entries: - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Residence permit ("visa")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31902&cHash=7ce64213ce44f86321e3da3c2d4faf36) - [Remission of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31930&cHash=ec520748e744f3ee49dc41584e03d431) - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Erasmus+ Erasmus+ is a mobility programme enabling students within Europe to complete a part of their degree programme abroad. An extension curriculum is a predefined module group comprising up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits for extension curricula is defined in your degree programme’s curriculum. Related Links: - [Service unit International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/waehrend-des-studiums-ins-ausland) Related entries: - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - Exchange programme Related entries: - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - Exercise (course type) Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Extension curricula ("Erweiterungscurricula", EC) The majority of bachelor’s programmes (and diploma programmes) require you to complete not only compulsory and elective courses within your own degree programme, but also extension curricula (EC). An extension curriculum is a predefined module group comprising up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits for extension curricula is defined in your degree programme’s curriculum. Almost

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: your own degree programme, but also extension curricula (EC). An extension curriculum is a predefined module group comprising up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits for extension curricula is defined in your degree programme’s curriculum. Almost all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless specified otherwise in your degree programme’s curriculum, you can, to a great extent, freely choose from the entire range of extension curricula. Example: Max is studying on the bachelor’s programme in Sociology, for which he has to complete extension curricula comprising 30 ECTS credits. The curriculum does not specify which extension curricula he has to attend, but he can not choose EC from his degree programme. Related Links: - [List of extension curricula (EC)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/)

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Faculty Scientific investigation and teaching activities are carried out at the different faculties and centres. Altogether, the University of Vienna is subdivided into 15 faculties with different locations. Each faculty is presided by a dean. Related Links: - [University of Vienna: Faculties and centres](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/organisation-and-structure#c1694) Related entries: - [Dean/Dean's Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30847&cHash=d1e9a958e9802fa9cad9c2da71047a73) - Field trip (course type) Field trip are research-related courses with continuous assessment during which students go on a research excursion. Field trips serve the purpose of raising and enhancing subject-specific knowledge. Usually, participants have to write a field report afterwards. Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Financial Aid Students can receive financial aid, such as family allowance or federal aid for students, if they meet certain requirements. Further information is provided by the Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH), the tax office and the Austrian Study Grant Authority. Related Links: - [Social services department of the ÖH](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/offices/social-affairs) - [Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/english) - [Family allowance](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/de/lebenslagen/Ich-bin-f%C3%BCr-die-Erziehung-eines-Kindes-verantwortlich/Finanzielle-Unterst%C3%BCtzung-mit-Kindern) (in German) - First-time admission First-time admission is the admission procedure for persons who have never studied at the University of Vienna before. Apply for admission to the degree programme online within the respective application deadline. Then complete all further steps in the admission procedure. Entrance examination/selection procedures take place for some degree programmes. Successful participation is one of the requirements for admission to these programmes. Related Links: -

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - General university entrance qualification You can only be admitted to a degree programme if you provide proof of the general university entrance qualification. Eligible proofs: Secondary-school leaving certificate, completion of a degree programme with a duration of at least three years at a recognised post-secondary educational institution (e.g. a university). Related Links: - [Admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung (university entrance qualification examination)](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [First-time admission](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30866&cHash=d40aa40f71c9ead7df1d946461e81e3d) - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31976&cHash=b71951a4fb2bb2fc18957130a051ca32) - German language proficiency/courses When applying for admission to all bachelor/diploma and German-language master degree programmes, you need recognised proof of your German language skills at level A2. In order to achieve the C1 level required for admission to a degree programme, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU). The Language Center of the University of Vienna is one of the cooperation partners of the VWU. Registration for the courses at the VWU and its co-operation partners is only possible with a letter of admission. In this letter, you will be required to take the supplementary German examination if you have not yet provided proof of C1 level at the time of application. You may also be required to take other supplementary subject-specific examinations. You must pass these before you can be admitted to the degree programme Related Links: - [Studying in the University Preperation Programme of the Vienna

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- Habilitation

  Habilitation is the authorisation to teach in a certain field of higher education (*venia docendi*). The positive completion of a doctoral/PhD programme in the relevant scientific discipline is one of the habilitation requirements. Several years of scientific teaching activity at higher education institutions as well as the writing of a habilitation thesis are preceded by the habilitation.

  Related Links:

  - [Statutes of the University of Vienna: habilitation](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/habilitation/)

  Related entries:
  - [Professor](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31235&cHash=891d0ecefd432706567b8d76900eb112)
  - [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31253&cHash=b714e0e2eec6eceec5b3fcfc992941d7)
- Helpdesk of the Vienna University Computer Center (ZID)

  The ZID Helpdesk provides information about the ZID services and supports members of the University of Vienna in solving university-specific IT problems. It helps users manage their u:account and password. Users can report technical issues and the Helpdesk forwards this information in a targeted manner.

  Related Links:

  - [Vienna University Computer Center (ZID)](https://zid.univie.ac.at/)
  - [ZID Helpdesk](https://zid.univie.ac.at/zid-helpdesk/)

  Related entries:
  - [Vienna University Computer Center (Zentraler Informatikdienst, ZID)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31349&cHash=c021a96532eb98f6dab4b369d4163412)
- Hours per week per semester (SWS)

  The hours per week per semester ("Semesterwochenstunden", SWS) indicate the value of a course. One hour per week per semester equals 45 minutes.

  Example: Maria attends a lecture in Law. This lecture takes place every week on Tuesday from 9:00 to 10:30 (2x45 minutes) during the entire semester. Therefore, the lecture corresponds to two hours per week per semester (2 SWS).

The instructions on the right to appeal at the end of an official notification include information on the possibilty, form and deadline for lodging an appeal against an official notification. Related entries: - [Application for admission to a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30197&cHash=d1463d320886c313223dcda9a42bda02) - Insurance A part of the Students’ Union fee you pay covers your casualty and personal liability protection within the buildings/university grounds of the University of Vienna. The insurance enters into force as soon as the Students’ Union fee is registered as paid. This insurance is no health insurance. Students must apply for health insurance at the health insurance fund of their choice. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students - insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/service/insurance) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - International Office The International Office informs students and employees of the University of Vienna about mobility programmes and enables them to participate in them. Together with faculties, the International Office works on the development of internationalization programs for students, academics and general university staff. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: Legalisation Documents required for admission must be legalised if they were issued in a non-EU/EEA country. Depending on the country of issuance, two types of legalisation are possible: an apostille or a full legalisation. Related Links: - [Information regarding the apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) - [Information about full legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) Related entries: - [Apostille](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31900&cHash=b73f3e20226f1cd8a29d5f74338e38cf) - [Full legalisation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31996&cHash=b2d27c84616f8a39b0d4f3ad8770c2f8) - List of subjects If your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country, you need a list of subjects to apply for admission to a bachelor/diploma programme. In many countries, the list of subjects is part of the school-leaving certificate. It shows how many hours of a subject you have completed in the course of your schooling. Related links: - [List of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) - Livestreaming Teachers who hold courses in u:stream lecture halls can offer their courses as a live stream. There are two types of live streams: a web stream or a room2room stream. Audio and video recordings can be made available to the students on the e-learning platform (=Moodle). Related Links: - [Recording courses – u:stream](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/ustream/) - [E-learning platform Moodle](https://moodle.univie.ac.at) Related entries: - [Moodle](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31195&cHash=f09a4dd9dabecf19aab1edecd36ac050)

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - "Matura" "Matura" is the name of the Austrian secondary school-leaving certificate. A secondary-school leaving certficate is one of the requirements for admission to a bachelor oder diploma programme. Related entries: - [Secondary-school leaving certificate](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31219&cHash=034fa8b22f596441976619f7a869f17d) - Master Access Guide For admission to a master programme, you need to have completed a bachelor or diploma programme in the same or an eligible discipline. The curriculum for the respective master programme specifies the degree programmes with which you are certainly admitted to the master programme. For some degree programmes, there is information about previous studies that is not mentioned in the curriculum. You can find this information in the Master Access Guide (MAG). For all degree programmes that are not listed there, an individual examination of the application for admission is necessary. Only after the documents have been examined, the University decides whether admission is possible or not. Related Links: - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) - [Curriculum (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) Related entries: - [Master programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31178&cHash=aee74ae5c32f863df6ded59c05feca62) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Eligible degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39936&cHash=dfd91548de08cd065d97a7e77cf3bdd3) - Master programme A master programme builds on an eligible previous degree programme. One of the requirements for admission is the completion of at least three years of study in Austria or abroad. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: completion of at least three years of study in Austria or abroad. For some master programmes, you can find out whether your previous studies are eligible in the Master Access Guide. text: / - Negative assessment Exam In the event of a negative grade, you will be able to re-sit an exam three times, which means that you can take an exam four times in total. The fourth attempt takes place orally before an examination committee. Please register to an examination in front of an examination committee at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. Course with continuous assessment In the event of a negative grade, you will have to repeat the entire course with continuous assessment. You can repeat a course offered by a different course instructor if it serves the same purpose with regard to your curriculum. The course can be repeated three times (four attempts in total). If the fourth attempt was not successful, you will be deregistered from this degree programme at the University of Vienna (and any other degree programmes for which this course is required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in mode of assessment that was not announced). You cannot appeal against the content-related assessment as this is at the examiner's discretion. In the event of serious irregularities at the time of the exam, you can apply for a deletion of your grade/attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within four weeks after grading. The plausibility of the serious irregularities has to be demonstrated. A proof is not necessary. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - Non-degree programme A non-degree programme means that you are admitted to “attending individual courses”. In this case, you do not study any particular degree programme. Especially persons who have to complete supplementary exams as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) are admitted to a non-degree programme. As part of the university entrance qualification examination(Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without a secondary-school leaving certificate), non-degree programme students can also complete courses. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna

Related Links: - [ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA – Recognition of university degrees](https://www.aais.at/) - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/)

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Office of the Studienpräses The officer of the Studienpräses is in charge of decisions related to study law and and their correct implementation as well as for the awarding of scholarships and the nostrification of non-Austrian university degrees. Often, he/she is supported by the directorates of study (Studienprogrammleitung). Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Advancement scholarship](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29994&cHash=0172fceaae25a607e1ee5c8b6998817a) - [Performance scholarship](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31172&cHash=985aca8980beb4dddc0407db73ca0250) - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Official notice of refusal ("Zurückweisungsbescheid") After you have submitted an application for admission to a degree programme, an official notice is issued if certain prerequisites are met (e.g. issuing country of the documents, type of degree programme, etc.). An official notice of refusal (‘Zurückweisungsbescheid’) is issued if an application is assessed negatively due to formal deficiencies (e.g. a document is missing, diplomatic legalisation is missing on a document, etc.). Persons with citizenship and secondary-school leaving certificate from EU/EEA/CH, who submit an application for a bachelor, diploma or a consecutive master programme, do not receive any official notice. Related entries: - [Official notice of rejection ("Abweisungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30175&cHash=bd73fa15486562aa72b70b91100f6b68) - [Admission letter (official notice of admission, "Zulassungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31353&cHash=df3d01a947ecdf5301df27c00a744fab) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - Official notice of rejection ("Abweisungsbescheid") An official notice of rejection (Abweisungsbescheid") is issued if an application is assessed negatively due to deficiencies in the content (e.g. the previous education is insufficient, etc.). Related entries: - [Admission letter (official notice of admission, "Zulassungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31353&cHash=df3d01a947ecdf5301df27c00a744fab) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Official notice of refusal ("Zurückweisungsbescheid")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31359&cHash=13d14e1c4ce86edf1aa441afde2c06f9) - Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) An Online Self-Assessment (OSA) is a guidance when choosing a degree programme. It can help you to better assess the requirements of a degree programme by matching your interests and expectations or by checking the necessary prior knowledge. The OSA is the first step in the admission procedure for bachelor's and diploma programmes with an entrance exam procedures and aptitude test. It is not graded. It is intended to give you an insight into the subject of your degree programme. You need the OSA code to apply for admission to a degree programme via u:space. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - "Promotion" What is called "Promotion" in German refers to the conferring of an academic degree to graduates of a doctoral/PhD programme. When doctorates are coferred, the Rector or Vice-Rector, Dean and Professor conferring the degree are present. A "Promotion" can be celebrated by taking part in an academic graduation ceremony. Related Links: - [Graduation ceremonies](https://event.univie.ac.at/en/ceremonies-and-celebrations/ceremonies-celebrations/) Related entries: - [Graduation Ceremony](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30178&cHash=9ddb0bcf6c9f35c935ca67da70afc04b) - [Academic degree](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30179&cHash=d228567a3a5b3afa103c538bf628a7aa) - [Alumni Association](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30183&cHash=af7264dfafa13d56a5dbfb42a68e23d8) - [Public defence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30846&cHash=792c91a61dadd4e25ab727e239f93133) - [Dean/Dean's Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30847&cHash=d1e9a958e9802fa9cad9c2da71047a73) - [Center for Doctoral Studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30853&cHash=2e5eb64068ad87726d630410277e8177) - Payment period The payment period (= admission period, period for the continuation of studies) is the period in which you have to pay the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee every semester. Related Links: - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Performance scholarship Performance scholarships are granted by the office of the Studienpräses. Students who fulfil certain requirements can apply for this scholarship. Excellent academic achievements or a successful completion of a degree programme is, above all, among these requirements. Related Links: - [Information on the performance scholarship](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/leistungsstipendium-nach-dem-studfg/) (in German) Related entries: - [Office of the Studienpräses](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30836&cHash=c3a56d28cd36231aea5c9162266cf396) - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Plagiarism Plagiarism is the theft of intellectual property, which occurs for example when a text written by somebody else is disguised as one’s own. In many cases, quotes are not correctly cited and thus plagiarised.

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: - [Grants and scholarships](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31972&cHash=dc6c9dc2432550f2466303879cfc5f49) - Plagiarism Plagiarism is the theft of intellectual property, which occurs for example when a text written by somebody else is disguised as one’s own. In many cases, quotes are not correctly cited and thus plagiarised. Special software exists and is also used at the University of Vienna for the purpose of identifying plagiarised texts. If there is proof of plagiarism, the academic degree of the respective student may be revoked. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses - plagiarism](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/information-on-study-law/safeguarding-good-scientific-practice/plagiarism/) - Point allocation system Every directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) determines how places for courses with a limited number of participants and exams are distributed in its degree programmes. With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams. If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants. The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted. The points account is created when you register for a course for the first time. If you cannot find any points in u:space, register for
