title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted. The points account is created when you register for a course for the first time. If you cannot find any points in u:space, register for the course and you will see that a new points account has been created for the current semester. The allocation of points can still be changed within the registration deadline. You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used. The directorate of studies can add additional regulations to the point allocation system: Bonus points: If certain criteria apply (e.g. academic progress or number of ECTS credits obtained in the previous semester), students automatically receive additional points. Points of the previous semester: If you are on the waiting list of a course, you will automatically be credited the allocated points for the same course in the following semester and these will be added to your newly allocated points. You can view these extra points in the section “points of previous semester”. This only applies to allocated points. Any points that you did not allocate are lost. After the end of the registration period and the subsequent allocation of places, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-address informing you about the status of your registration. For further information on

text: you did not allocate are lost. After the end of the registration period and the subsequent allocation of places, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-address informing you about the status of your registration. For further information on the registration procedure, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme. Example: Max would like to attend a course with 25 available places. A total number of 30 students register for this course. Max allocates 200 points to this course. As 27 other students allocated between 210 and 500 points, he does not get a place. 70 students register for another course with 50 available places. Max allocates 100 points and gets a place, because several students allocated fewer points than he did. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) Related entries: - [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31232&cHash=71ef139afe33c7e9cb44ca0bdf597ad4) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - [Allocation of available places](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31956&cHash=cd316add293db7beb7397f54a0ba5b64) - [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32965&cHash=f6d48ac305556ae51a0a3869bf9a2e10) - Positive assessment Your grade will be entered within four weeks after the exam/the completion of a course or final partial achievement. You can check your grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module).

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module). With your new attempt, the previous examination result becomes void and the new grade is entered, even if the second grade is lower than the first. If no courses with continuous assessment are offered in the semester following your first attempt, you can only re-sit the exam in the semester after next at the latest, but not after you have completed the corresponding module, stage of the degree programme or degree programme. You should announce that you would like to re-sit the exam within 6 months. If you do not use this opportunity, you will not be able to repeat the exam at a later point. Related entries: - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - Postgraduate Center The University of Vienna's Postgraduate Center offers the possibility of continuing education and training by means of continuing education programmes and certificate courses. For the participation in a continuing education and training programmes, a tuition fee is charged. embed_text:
Study organisation > ABC of terminology > P

grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module). With your new attempt, the previous examination result becomes void and the new grade is entered, even if the second grade is lower than the first. If no courses with continuous assessment are offered in the semester following your first attempt, you can only re-sit the exam in the semester after next at the latest, but not after you have completed the corresponding module, stage of the degree programme or degree programme. You should announce that you would like to re-sit the exam within 6 months. If you do not use this opportunity, you will not be able to repeat the exam at a later point. With the preference system, several courses are offered for the same curriculum items. Students rank their registrations for courses according to their personal preference (from top to bottom). After the end of the registration deadline, the available places are allocated. On average, 98% of all students receive a suitable course place. After the registration deadline you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-adress informing you about the outcome of the registration. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: - [Study documents](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31288&cHash=96775fee4d128222ffe4a11102a051f9) - Prüfungsaktiv - examination activity Students who obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in their degree programme per academic year have taken a certain number of exams and are thus considered 'prüfungaktiv’. This is relevant in bachelor’s, diploma and master's programmes. It does not apply to doctoral programmes. Only ECTS credits of courses passed are considered in the calculation. ECTS credits from recognitions are not taken into account for the calculation. All degree programmes in which students take a certain number of exams are taken into account. The more students are ‘prüfungsaktiv’ in all their degree programmes, the more financial resources the University can use for research and teaching. In addition, evidence of having taking a certain number of exams can be a prerequisite for receiving financial aids, such as Austrian family allowance. Related links: - [Minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) - [Prüfungsaktiv - examination activity](/en/pruefungsaktiv-examination-activity/) Related entries: - [Minimum number of credits](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=35543&cHash=505350cd46716299d4f258eb59392d28) - Public defence The public defence is the final exam in a master's or doctoral programme. It includes the defence of your academic thesis (master's or doctoral thesis) and an oral exam in front of an examination committee of at least three persons. The regulations are specified in the curriculum of your degree programme. Immediately after you have successfully completed your public defence, you will get a confirmation of completion of the last exam required

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
/

- Qualitative admission requirements (QZB)

  Admission to a master programme is only possible if you have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme. For some master programmes, you must also fulfil so-called qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen", QZB) when submitting your application. QZB are requirement for admission. You can therefore not make up for these as part of the degree programme you are applying for (e.g. as supplementary examinations).

  QZB are a certain number of ECTS that you must have already completed in parts of your previous degree programme. In addition, individual documents may also be required from you, e.g. a description of qualifications or proof of language proficiency. You can find out whether QZB are required for your master programme either in the [curriculum of the respective degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (in German only) or in the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271).

  Related entries:
  - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31175&cHash=4eba972ee804cb8bd7bcba120d0149d6)
  - [Master programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31177&cHash=4a88be10c3b363efc2e78dc594e8873a)

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

text: the associated degree programme expires. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - [Attempts](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31239&cHash=e87c5fe84f21a0875fa06b7e65176b98) - Recognition of exams You can apply for the recognition of exams that you have taken at the University of Vienna or at a different university, in your degree programme at the University of Vienna. This also applies to internships and academic activities outside the University. The directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) of your degree programme decides on the recognition of exams. A study achievement can be recognised only once in your degree programme. It cannot be recognised more than once in the same degree programme. Please note: Before applying for recognition of exams, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme. Internal study achievements: The application for recognition of exams taken at the University of Vienna is done via u:space. Exception: If you are enrolled on two (bachelor's) teacher education programmes at the same time or one after the other, you do not have to apply for recognition of exams. For this purpose please check your record of examinations in u:space on a regular basis. External study achievements: You have to apply for the recognition of external study achievements separately. For further information,

Related Links: - [Directorate of studies (SPL)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/home) Related entries: - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06) - Recommended path of study The recommended path of study is part of your curriculum and divides it into semesters. It shows you in which semester and in which order you should ideally complete the individual modules of the curriculum, so that you can complete your degree programme as fast and efficiently as possible. Thus, the path of study serves as a guide: It indicates the best path through your degree programme based on content-related aspects and supports you in planning the individual semesters. Since the winter semester of 2020/2021, some fields of study have already been allocating places in courses with continuous assessment along the recommended path of study. Places in courses with continuous assessment in those studies are not allocated by means of a points or preference system, but on the recommended path of study. In the coming semesters, further fields of study will be released for the allocation of places by means of the study path. Related Links: - [Recommended path

In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: registrations. For courses with non-continuous assessment (e.g. lectures) the following status values may appear: - Registered (Angemeldet): You have registered for the course. - Deregistered (Abgemeldet): You have successfully deregistered from the course. For courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, exercises) the following status values may appear: - Prerequisite currently not met (Voraussetzung noch nicht erfüllt): You have started but not completed your registration or you do not meet the course prerequisites yet. If you meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline, you will automatically be considered for the allocation. - **Allocation by registration deadline (Zuteilung nach Anmeldefrist)**:If you have registered for a course with a limited number of participants, you are in consideration for the course first. Once the places have been allocated (after the end of the registration deadline), it will be decided whether you gain a place on the course. - Registered (Angemeldet): You have gained a place on the course. In case you do not want to attend, you have to deregister in due time. - On waiting list (Auf Warteliste): You have not been assigned a place on the course. If other students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place

registrations. For courses with non-continuous assessment (e.g. lectures) the following status values may appear: - Registered (Angemeldet): You have registered for the course. - Deregistered (Abgemeldet): You have successfully deregistered from the course. For courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, exercises) the following status values may appear: - Prerequisite currently not met (Voraussetzung noch nicht erfüllt): You have started but not completed your registration or you do not meet the course prerequisites yet. If you meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline, you will automatically be considered for the allocation. - **Allocation by registration deadline (Zuteilung nach Anmeldefrist)**:If you have registered for a course with a limited number of participants, you are in consideration for the course first. Once the places have been allocated (after the end of the registration deadline), it will be decided whether you gain a place on the course. - Registered (Angemeldet): You have gained a place on the course. In case you do not want to attend, you have to deregister in due time. - On waiting list (Auf Warteliste): You have not been assigned a place on the course. If other students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. If your status is "Prerequisite currently not met" (Voraussetzung noch nicht erfüllt) you have to meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline to get registered for the course. For courses with continuous assessment the allocation process begins after the registration period has ended. Registrations are ranked according to the stipulations in the curricula (e.g. point system/preference system/study achievement system) and places allocated. Afterwards, you will be

text: the course. For courses with continuous assessment the allocation process begins after the registration period has ended. Registrations are ranked according to the stipulations in the curricula (e.g. point system/preference system/study achievement system) and places allocated. Afterwards, you will be informed about whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If students deregister from a course, you possibly move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. You can find detailed information about the registration procedure at the website of the course and exam registration system. For further information about the registration for courses/exams, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme. If you have already studied at the University of Vienna and would like to start another degree programme or a master/doctoral programme, please submit an application for admission to the degree programme via u:space. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: refers to an admission to two degree programmes at the same or different universities. Students have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee only once per semester, even if they are admitted to multiple degree programmes in Austria. You are not allowed to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. Related entries: - [Studying at several universities](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30185&cHash=364e3731ebe62063ff39ad8fcc0a8f18) - [Additional degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31361&cHash=e12f215c7a0b1ef3c7839058fc004954) - [Second degree programme ("Zweitstudium")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31365&cHash=03b0043a8f69fcd61c5246f8bb5a2def) - Second degree programme ("Zweitstudium") In addition to your selected degree programme, you may opt for a second degree programme at a different Austrian university or university of applied sciences. You decide for yourself how intensively you pursue your second degree programme. Related entries: - [Studying at several universities](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30185&cHash=364e3731ebe62063ff39ad8fcc0a8f18) - [Second degree programme ("Doppelstudium")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30854&cHash=0d3f1e663fb676ce42fc0d77a37af3c1) - Secondary-school leaving certificate The secondary-school leaving certificate is a proof of general university entrance qualification. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: The University of Vienna conducts selection procedures for some master programmes. This mainly applies to English-language programmes. For some master programmes, you must provide proof of qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen") and/or upload a letter of motivation when applying for admission. These programmes have their own application deadlines (always in spring, for the following academic year). Related links: - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30198&cHash=83dcf3f2a3a1fe41d6eef2f91f47ba7c) - Semester abroad As a degree student you can pass one or more semesters abroad at a different university (Outgoing Mobility). The University of Vienna cooperates with numerous partner universities abroad. There are also several mobility programmes offered for incoming students. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [CEEPUS](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29987&cHash=72c1e3c31b64c2842340511847949735) - [Free Mover](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29995&cHash=5488b37118f3efe558bb850a9466ab5d) - [Recognition of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30187&cHash=7ad03787bb35116164cf00dbe2cdd76a) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - [Visiting Master](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31337&cHash=70e303dffac1661c3aafc82b37c76b70) - Seminar (course type) Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Senate The Senate consists of 18 members. Together with the Rectorate and the University Board, it forms the governing body of the University of Vienna. Every three years, the Senate is elected by the academic and non-academic university staff. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: Related Links: - [Senate of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-senat/) Related entries: - [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31252&cHash=9dc01c87dda397fab5d23352329e6a42) - Special libraries The special libraries belong to the University Library, however, they are located in the different institutes. There you can find literature related to the thematic areas of the respective institute. The University of Vienna has 38 special libraries. Related Links: - [University Library – University of Vienna](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/bibliotheken.html) Related entries: - [u:print](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31327&cHash=e2b58def8fe69e76e900f1e7c4b0c111) - Sporting aptitude admission test Before admission to the bachelor programmes in Sport Science and in Teacher Education with the teaching subject of Sports and Physical Education, you must pass the sporting aptitude admission test. Related Links: - [Sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) (in German) - Stage of the degree programme Diploma programmes are divided into two or three stages which build on one another and must be completed successively. Each stage consists of modules comprising individual courses. Detailed information regarding the structure of a degree programme is outlined in its curriculum. After having passed all exams of one stage of your degree programme, they must be "handed in" at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceunit (SSS) responsible for your degree programme. Information on the administrative steps is provided by the SSC/SSS in charge. For the completion of your degree programme, all stages must be passed successfully. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: for a unique identification throughout Austria. The student ID number consists of eight digits. It shows at which educational institution and in which year a student was admitted and additionally consists of consecutive numbers. If you transfer to another Austrian university, university college of teacher education, university of applied sciences or private university, the student ID number remains the same. - Student record sheet ("Studienblatt") The student record sheet is one of the so-called study documents. You will receive it once you have been admitted to the University of Vienna. The student record sheet is a semester-specific document and serves as confirmation of the current status of all your studies at the University of Vienna. You can print out the study sheet in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My documents" as soon as your tuition fee/study grant for the respective semester has been received by the University of Vienna. Related entries: - [Confirmation of courses passed (‘Bestätigung über positiv absolvierte Prüfungen’)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30833&cHash=384abc77d3971dc5719c107fd6ea13f4) - [Confirmation of enrolment ("Studienbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31270&cHash=2d54c226eb7b6b7ca5875bf23578fd10) - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - [Study documents](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31288&cHash=96775fee4d128222ffe4a11102a051f9) - [Confirmation of study periods ("Studienzeitbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31294&cHash=be04add5fe3411a86e9b4c39c5b95416) - Students' representatives Students’ representatives are groups of students at the University of Vienna who are addressing the interests and concerns of students. They are also central contact points of address for prospective students and provide answers and advice in case of questions. Related Links: - [Faculty and study

The study achievement system has since been replaced by the recommended path of study. Among other things, they serve as proof that you have been admitted to the University of Vienna. Various authorities/institutions require you to submit your study documents. You can find all study documents in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My documents". As soon as your tuition fee contribution appears in u:space as "received on...", you can print out your student record sheet and your confirmation of enrolment for the respective semester. If you have any questions, you can contact the following offices: - The Directorate of Studies (SPL) is responsible for your degree programme and should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and administrative enquiries during your studies. - Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of alternative modes of assessment for students with impairments. The SPL takes the fundamental decisions about this. The Accessible Studying team (‘Team Barrierefrei’) offers (also anonymous) advice. - The Office of the Studienpräses provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the SPL can solve. - If you have questions about admission and the tuition fee, please contact the Admission Office. - In individual cases, the ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende') of the Federal Ministry can help

If you have any questions, you can contact the following offices: - The Directorate of Studies (SPL) is responsible for your degree programme and should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and administrative enquiries during your studies. - Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of alternative modes of assessment for students with impairments. The SPL takes the fundamental decisions about this. The Accessible Studying team (‘Team Barrierefrei’) offers (also anonymous) advice. - The Office of the Studienpräses provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the SPL can solve. - If you have questions about admission and the tuition fee, please contact the Admission Office. - In individual cases, the ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende') of the Federal Ministry can help

title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: "subject-specific" supplementary examinations can comprise several courses and must be taken additionally. They are best taken at the beginning of the master degree programme. Example: Laura has completed her bachelor degree in Astronomy and would now like to study for the master programme in Meteorology. She has received a letter of admission in which the following supplementary examinations are prescribed:VU Dynamik I (6 ECTS), VO Dynamik II (4 ECTS) and VU Klimasystem der Erde (8 ECTS) totalling 18 ECTS. She can be admitted to the master degree programme in Meteorology, but after admission she must successfully complete the courses "VU Dynamik I", "VO Dynamik II" and "VU Klimasystem der Erde" by the end of the second semester in addition to the coursework prescribed in the degree programme. text: / - Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria The teacher education programme for general secondary education in Vienna and Lower Austria is jointly offered by several higher education institutions. This association of higher education institutions is comprised of the University of Vienna, the university colleges of teacher education in Vienna and Lower Austria, the University College for Agrarian and Environmental Pedagogy and the University College for Teacher Education of Christian Churches. Related Links: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lowe Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) - [Teacher Education Programme](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/) Related entries: - [Teacher Education Programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31166&cHash=0eb63d297127a58723d1581f51e6a92c) - Teacher Education Programme The University of Vienna offers the Teacher Education Programme within the association for general secondary education in Vienna and Lower Austria ("Verbund Nord-Ost"). This degree programme consists of two or more teaching subjects as well as general educational basics. The Teacher Education Programme is divided into a bachelor and master programme. The prescribed duration of studies is 12 semesters (bachelor: 8 semesters, master: 4 semesters). Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need for public authorities such as tax offices or the Austrian Study Grant Authority. You can print your transcript of records via u:space ("Personal Matters" > "My Documents") at any time. text: period usually equals the length of the standard period of the curriculum. The transfer to the new curriculum comes into effect automatically at the end of this period. Information about the transfer is available in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your student record sheet (u:space > Personal matters > My documents). Application for transfer: You have to apply for transfer to a new curriculum at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme, provided that the transition period has not ended yet and both curricula are offered simultaneously. Recognition of exams: If your curriuclum does not provide any regulation on the recognition, your exams will be automatically recognised from the old curriculum to the new one after the admission . Otherwise, you must apply for the recognition of exams. For more information and advice, contact the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) in charge of your curriculum. Please note: Previously, inform yourself about possible legal consequences at your StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). It is not possible to reverse the transfer! You can order your u:card in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My u:card" as soon as your tuition fee/Student’s Union fee appears as "received on..." in u:space. You will than have to validate your u:card every semester as long as you are studying. You can - search for courses, exams, persons or organisational units - view course and exam dates - register for courses and exams during the registration period (forwarding to u:space) - export a calendar - view the area map of the University of Vienna via Google Maps The course directory is being continuously updated at the beginning of the semester. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: you are registered for a course with continuous assessment or an exam, you can deregister from it within the deregistration period. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment, but do not participate in it or do not provide performances, you will be deregistered in most cases. If you are registered for an exam and do not show up, you will not be graded and will potentially be blocked for the following exam date. To avoid this, make sure to deregister in due time! If you could not deregister in due time due to unforeseen circumstances (e.g. because you had an accident), you will have to provide evidence in the most credible way to be deregistered retrospectively. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31253&cHash=b714e0e2eec6eceec5b3fcfc992941d7) - University Board The University Board consists of nine members, eight of whom are appointed in equal parts by the University and the Federal Government. These members elect the ninth member. Together with the Rectorate and the Senate, the University Board forms the governing body of the University of Vienna. The University Board elects the vice-rectors from a short-list of three candidates nominated by the Senate. The University Board members play an important role in the strategic planning and the further development of the University. Related Links: - [University Board (in German)](https://universitaetsrat.univie.ac.at/) - University continuing education and training programme To participate in one of the university continuing education and training programmes, you must already have a subject-related educational background. The admission requirements differ from programme to programme. For the university continuing education and training programmes, fees are charged. Related Links: - [Postgraduate Center](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/) Related entries: - [Postgraduate Center](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31233&cHash=52cf780f415c9795e7b240a943e443ad) - University Gazette According to § 20 Abs. 6 University Act 2002, the University of Vienna must publish news regarding its degree programmes (scholarships, guidelines and regulations, organisation and structures, university's intern elections, award of authorisations to teach, etc.) in its university gazettes ("Mitteilungsblätter") and make them available to the public. title: ABC of terminology

text: university gazettes ("Mitteilungsblätter") and make them available to the public. Related Links: - [Mitteilungsblätter – University of Vienna](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Universities Act 2002](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31316&cHash=c7bad838c1eb526ca9f423a2b18e3c38) - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU): The University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) offers courses and supplementary examinations for future students who - do not have sufficient German language proficiency or - whose secondary-school leaving certificate was issued in a non-EU/EEA country. Example 1: John has received an admission letter which stipulates supplementary examinations in English and Mathematics. He registers for the relevant courses in the VWU and takes the supplementary examinations there. Afterwards, he is admitted to a degree programme. Example 2: Mary has applied for admission to the bachelor programme in Physics. Her secondary-school leaving certificate was issued in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, but she does not have proof of German language proficiency at level C1. She completes the online first-time admission at the Admission Office and receives an admission letter. Afterwards, she registers for a German course in the VWU. After having passed the supplementary examination in German, she is admitted to a degree programme. Related Links: - [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) – website](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/) Related entries: - [Supplementary examination (in the admission letter of bachelor/diploma programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31928&cHash=cf8842ba1574a00f9737609278349b82) - University Sport Institute (USI)

text: / - Directorates of Education ("Bildungsdirektionen") The Directorate of Education is responsible for the schools in the respective austrian federal province. For some bachelor and diploma programmes, you have to provide proof of additional qualifications in addition to meeting the general admission requirements prior to admission (Latin, Greek, Descriptive Geometry). If you are unable to provide evidence of this knowledge via your school reports (school leaving certificate, annual reports of the individual school levels), you will have to make up for this externally. Please enquire about this at the Direcotrate of Education of the federal province in which you live. Related Links: - [Directorates of Education (only in German)](https://www.bmbwf.gv.at/Themen/schule/schulsystem/bdir/bildungsdirektionen.html) - Validating your u:card You have to validate your u:card at one of the u:card terminals at the beginning of every semester to guarantee its validity. You can validate it as soon as your tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee has the status “received on...” in u:space. text: (ZID) is to support the University of Vienna with a stable and modern IT infrastructure and a wide range of IT services. The Vienna University Computer Center (ZID) develops, implements and maintains over 400 IT services at the University of Vienna. A u:account is required for the use of most of the IT services. The ZID Helpdesk provides information about the ZID services and supports members of the University of Vienna in solving university-specific IT problems. It helps users manage their u:account and password. Users can report technical issues and the Helpdesk forwards this information in a targeted manner. The Library holds more than 7.4 million books as well as thousands of e-books, e-journals and print journals. The Main Library, located in the Main Building of the University of Vienna, the 38 special libraries as well as the Vienna University Archive form part of the Vienna University Library. title: ABC of terminology
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text: Being a student ist more than attending lectures and preparing for exams - lending books, eating, sleeping and fun is also part of student life! We gathered info about services that can make student life easier here - of course, the list is far from complete, but we hope it helps!
embed_text:
Study organisation > Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?

Being a student ist more than attending lectures and preparing for exams - lending books, eating, sleeping and fun is also part of student life! We gathered info about services that can make student life easier here - of course, the list is far from complete, but we hope it helps!

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
heading_path: Services of the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Services of the University of Vienna
text: There are many services at the University of Vienna that provide help during your studies. Some of them are listed here: - [Admission Office](/en/): All administrative matters regarding the admission and continuation of degree programmes at the University of Vienna are solved here. - [Directorate of studies - Studienprogrammleitung (SPL)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/): The director of studies plans the teaching offered in a particular degree programme, makes decisions in matters concerning study law and study organisation. - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/): The SSC/SSS of each degree programme organises and coordinates the teaching and the exams offered in each degree programme. At the SSC/SSS office you get information about the contents of a degree programme, help in planning the semester and information for completing a degree programme. - [Center for Doctoral Studies](http://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/): all info about Doctoral/PhD Programmes at the University of Vienna - [Postgraduate Center](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/): The Postgraduate Center of the University of Vienna covers all affairs concerning postgraduate programmes. - [Vienna University Computer Center](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en): The Computer Center is responsible for all technical matters of the University of Vienna such as free WiFi, computer rooms, your u:account, the [u:book](https://ubook.at/) sales etc. - [University Library](http://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/english/) - [Center for Teaching and Learning (CTL)](/en/): The CTL offers mentoring programmes for writing scientific papers and many other interesting lectures/info talks. An overview about the university communities is available here: - [#univie Communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/) - [Social Media Hub](https://socialmedia.univie.ac.at/) - up-to-date social media

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
Some services cooperate closely with the University of Vienna and help in all matters regarding studying (for example planning the semester) and in matters concerning study law.

- [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](http://oeh.univie.ac.at/) - the legal representation of all students
- [Students' Union (ÖH) of the University of Vienna](https://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/)
- [Students' representatives of the respective degree programmes](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen) - students who themselves are studying the degree programme and assist you with any related questions
- [Ombuds office for students](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/) - the Ombuds office assists you in legal issues (with the university)

There are numerous ways of [financial support](#c144895), however, many students still need to work beside studying. Bear in mind that your income may influence the scholarships you receive.

- [Uniport - career service of the University of Vienna](https://www.uniport.at/)
- [Chamber of Labour (Arbeiterkammer - AK)- work contracts](https://www.arbeiterkammer.at/beratung/arbeitundrecht/Arbeitsvertraege/index.html)
- [Chamber of Labour (Arbeiterkammer - AK) - tax information](https://wien.arbeiterkammer.at/beratung/steuerundeinkommen/steuertipps/index.html)
- [Public Employment Service Austria (Arbeitsmarktservice - AMS) - working as a non-EU citizen](https://www.ams.at/unternehmen/service-zur-personalsuche/beschaeftigung-auslaendischer-arbeitskraefte)
- [Work in Austria - Austrian Business Agency](https://www.workinaustria.com/en/)
- [unijobs.at](http://www.unijobs.at/)

Students have several options to spend one or more semesters abroad. The International Office supports students in the realisation of this goal and manages mobility programmes.

- [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/) - all info regarding the mobility/exchange programmes Erasmus+, Non-EU Student Exchange and others

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
Students can get a health insurance for students, an exemption from radio/TV fees or reduced tickets for theaters, museums and cinemas.

- [Wiener Gebietskrankenkasse](https://www.gesundheitskasse.at/cdscontent/?contentid=10007.866742&portal=oegkportal) (WGKK - Vienna Regional Health Insurance Fund)
- [FeelSafe - Private Insurance for Students](https://www.feelsafe.at/)
- [Radio/TV fees - ORF Beitrag](https://orf.beitrag.at/) (available in German, BCS, Polish, Russian, Turkish and Ukrainian)

State support is vital to many students. Information related to family allowance, study grant and housing assistance in available on the following websites:

- [Family allowance - Federal Ministry for Family and Youth](https://www.frauen-familien-jugend.bka.gv.at/familie/finanzielle-unterstuetzungen/familienbeihilfe0.html) (available in German)
- [Study grant - Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/english)
- [Maintenance grant - Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studium-beruf#c67) (available in German)
- [Grant for studying abroad - Austrian Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studieren-im-ausland) (available in German)
- [Housing assistance](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/bauen-wohnen/wohnbaufoerderung/unterstuetzung/wohnbeihilfe-antrag.html) (available in German)
- [Department for social affairs - ÖH (Austrian National Union of Students)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/offices/social-affairs)
- [Social funds - ÖH (Austrian National Union of Students)](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/service/funding-pots/social-fund/)

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
The University of Vienna awards merit-based and need-based scholarships several times a year. Other institutions also provide financial support.

- [Scholarship announcements](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (available in German)
- [Bulletin of the University of Vienna](http://www.univie.ac.at/mtbl02/?t=0;f=Stipendien,_Foerderungen) (available in German)
- [GRANTS database](http://www.grants.at/)
- ["AK-Wissenschaftspreis" - Science award of the Austrian Chamber of Labour](https://ooe.arbeiterkammer.at/wissenschaftspreis/) (available in German
- ["Stipendium für Bahnbrecher" - Scholarship for pioneers](https://www.idealo.de/unternehmen/stipendium/) (available in German)

#### Scholarships of individual federal states

- [Scholarship for scientific work granted by ÖGB (Austrian Federation of Trade Unions)](https://www.oegb.at//cms/S06/S06_0.a/1342537100485) (available in German)
- [Graduation scholarship of the Study Grant Authority](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/studium-beruf#header-ce-112) (available in German)

Students get reduced tickets for public transport and special offers for getting from A to B.

- [Wiener Linien (Vienna transport authority)](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en/tickets-for-students)
- [ÖBB (Austrian federal railways)](https://www.oebb.at/de/tickets-kundenkarten/schueler-gruppen/zeit-schueler-karten)
- [Transportation in Vienna - wien.gv.at](https://www.wien.gv.at/verkehr/oeffentlicher-verkehr) (in German)
- [City of Vienna: Parking in Vienna](https://www.wien.gv.at/verkehr/parken) (in German)
- Travel allowance is granted by individual federal states, and to [study grant recipients](https://www.stipendium.at/stipendien/weitere-foerderungen#header-ce-72)

© Universität Wien / Franz Pfluegl

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
In everyday life, students are sometimes faced with difficult situations. There are several institutions that provide support and assistance:

- Studying with a child
  - [Vienna University Children's Office (Kinderbüro)](https://kinderbuero-uniwien.at/en/)
  - [Information platform UniKid-UniCare Austria](https://www.unikid-unicare.at/) (available in German)
  - [University of Vienna Human Resources and Gender Equality - Reconciling work & family life](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/equality#c11601)
  - [Child care Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/equality#c11601)
  - [wienXtra - Kinderinfo](http://www.wienxtra.at/kinderaktiv/) (available in German)

- Counselling Office Sexual Harassment and Bullying
  The office is a point of first contact and central office for persons affected by sexual harassment and mobbing at the University of Vienna.
  - [Counselling Office Sexual Harassment & Bullying](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/prevention-counselling)

- Psychological Counseling Centers
  The Psychological Counseling Centers offer support to students and applicants by using psychological and psychotherapeutical methods.
  - [Psychological Counseling Centers](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)

© Universität Wien / derknopfdruecker.com

You would like to learn a new language or improve your language skills? The Language Center (Sprachenzentrum) offers general as well as sicentific language courses at various levels in over 30 languages to (future) students of all disciplines, university staff and alumni.

- [Sprachenzentrum of the University of Vienna](https://sprachenzentrum.univie.ac.at/)

title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text: You like sports? The University Sports Institute (Universitätssportinstitut - USI) offers a wide range of courses (from Aerobics to Zen Meditation) at reasonable prices to active students, alumni and university staff. Unisport, which is organised by students, offers sports camps, courses and offers reduced membership cards for fitness centers The Österreichischer Behindertensportverband (ÖBSV - Austrian Federation of Disability Sport) provides information about offers for persons with an impairment and, additionally, training and guidance in this field. The SLS-program is offered together with the KADA association. It serves to support students who want to manage high-performance sports and studies at the same time. - [USI](https://www.usi.at/en/) - [Unisport (link in German)](http://www.unisport.at/wp/) - [ÖBSV (link in German)](https://obsv.at/) - [Compatibility of studying and competitive sports (link in German)](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/waehrend-des-studiums/sls-programm-unterstuetzung-fuer-leistungssportlerinnen/) [#### Humans of #univie @ Footballteam](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/humans-of-university-of-vienna-en/how-to-achieve-outstanding-athletic-and-academic-performance/) © Universität Wien © Universität Wien / Barbara Mair # Accommodation The University of Vienna does not have its own student residence. The buildings that belong to the University of Vienna are situated in various places all around town, so there is no particular are that would be best living in. Accommodation is rather expensive, therefore student dormitories (which are run by private companies) or flat sharing are reasonable alternatives to renting a flat. We list a few sites to find accommodation, but the internet also offers many other useful websites: - [home4students](https://www.home4students.at/en/) - [OeAD student housing](https://housing.oead.at/en/) - [STUWO Student

title: Welcome to the communities of the University of Vienna!
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
Student Life - Who can help me with...?
text:
The communities at the University of Vienna offer you the ideal opportunity to try out new things and meet fascinating people at the same time. Here you find groups of students that make university life more colourful, diverse and exciting. Check it out and find your #univie community!

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

title: Services for graduates
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Be part of the Alumni Community
Your first steps into professional life
text:
© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

Welcome to the University of Vienna’s alumni community. Now that you have graduated you are entering a new stage of life. This also means that your relationship to your alma mater changes. The University of Vienna would like to continue to accompany you on your path. For this purpose, we are offering the following services to you.

The University of Vienna [Alumni Community](https://alumni.univie.ac.at/) is a vibrant, open network for all graduates. Here, we connect, exchange experiences, share knowledge – and generate new ideas together.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

Graduation ceremonies offer the ideal setting for celebrating the completion of your bachelor’s, master’s, diploma or doctoral/PhD programme together with your friends and family.

- [Click here for more information.](https://event.univie.ac.at/en/ceremonies-and-celebrations/ceremonies-celebrations/)

© Universität Wien / Klaus Ranger

title: Services for graduates
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Your first steps into professional life
heading_paths: Your first steps into professional life
Your first steps into professional life
Your first steps into professional life
text:
**Advice on getting started on your career.** Many questions arise when it comes to starting a career after graduation. Our advisory team at Uniport, the career service of the University of Vienna, are happy to assist you with personalised consultations and workshops. A number of career events provide insight into different job profiles and the opportunity to get in touch with prospective employers.

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 15 % discount on the JobStart seminars offered by the career service Uniport.*

- [Career service of the University of Vienna - UNIPORT](https://www.uniport.at/uniport-fuer-studierende) (in German)
- [Coaching for job applications and CV check](https://www.uniport.at/beratung/coaching) (in German)
- [Current career events](https://www.uniport.at/events/termine) (in German)

#### Finding a mentor

On the [**alma Mentoring Platform**](https://mentoring.univie.ac.at/en/) of the University of Vienna you can find a suitable mentor and benefit from the experiences of other graduates when it comes to starting a career after graduation.

***Benefits for alumni:*** *mentoring free of charge for members of the Alumni Association*

#### Finding jobs

Your chances of finding a job after graduation increase if you have already collected some work experience during your studies. Uniport, the career service of the University of Vienna, offers its own [**job exchange**](https://www.uniport.at/jobs-praktika/), where internships, trainee programmes as well as jobs in Vienna and all of Austria are advertised.

title: Services for graduates
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Your first steps into professional life
Language courses for beginners and advanced learners
Language courses for beginners and advanced learners
Language courses for beginners and advanced learners
text:
#### Incorporation programme

[**u:start**](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/berufkarriere/ustart) is the incorporation programme for graduates. It supports academics who are considering self-employment or establishing their own company or enterprise. The training programme assists graduates in developing their initial business idea into a high-quality business plan.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

**Lifelong learning.** The University of Vienna offers more than 70 postgraduate master’s programmes, university continuing education and training programmes and certificate courses, most of them part-time. They provide additional interdisciplinary, practice-oriented qualifications for graduates

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 15 % discount on select postgraduate programmes.*

- [Postgraduate Center of the University of Vienna](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/)
- [Helpdesk](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/)

© Universität Wien / Klaus Ranger

**Multilingual.**Be it Arabic, Russian or Business English — at the Language Centre of the University of Vienna you can improve your language skills in more than 30 foreign languages or work on your language skills together with language exchange partners.

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 10 % discount on all courses offered by the Language Centre of the University of Vienna.*

- [Language Centre of the University of Vienna](https://sprachenzentrum.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Language swap](https://sprachenzentrum.univie.ac.at/en/language-swap/)

#### Discipline-specific initiatives for alumni

Biologists have different interests than archaeologists or physicists. The [**discipline-specific initiatives for alumni**](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/deralumniverband/alumnifachinitiativen) offer the opportunity to keep up with developments in your own discipline even after graduation. Regular newsletters provide information on subject-specific activities for alumni.

title: Services for graduates
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
heading_paths: Language courses for beginners and advanced learners
text:
#### Becoming a mentor

No matter if you would like to share your experiences on finding a job, staying abroad or establishing companies and enterprises — on the alma Mentoring Platform of the University of Vienna you can [**become a mentor**](https://mentoring.univie.ac.at/en/page/MentorInnen) and share your knowledge with other graduates.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

**Continuing to seek knowledge.**Graduating from the University of Vienna does not mean that you can no longer access the University’s information resources. Graduates can continue to use their library accounts or extend them (€ 15 per year) and continue to use electronic resources free of charge (at the locations of the Vienna University Library and via the internal university Wi-Fi).

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 50 % discount on library cards (€ 7.50).*

- [Vienna University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/)

© USI Wien / Barbara Mair

**Staying fit.**From Aikido to Zumba — the University Sport Institute (USI) offers courses for around 160 different types of sports as well as continuing education and training programmes.

***Benefits for alumni:****All USI courses are available to university graduates at special alumni rates.*

- [University Sport Institute of the University of Vienna](https://musi.usi.at/?lang=en)

© Universität Wien / Elia Zilberberg

title: Services for graduates
breadcrumb: Studying at the University of Vienna
text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)
